PhD from MIT (1988) in qualitative probabilistic reasoning and decision-theoretic planning. Led the Strategic Reasoning Group at Michigan. Research focuses on computational game theory, agent-based modeling, and strategic reasoning with applications in finance, cybersecurity, and network behavior. Investigated risks of autonomous trading agents and algorithmic market instabilities. Served as CSE Division Chair at Michigan (2020-2024). Won the ACM/SIGAI Autonomous Agents Research Aw

name: Kate Larson
notes_head: Professor, Pasupalak AI Fellow, and University Research Chair in the Cheriton School of Computer Science at the University of Waterloo. Research centers on multi-agent systems at the intersection of computer science, mathematics, and economics — specifically algorithmic mechanism design, cooperative game theory, and coalition formation. Named 2025 AAAI Fellow for significant and sustained contributions to AI. Won Best Paper Award at AAMAS (International Conference on Autonomous Agents and Multiagent Systems). Studies strategic challenges in cooperative, semi-cooperative, and negotiation-based

name: Vincent Conitzer
notes_head: Professor of Computer Science at Carnegie Mellon University with affiliate appointments in Machine Learning, Philosophy, and Tepper School of Business. Directs the Foundations of Cooperative AI Lab at CMU. Also Head of Technical AI Engagement at the Institute for Ethics in AI and Professor of Computer Science and Philosophy at the University of Oxford. PhD from CMU (2006), AB in Applied Mathematics from Harvard. Research spans AI-game theory intersection, computational social choice, and AI ethics — focuses on determining objectives AI systems should pursue when affecting diverse stakeholders.

name: John Bargh
notes_head: James Rowland Angell Professor of Psychology at Yale University. Ph.D. in Social Psychology from University of Michigan (1981). Leading researcher on automaticity, unconscious processing, and priming effects on behavior. Directs the ACME (Automaticity in Cognition, Motivation, and Evaluation) Lab at Yale. Faculty at NYU (1981-2003) before moving to Yale. Advisor to AE Studio's alignment research, connecting social psychology and unconscious cognition to AI alignment work. Judd Rosenblatt took his cognitive science course at Yale.

name: Pieter Abbeel
notes_head: Pieter Abbeel is a Professor at UC Berkeley and a leading robotics and reinforcement learning researcher. He co-founded Covariant, a robotics AI startup whose foundation models were licensed by Amazon in August 2024, bringing Abbeel into Amazon as an Amazon Scholar. In December 2025, he was appointed head of Amazon's large language model efforts within the AGI organization while continuing to lead robotics work, effectively co-leading Amazon's Frontier AI & Robotics team. At Berkeley, he leads the AI Leadership Accelerator program. Abbeel is primarily a capabilities-focused researcher and entr

name: Anca Dragan
notes_head: Anca Dragan is an Associate Professor in EECS at UC Berkeley, currently on leave as Senior Director of AI Safety and Alignment at Google DeepMind. She runs the InterACT Lab at Berkeley, focused on algorithms for human-AI and human-robot interaction. At DeepMind, she leads teams responsible for Gemini model safety, alignment with human goals and values, and mitigating both present-day harms and catastrophic risks. A faculty member of CHAI, her research emphasizes that 'what to optimize' is the harder question than 'how to optimize.' She advocates for accounting for human cognitive biases in tra

name: Michael Littman
title: Division Director, Information and Intelligent Systems (IIS), NSF CISE
notes_head: Michael Littman is a computer science professor at Brown University who took an NSF rotation beginning 2022 as Division Director for Information and Intelligent Systems (IIS) within the Computer and Information Science and Engineering directorate. Long-time AI/ML researcher and educator specializing in reinforcement learning; widely known for public-facing AI education content on YouTube.

name: Greg Hager
title: Assistant Director, NSF Computer and Information Science and Engineering (CISE) Directorate
notes_head: Greg Hager became Assistant Director of NSF's Computer and Information Science and Engineering (CISE) directorate on June 3, 2024, and is serving in 2026. The Mandell Bellmore Professor of Computer Science at Johns Hopkins University and founding director of the JHU Malone Center for Engineering in Healthcare. Succeeded Margaret Martonosi (2020–2023) after Dilma Da Silva served as acting AD.

name: Kim Stanley Robinson
title: Science Fiction Author, Climate Fiction Writer
notes_head: Kim Stanley Robinson is described as "one of human civilization's greatest living science fiction writers" and author of works including the Hugo Award-winning Mars trilogy, Nebula Award-winning 2312, and The Ministry for the Future. He was born on March 23, 1952, and is best known for his Mars trilogy of novels. Robinson earned a PhD in English from UC San Diego in 1982, writing his dissertation on Philip K. Dick under Marxist scholar Fredric Jameson. He has lived in Davis, California since 1978 and identifies as a democratic socialist, being a dues-paying member of the Democratic Socialists

name: Natasha Lyonne
title: Co-founder, Asteria Film Co.; Actress, Writer, Director
notes_head: Co-founded Asteria Film Co. in 2022 with Bryn Mooser as an 'ethical AI' film studio using the Marey model trained exclusively on licensed data. Organized 400+ Hollywood figures including Paul McCartney and Ben Stiller to sign letter to Trump administration in March 2025 opposing AI deregulation. Spoke at TIME100 AI Impact Dinner in October 2025 calling for 'responsible reassessment' of AI regulation. Currently directing 'Uncanny Valley' combining AI tools with traditional filmmaking, set for release in 2025.

name: Refik Anadol
title: Media Artist and Pioneer of AI Art, Director of Refik Anadol Studio
notes_head: Turkish-American artist who coined the terms "AI Data Painting and Sculpture" in 2016 during his Google AMI residency. His 2022 MoMA installation "Unsupervised" drew 3 million visitors and was trained on the museum's entire collection. Opening DATALAND, the world's first AI art museum, in Los Angeles in 2026. Emphasizes using only ethically sourced datasets and advocates for transparency in AI art creation processes.

name: Rick Rubin
title: Music Producer and Author
notes_head: Published 'The Way of Code: The Timeless Art of Vibe Coding' in 2025, an interactive book created with Claude AI that explores 'vibe coding' - giving AI directions in natural language. Called AI the 'punk rock of coding' for democratizing creation by prioritizing ideas over technical expertise. Appeared on The Ben & Marc Show podcast in May 2025 to discuss his AI philosophy and creative applications.

name: Ted Chiang
title: Science Fiction Author; Stories include "Story of Your Life" (Arrival), "Exhalation"; Computer Science Background; AI Critic
notes_head: Won four Hugo Awards, four Nebula Awards, and 2024 PEN/Malamud Award for short story excellence. Named one of Time's 100 Most Influential People in AI (2023). Wrote influential New Yorker essays including "Why A.I. Isn't Going to Make Art" and "ChatGPT Is a Blurry JPEG of the Web" arguing AI cannot create true art due to lack of intentional choice-making. Studied computer science at Brown University, providing technical foundation for his AI commentary.

name: Yuval Noah Harari
title: Historian & Author; Co-founder of Sapienship; Distinguished Research Fellow at Cambridge Centre for the Study of Existential Risk
notes_head: Delivered major AI warnings at Davos 2026 WEF, predicting AI will create identity and immigration crises for every country. Warns that 'anything made of words will be taken over by AI' including laws, books, and religions. Has sold over 50 million books in 65 languages and regularly advises world leaders including Emmanuel Macron and Angela Merkel. Published 'Nexus: A Brief History of Information Networks from the Stone Age to AI' in 2024.

name: Cory Doctorow
title: Science Fiction Author, Activist & Journalist; Special Advisor, Electronic Frontier Foundation; Creator of 'Enshittification'
notes_head: Coined the viral term 'enshittification' to describe platform decay. Has upcoming book 'The Reverse Centaur's Guide to AI' (June 2026) arguing AI is a bubble that will cause economic catastrophe. Views AI as 'a collection of moderately useful tools being billed as a supernatural being.' Warns that 'AI can't do your job, but an AI salesman can convince your boss to fire you and replace you with AI that can't do your job.'

name: Naomi Klein
title: Author, Activist & Professor of Climate Justice at University of British Columbia; Co-Director, Centre for Climate Justice
notes_head: Published 2023 Guardian piece 'AI machines aren't hallucinating, But their makers are' arguing AI development represents 'the largest theft in human history.' Describes AI as creating a 'three-legged stool' of tech, fossil fuels, and state power that provides a lifeline to fossil fuel industries. Warns AI undermines belief in reality itself through deepfakes and generated content.

name: Brian Christian
title: Author, The Alignment Problem, The Most Human Human, Algorithms to Live By; DPhil student at Oxford
notes_head: Won 2022 National Academies Eric and Wendy Schmidt Awards for Excellence in Science Communications. Currently pursuing DPhil at Oxford under Chris Summerfield focusing on cognitive models of human decision-making for AI alignment. His book 'The Alignment Problem' was called 'the one book to read on AI' by The New York Times and bridges technical AI safety with present-day algorithmic bias issues.

name: Shalini Kantayya
title: Documentary Filmmaker; Director, Coded Bias (Netflix) and TikTok, Boom (Sundance)
notes_head: Her 2020 Sundance documentary 'Coded Bias' follows MIT researcher Joy Buolamwini's discovery of racial bias in facial recognition systems and advocates for algorithmic justice. The film was broadcast on PBS Independent Lens and globally on Netflix, winning Best Director at Social Impact Media Awards. Her work focuses on how AI systems perpetuate inequality and erode civil rights, particularly impacting marginalized communities through biased hiring, healthcare, and criminal justice algorithms.

name: Walter Isaacson
title: Author, Biographer & Professor of History
notes_head: Bestselling biographer of major tech figures including Steve Jobs (2011), Leonardo da Vinci (2017), Jennifer Doudna (2021), and Elon Musk (2023). Rhodes Scholar and Harvard graduate who served as Time Magazine editor and CNN chairman. Received National Humanities Medal from President Biden in 2023. Currently teaches 'The Digital Revolution' course at Tulane University and co-chairs the New Orleans Book Festival.

name: Kevin Kelly
title: Senior Maverick at Wired Magazine, Founding Executive Editor, Technology Author and Futurist
notes_head: Author of multiple bestselling technology books including 'The Inevitable' (2016), 'What Technology Wants' (2010), and 'Excellent Advice for Living' (2023). Co-founded Wired magazine in 1993 and served as executive editor for seven years, during which the magazine won two National Magazine Awards. Maintains active Substack newsletter with detailed analysis of AI development and publishes Cool Tools website with 1 million monthly visitors. Known for radical technological optimism and long-term perspective on technological change.

name: Melania Trump
notes_head: Former and current First Lady of the United States. Emerged as unexpected AI policy voice in 2025-2026 through education and child safety lens. Launched Presidential AI Challenge — nationwide contest inviting K-12 students to develop AI projects addressing community challenges. Convened White House Task Force on Artificial Intelligence Education (September 2025) with tech CEOs and Cabinet members. Advocated 'watchful guidance' approach to AI growth. Championed the TAKE IT DOWN Act (signed May 2025) criminalizing non-consensual sharing of explicit images including AI-generated deepfakes — grew

name: Elena Andreicheva
notes_head: Oscar and BAFTA-winning documentary filmmaker. Director of "Intelligence Rising" (premiered CPH:DOX March 2026), a documentary examining AI regulation through military-style war games featuring military strategists, economists, and philosophers including former British Army head General Patrick Sanders, Skype creator Jaan Tallinn, and Yuval Noah Harari. Ukrainian-born, studied physics at Imperial College London. Previously produced the Oscar-winning short "Learning to Skateboard in a Warzone (If You are a Girl)." Intelligence Rising war game was designed by researchers at Cambridge, Oxford, an

name: Molly Crabapple
title: Artist and author; anti-generative-AI-art organizer
notes_head: Molly Crabapple is a New York-based artist and author and the most visible artist-organizer in the anti-generative-AI movement. Organized a 1,000-signatory open letter calling on publishers not to use AI-generated illustrations. Regular contributor to Hyperallergic; September 2025 essay 'Can Artists Stop the AI Slop Machine?' is widely cited by critics of generative-AI image tools.

name: Douglas Rushkoff
title: Media theorist; Professor of Media Theory, CUNY/Queens; host, 'Team Human'
notes_head: Douglas Rushkoff is a Professor of Media Theory at Queens College CUNY, host of the Team Human podcast, and a prolific public commentator on technology and society. His updated edition of 'Program or Be Programmed' includes '11 Commands for the AI future'. Scholar-in-residence at Andus Labs; produced the Andus 'After Now' event (July 2025) featuring Brian Eno, Nick Thompson, and others.

name: Jaron Lanier
title: Prime Unifying Scientist, Microsoft; New Yorker contributor
notes_head: Jaron Lanier is Microsoft's 'Prime Unifying Scientist' (the company's informal chief philosophical advisor on AI) and a longtime New Yorker contributor. Pioneering VR researcher and one of the field's most prominent public dissenters on AI-as-entity framing — argues 'there is no AI' and advocates for 'data dignity' (compensating humans whose data trains models). Shapes elite skepticism about anthropomorphic AI framing.

name: Sherry Turkle
title: Abby Rockefeller Mauzé Professor, MIT; author
notes_head: Sherry Turkle is the Abby Rockefeller Mauzé Professor of the Social Studies of Science and Technology at MIT and one of the most prominent U.S. voices on AI companionship and the psychology of human-AI relationships. Author of 'Artificial Intimacy: Who We Become When We Talk to Machines' (2025), building on her earlier 'Alone Together' and 'Reclaiming Conversation'. Widely cited as chatbot-companion and emotional-AI products become mainstream.

name: Dario Amodei
title: CEO and Co-founder
notes_head: Dario Amodei (born 1983) is an American artificial intelligence researcher and entrepreneur who co-founded Anthropic in 2021 with his sister Daniela Amodei. He previously served as vice president of research at OpenAI, where he led the development of large language models like GPT-2 and GPT-3. Amodei earned his doctorate degree in biophysics from Princeton University as a Hertz Fellow, and was a postdoctoral scholar at the Stanford University School of Medicine. In 2025, Time magazine listed Amodei as one of the world's 100 most influential people.

name: Daniela Amodei
title: Co-Founder and President, Anthropic
notes_head: Daniela Amodei is the president and co-founder of Anthropic, an AI safety and research company that develops the Claude large language model. She co-founded the company with her brother Dario Amodei in 2021 after leaving OpenAI, where she served as VP of Safety and Policy. According to Wikipedia, she was born in 1987 and graduated from the University of California, Santa Cruz with a Bachelor of Arts in English Literature. As of February 2026, Forbes estimated her net worth to be $7 billion.

name: Sam Altman
title: CEO of OpenAI
notes_head: Sam Altman is the CEO of OpenAI, the artificial intelligence research organization that developed ChatGPT. He became CEO of OpenAI in 2019 after co-founding the organization in 2015. In November 2023, he was briefly fired by OpenAI's board of directors but was reinstated five days later. His net worth was estimated at US$3.3 billion by Forbes in March 2026.

name: Mike Krieger
title: Chief Product Officer, Anthropic
notes_head: Mike Krieger is the chief product officer of Anthropic. He co-founded Instagram with Kevin Systrom in 2010 and served as its Chief Technology Officer until 2018. He recently moved from his CPO role to co-lead Anthropic's internal incubator called the 'Labs' team. He previously co-founded the AI-powered news app Artifact which was sold to Yahoo.

name: Josh Woodward
title: VP Google Labs, Gemini App, and AI Studio
notes_head: Josh Woodward is a 42-year-old Oklahoma native who started at Google by way of a product marketing internship in 2009. He is currently Vice President at Google, leading both Google Labs and the Gemini app. Woodward was promoted to run the Gemini app in April while keeping his existing job running Google Labs. Google said in October that the Gemini app's monthly active users swelled to 650 million from 350 million in March.

name: Demis Hassabis
title: CEO and Co-Founder, Google DeepMind
notes_head: Demis Hassabis is the CEO and co-founder of Google DeepMind, an artificial intelligence company that was founded in London in 2010 and acquired by Google in 2014. He shared the 2024 Nobel Prize in Chemistry with John M. Jumper for their work on AlphaFold, an AI tool that predicts protein structures. He is also the founder and CEO of Isomorphic Labs, a biotechnology company focused on drug discovery. Hassabis was born on July 27, 1976, in London to a Greek Cypriot father and Chinese Singaporean mother.

name: Mark Zuckerberg
title: CEO and Chairman, Meta Platforms
notes_head: Mark Zuckerberg is building an artificial intelligence agent to help him perform his executive duties autonomously, according to multiple reports. The AI agent helps Zuckerberg get information faster, such as by retrieving answers for him that he would typically have to go through layers of people to get. Zuckerberg serves as CEO of both Facebook and its parent company Meta Platforms. He is married to Priscilla Chan and they have three daughters.

name: Elon Musk
title: Founder and CEO, xAI; CEO and Product Architect, Tesla; Founder, CEO, and Chief Engineer, SpaceX
notes_head: Elon Musk is a businessman and entrepreneur known for his leadership of Tesla, SpaceX, X, and xAI. Musk has been the wealthiest person in the world since 2025; as of April 2026, Forbes estimates his net worth to be US$809 billion. He made predictions at the World Economic Forum that artificial intelligence will be smarter than all of humanity by the end of the decade. Musk spent more than $290 million on the 2024 election, boosting President Donald Trump and Republicans.

name: Liang Wenfeng
title: CEO and Founder, DeepSeek
notes_head: Liang Wenfeng is a Chinese entrepreneur born in 1985 who serves as CEO of DeepSeek and co-founder of quantitative hedge fund High-Flyer. He launched DeepSeek in 2023 with backing from High-Flyer, focusing on developing general artificial intelligence through efficient, low-cost approaches. DeepSeek gained international attention in January 2025 with the release of DeepSeek-R1, which achieved performance comparable to top models like GPT-4 at significantly lower training costs. He was ranked No. 5 on AI Magazine's Top 100 Leaders 2026 list and included in Nature's top 10 'people who shaped scie

name: Jared Kaplan
title: Co-founder and Chief Science Officer, Anthropic; Responsible Scaling Officer
notes_head: Jared Kaplan is Anthropic's chief scientist and co-founder. He previously worked as a researcher at OpenAI where he co-authored "Scaling Laws for Neural Language Models" (2020) and "Language Models are Few-Shot Learners" (2020), which introduced GPT-3. He is also an associate professor in the Johns Hopkins University Department of Physics & Astronomy. In October 2024, Anthropic announced that Kaplan would serve as the company's "Responsible Scaling Officer", overseeing its responsible scaling policy (RSP).

name: Marius Hobbhahn
title: CEO and Co-Founder, Apollo Research
notes_head: Marius Hobbhahn is the CEO and co-founder of Apollo Research, a nonprofit AI safety research organization that focuses on understanding AI deception and scheming behavior. In 2025, he was named to TIME100 in AI 2025. Apollo Research has collaborated with major AI companies including OpenAI to reduce scheming in models like o3, and works with government bodies like the U.K.'s AI Safety Institute. He holds a Ph.D. in Machine Learning from the University of Tübingen, where he worked under the supervision of Philipp Hennig.

name: Chris Lehane
title: Chief Global Affairs Officer, OpenAI
notes_head: Chris Lehane is Chief Global Affairs Officer at OpenAI, having previously served as vice president of global policy starting in early 2024. He is a veteran of the Clinton administration who famously coined the term "vast right-wing conspiracy" and worked during the Monica Lewinsky scandal. Before OpenAI, he served as Head of Global Policy and Public Affairs for Airbnb from 2015-2022 and was an Operating Partner at Haun Ventures, a crypto investment firm. He co-authored a book called "Masters of Disaster: The Ten Commandments of Damage Control" in 2012.

name: Matthew Prince
title: Co-founder and CEO, Cloudflare
notes_head: Prince launched Cloudflare's 'pay-per-crawl' initiative on July 1, 2025, blocking AI crawlers unless they compensate content creators. He has been actively lobbying regulators including the UK's Competition and Markets Authority to force Google to separate its search and AI crawlers. Prince proposed five responsible AI bot principles in 2025 requiring public disclosure, self-identification, single purpose declaration, respect for preferences, and good intent behavior.

name: Jensen Huang
title: Founder, President & CEO, Nvidia
notes_head: In January 2026 stated 'I think we've achieved AGI' on Lex Fridman podcast. Predicted AGI by 2028 at November 2023 NYT DealBook Summit. Advocates against 'doomer narratives' about AI, arguing excessive fear-mongering deters investment and slows safety innovation. Met with President Trump in December 2025 to discuss chip export controls and AI policy. Committed to building $500 billion in AI supercomputers during Trump's term to reindustrialize America.

name: Fidji Simo
title: CEO of Applications, OpenAI
notes_head: Joined OpenAI as CEO of Applications in August 2025 after serving on the board since March 2024. Previously CEO of Instacart (2021-2025) and VP/Head of Facebook App at Meta (2011-2021). Led OpenAI's pivot toward enterprise focus and advertising monetization, stating the company 'cannot miss this moment because we are distracted by side quests.' Emphasizes mental health risks as a priority area, contrasting OpenAI's proactive safety approach with Meta's reactive stance.

name: Andy Jassy
title: President and CEO, Amazon
notes_head: Leads Amazon's massive AI investments including $100+ billion in capital expenditures for 2025, predominantly for AWS AI capabilities. Has developed Amazon's Frontier Model Safety Framework with specific critical capability thresholds and safeguards. Joined US AI Safety Institute Consortium in 2024 and contributed $5 million in compute credits for AI safety evaluation tools. Emphasized urgent need for companies to invest 'aggressively' in AI infrastructure while maintaining responsible development practices.

name: Allie K. Miller
title: CEO, Open Machine; Former Global Head of Machine Learning for Startups and VC at AWS
notes_head: Miller predicts AGI by 2027 based on conversations with AI researchers, with some suggesting 2026. She emphasizes AI as strategic partner rather than tool, advocating against 'pilot purgatory' where companies run endless experiments without systematic transformation. Named to TIME100 Most Influential People in AI 2025 and has over 2M followers across social platforms.

name: Strive Masiyiwa
title: Founder and Executive Chairman, Cassava Technologies
notes_head: Leading a $720 million investment to build five AI factories across Africa (South Africa, Nigeria, Kenya, Egypt, Morocco) with 15,000 NVIDIA GPUs by 2026. Strategic partner with Google to roll out Gemini across Africa and NVIDIA's first preferred cloud partner on the continent. Focuses heavily on digital sovereignty and ensuring Africa becomes an AI producer rather than just consumer, with emphasis on local language models and keeping data within African borders.

name: Cristiano Amon
title: President and CEO, Qualcomm Incorporated
notes_head: Has led Qualcomm for 30 years including as CEO since June 2021, positioning the company as a leader in edge AI computing with chips powering Meta's AI glasses. Believes 'AI will be democratized' and run on every device, predicting smart glasses will be breakthrough AI devices. Strong advocate for on-device AI processing for privacy and efficiency, competing directly with Nvidia in data center inference markets launched in October 2024.

name: Alexandr Wang
title: CEO and Founder, Scale AI
notes_head: Testified before House Energy and Commerce Committee in 2025 calling for US AI leadership through data dominance and agentic government. Co-authored 'Superintelligence Strategy' policy paper with Eric Schmidt and Dan Hendrycks proposing Mutual Assured AI Malfunction (MAIM) deterrence framework. Became world's youngest self-made billionaire at age 24 through Scale AI, which provides AI training data to major tech companies and government agencies including the Department of Defense.

name: C.C. Wei
title: Chairman and CEO, Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC)
notes_head: Wei leads TSMC, which produces ~95% of advanced AI chips globally, making him central to AI development. He admits being 'very nervous' about AI demand sustainability given TSMC's $52-56 billion annual capital expenditure. Under his leadership, TSMC announced a $100 billion U.S. investment in 2025, the largest foreign direct investment in U.S. history. He has been recognized in TIME's 100 Most Influential People in AI for 2024 and 2025.

name: David Holz
title: Founder and CEO, Midjourney
notes_head: Admits to training Midjourney on hundreds of millions of images without consent, stating there's no practical way to get permission. Built Midjourney as self-funded company with ~15 employees, avoiding VC funding to maintain control. Faces multiple major copyright lawsuits from Disney, Universal, and Warner Bros. as of 2025.

name: Ren Zhengfei
title: Founder and CEO, Huawei Technologies
notes_head: In November 2025 speech, distinguished between US pursuit of AGI for philosophical questions versus China's focus on practical AI applications in mining, manufacturing, and public services. Believes quantum computing breakthroughs are inevitable but should be left to nation-states rather than individual companies. Advocates for massive retraining programs to address AI-driven labor displacement, suggesting conversion of empty schools and factories into vocational training centers.

name: Steve Huffman
title: Co-founder and CEO, Reddit
notes_head: Named to TIME's 100 Most Influential People in AI (2024). Plans to hire 'AI native' new graduates and sees AI as beneficial for Reddit's content moderation and user safety. Implementing bot detection measures including ID verification and World ID integration to combat 'AI slop' while positioning Reddit as 'the most human place on the internet.' Has lucrative data licensing deals with AI companies including a $60M annual agreement with Google.

name: Adam Evans
title: EVP & GM, Salesforce AI Platform
notes_head: Evans became EVP & GM of Salesforce AI in November 2024 after Clara Shih's departure, leading Agentforce development with $540M ARR and 18,500 deals. He's the only founder to sell two companies (RelateIQ for $392M in 2014, Airkit.ai in 2023) to the same acquirer. His technical architecture focuses on multi-agent orchestration, reasoning engines, and Einstein Trust Layer for enterprise AI safety.

name: Rene Haas
title: CEO, Arm Holdings
notes_head: Leads Arm Holdings, which designs chips used by 70% of world's population. Expressed specific concerns about humans losing capability to machines and need for override mechanisms in AI systems (Bloomberg 2023). Led Arm's historic shift from IP licensing to producing its own AI chips with the Arm AGI CPU launch in 2026. Focuses heavily on AI energy efficiency, claiming Arm Neoverse could save hyperscalers 15% of data center power.

name: Wang Xingxing
title: Founder and CEO, Unitree Robotics
notes_head: Wang Xingxing founded Unitree Robotics in 2016 after leaving DJI after just two months. At age 35, he was the youngest participant in Xi Jinping's February 2025 high-profile business symposium alongside tech giants like Jack Ma and Ren Zhengfei. Unitree gained global attention when its humanoid robots performed at the 2025 CCTV Spring Festival Gala, and the company has achieved over 60% of the global quadruped robot market share by 2023. Wang predicts robotics could have its 'ChatGPT moment' within 1-3 years and believes current robot technology is at the level of a 10-year-old child.

name: Amnon Shashua
title: President and CEO, Mobileye; Senior Vice President (former), Intel; Professor, Hebrew University of Jerusalem
notes_head: Co-founded Mobileye in 1999, sold to Intel for $15.3B in 2017, returned public in 2022. Developed Responsibility-Sensitive Safety (RSS) framework that became basis for IEEE 2846 standard and influenced global AV safety regulations. Recently announced $900M acquisition of humanoid robotics company Mentee Robotics, positioning Mobileye as a dual-engine physical AI company. Holds over 200 patents and has published 160+ papers in computer vision and machine learning.

name: Alex Blania
title: Co-founder and CEO, Tools for Humanity; Co-founder, World; Co-founder, Merge Labs
notes_head: Co-founded Tools for Humanity with Sam Altman in 2020 to create proof-of-humanity systems using iris-scanning Orb devices. Recently launched Merge Labs in January 2026 to bridge biological and artificial intelligence. Has testified before regulators about biometric privacy and AI identity verification. Company faced high-level executive departures in early 2026 amid reports of hardcore work culture.

name: Maithra Raghu
title: Co-founder and CEO, Samaya AI
notes_head: Named to TIME's 2025 AI 100 list for building specialized AI agents for financial services. Co-founded Samaya AI in 2022, raising $43.5M from investors including Eric Schmidt, NVIDIA Ventures, and Databricks Ventures. Former Google Brain researcher who studied neural network interpretability and co-authored survey on deep learning with Eric Schmidt. Company's Agent Control Plane serves over 10,000 professionals at major banks for high-stakes investment workflows.

name: Mati Staniszewski
title: Co-founder and CEO, ElevenLabs
notes_head: In February 2026, ElevenLabs became the first company to secure AIUC-1-backed insurance for AI voice agents, establishing new trust standards for enterprise AI deployment. The company has grown to $330M ARR and $11B valuation since 2022, powering over 3 million voice agents for 75% of Fortune 500 companies including Cisco, Square, and Revolut. ElevenLabs has restored over 3,000 voices for people who lost speech ability and paid out $11M through its voice marketplace to contributors.

name: Peggy Johnson
title: CEO, Agility Robotics
notes_head: Became CEO of Agility Robotics in March 2024, leading company deploying humanoid robots in Amazon warehouses and other commercial facilities. Previously spent 24 years at Qualcomm, 6 years as Microsoft EVP where she launched M12 venture fund, and served as Magic Leap CEO 2020-2023. Named Business Insider's most powerful female engineer in US (2017) and inducted into Women in Technology Hall of Fame (2013).

name: James Peng
title: Founder and CEO, Pony.ai
notes_head: Peng co-founded Pony.ai in 2016 after 11 years at Google and Baidu, including leading Baidu's autonomous driving division. The company went public on Nasdaq in November 2024 with a $413M IPO and later raised $863M in Hong Kong listing. Pony.ai operates over 1,400 robotaxis across China and internationally, achieving unit economics breakeven in Guangzhou and Shenzhen by 2025. Peng holds a Ph.D. from Stanford and previously worked on Google's ads teams.

name: Tareq Amin
title: CEO, Humain
notes_head: Leads Saudi Arabia's $600 billion AI infrastructure initiative backed by PIF, with partnerships including $23 billion in agreements with Nvidia, AMD, AWS, and Qualcomm signed in 2025. Named to TIME's 100 Most Influential People in AI 2025. Focuses on building multi-gigawatt AI data centers and Arabic-first AI models including the ALLAM LLM to establish Saudi Arabia as the world's third major AI compute center after US and China.

name: Mfikeyi Makayi
title: CEO, KoBold Metals Africa
notes_head: Named to TIME's 100 Most Influential People in AI 2025 for leading KoBold Metals' AI-driven mineral exploration in Africa. Oversees the $2 billion Mingomba copper project in Zambia's Copperbelt, using AI tools that analyze geological data to pinpoint drilling locations 10,000 times faster than traditional methods. First Zambian woman to lead a major mineral exploration company, appointed CEO in 2023. KoBold Metals has raised over $1 billion in funding with backing from Bill Gates, Jeff Bezos, and Reid Hoffman.

name: Sam Rodriques
title: Co-founder and CEO, FutureHouse; Co-founder and CEO, Edison Scientific
notes_head: Named to TIME100 AI 2025 list for influence in AI science. Led development of Robin multi-agent system that demonstrated first end-to-end AI-driven scientific discovery in October 2025. Spun out Edison Scientific as for-profit company in November 2025, raising $70M seed funding. Believes AI will enable scientists to do '10x or 100x' more work with AI assistance while maintaining human control over final decisions.

name: Andy Parsons
title: Senior Director of Content Authenticity Initiative, Adobe
notes_head: Leads Adobe's Content Authenticity Initiative with 5,000+ members including major tech companies and media organizations. Developed Content Credentials technology using cryptographic signatures and watermarking to verify digital content provenance. Named to TIME's 100 Most Influential People in AI 2025 for work on content authentication standards.

name: Navrina Singh
title: Founder and CEO, Credo AI; Member, National AI Advisory Committee (NAIAC)
notes_head: Founded Credo AI in 2020 as an AI governance SaaS platform after 18+ years at Microsoft and Qualcomm. Named Leader in Forrester Wave for AI Governance Solutions Q3 2025. Serves on Biden administration's National AI Advisory Committee and was appointed to UN Secretary-General's High-Level Advisory Body on AI in 2024. Hosts annual Responsible AI Leadership Summit bringing together 250+ industry leaders.

name: David Ha
title: Co-founder and CEO, Sakana AI
notes_head: Created AI Scientist system that produced the first AI-generated peer-reviewed workshop paper in 2025. Sakana AI achieved $1 billion valuation as fastest Japan-based company to unicorn status. Named to Time100 AI 2025 list for groundbreaking work in automated scientific discovery. Holds PhD from University of Tokyo and previously led Google Brain Research team in Japan.

name: Edwin Chen
title: Founder and CEO, Surge AI
notes_head: Built Surge AI to over $1 billion revenue in 2024 with fewer than 100 employees, completely bootstrapped without VC funding. Named to TIME's 100 Most Influential People in AI 2025. Works with major AI labs including Google, Anthropic, and OpenAI providing high-quality training data. Warns that the AI industry is 'optimizing for dopamine instead of truth' which could delay AGI progress.

name: Alan Descoins
title: CEO, Tryolabs
notes_head: Featured in TIME Magazine's 100 Most Influential People in AI 2025 for his work combining commercial AI applications with social good projects including partnerships with UNICEF and The Nature Conservancy. Has 15 years experience building AI systems and emphasizes the critical importance of organizational readiness and data foundations before AI implementation. Warns against attributing consciousness or therapeutic capabilities to AI systems and stresses the need for human oversight in high-stakes applications.

name: Kakul Srivastava
title: CEO, Splice
notes_head: CEO of Splice since May 2022, leading AI integration in music creation platform. Partnered with Universal Music Group in December 2025 on AI-powered music tools. Previously held senior product roles at major tech companies including Adobe, GitHub, and WeWork. MIT mechanical engineering graduate with Berkeley MBA.

name: Brandon Tseng
title: Co-founder and President, Shield AI
notes_head: Former Navy SEAL with deployments to Afghanistan who testified before Congress in December 2023 on AI for national security. Shield AI has successfully flown F-16s completely autonomously in combat scenarios and raised $1.5 billion in 2026 at a $12.7 billion valuation. Compares AI development to the Manhattan Project, warning the US must win the 'AI race' against China or risk losing military dominance for the next 80 years.

name: Jeff Leek
title: VP and Chief Data Officer, Fred Hutchinson Cancer Center; Co-founder and Co-CEO, Synthesize Bio
notes_head: Leads the Cancer AI Alliance (CAIA), a $40+ million initiative launched in October 2024 uniting four major cancer centers with tech partners including AWS, Microsoft, NVIDIA, and Google. Named to TIME's AI 100 Most Influential People in 2025. Previously described himself as an 'AI skeptic' until viewing AI as a 'better computer' and support tool in human-AI partnerships. Co-founded Synthesize Bio, a generative genomics company building AI models for biomedical experiments.

name: Mira Murati
title: Founder & CEO, Thinking Machines Lab; Former CTO, OpenAI
notes_head: Left OpenAI in September 2024 citing governance changes and founded Thinking Machines Lab in February 2025, raising $2 billion at $12 billion valuation. Served as interim OpenAI CEO during November 2023 leadership crisis. Led development of ChatGPT, DALL-E, GPT-4, and Sora. Founded Thinking Machines Lab with focus on embedding human values into AI systems and defeating nondeterminism in LLM inference.

name: Alvin Graylin
title: Chairman, Virtual World Society; Digital Fellow, Stanford HAI; Guest Lecturer, MIT; Former President, HTC China
notes_head: Co-authored 'Beyond Rivalry: A US-China Policy Framework for the Age of Transformative AI' (Dec 2024) arguing against zero-sum AI competition. Believes misuse risks from bad actors outweigh AI alignment risks and advocates for Universal Basic Individual Income (UBII) as economic safety net. Published 'Our Next Reality' (Hachette Books) on AI-XR convergence and spent decade leading HTC's VR business in China.

name: Sundar Pichai
title: CEO of Google and Alphabet Inc.
notes_head: CEO of Google since 2015 and Alphabet since 2019. Born in Chennai, India, earned bachelor's in metallurgy from IIT Kharagpur (1993), master's from Stanford (1995), MBA from Wharton (2002). Joined Google in 2004, led development of Chrome browser and Android. Announced $15 billion investment in India AI infrastructure including hub in Visakhapatnam. Became billionaire in 2025, earned $226 million in 2022. Led Google through major AI race with launch of Bard/Gemini chatbot.

name: Satya Nadella
title: Chairman and CEO, Microsoft
notes_head: Led Microsoft's $13B+ investment in OpenAI partnership since 2019. Established joint Microsoft-OpenAI Deployment Safety Board in 2021 for pre-release safety reviews. Published 'Governing AI: A Blueprint for the Future' (2023) outlining five-point regulatory framework. Under his leadership, Microsoft has nearly 350 employees working on responsible AI. Made voluntary commitments at 2023 White House AI Safety Summit including annual transparency reporting and pre-deployment safety testing.

name: Palmer Luckey
title: Founder and CEO
notes_head: Founded Oculus VR at age 19 in 2012, sold to Facebook for $2 billion in 2014. Fired from Facebook in 2017 after donating to pro-Trump political group, received $100+ million severance. Founded Anduril Industries in 2017, now valued at $14 billion as of August 2024. Company has secured major Pentagon contracts including $967M for Advanced Battle Management Systems (2020) and $1B counter-unmanned systems contract for SOCOM (2022). Received Pepperdine University's inaugural President's Award for Excellence in Freedom (2024). Net worth estimated at $3.5 billion by Forbes (2026).

name: Marc Benioff
notes_head: CEO of Salesforce and owner of TIME magazine. Major tech industry figure who has been increasingly vocal about AI regulation. At Davos 2026, called AI models "suicide coaches" and demanded reform of Section 230 to hold AI companies accountable for harms. Framed AI safety as a values question: "What's more important to us, growth or our kids?" Salesforce has launched Agentforce (enterprise AI agents) while Benioff simultaneously pushes for guardrails. Consistent pattern of calling for tech regulation — made similar calls about social media at Davos 2018. Advocates for responsible AI deployment

name: Allen Karns
notes_head: Chief AI and Technology Officer at Vanderbilt University, leading the Amplify Generative AI Innovation Center within the College of Connected Computing. 25+ years IT experience spanning cloud architecture, generative AI, and infrastructure. Built Amplify, Vanderbilt's open-source generative AI platform available to all faculty, staff, and students since Nov 2024. Briefed a U.S. Senate AI working group; keynote speaker at Meta, Oracle, Wells Fargo. Named 2025 AWS Champion by CDG/CDE. Featured in WSJ, Bloomberg, WaPo, CNN. Gartner Eye on Innovation finalist. Holds AWS Solutions Architect Profess

name: Brad Smith
notes_head: Brad Smith is Vice Chair and President of Microsoft, the company's top legal and policy voice. Prolific advocate for AI regulation: supports a dedicated federal AI regulatory agency, licensing regimes for frontier models and high-risk AI data centers, and the Blumenthal-Hawley AI framework. Told CBS the government should require companies to implement AI safety protocols. Testified before the Senate Judiciary Subcommittee. Warned in 2026 about Chinese AI subsidies undercutting American firms. Believes AI can be "both a tool and a weapon" — insists on human control with ability to "slow things

name: Daniel Barcay
notes_head: Daniel Barcay is a Senior Fellow at the Center for Humane Technology (CHT), where he previously served as Executive Director during the rapid arrival of generative AI. He develops intellectual frameworks to understand emerging AI issues and co-hosts CHT's podcast "Your Undivided Attention." Previously VP of Product at Planet Labs and a research fellow at the Berggruen Institute's Antikythera Program. He has written and spoken publicly about AI risks including advertising in AI systems, AI capturing human interiority, and AI's impact on work and relationships. Advocates for responsible AI deplo

name: Valérie Pisano
notes_head: Valérie Pisano is President and CEO of Mila – Quebec Artificial Intelligence Institute, leading one of the world's foremost academic AI labs. She advocates for responsible, ethics-centered AI governance and has emphasized the urgent need for international cooperation on AI regulation and safety standards.

name: Pierre Boivin
notes_head: Pierre Boivin is the Chairman of the Board of Directors at Mila (Quebec AI Institute), the world's largest academic research center in deep learning. He is also President and CEO of Claridge Inc., a private investment firm, and Chancellor of McGill University. Through his leadership of Mila's board, he supports the institute's mission under Scientific Director Yoshua Bengio, one of the most prominent voices on AI safety and governance. His role connects Montreal's business community with its AI research ecosystem.

name: Zach Seward
notes_head: Zach Seward is the Editorial Director of A.I. Initiatives at The New York Times, which he joined in 2023. He leads a newsroom team responsible for experimenting with generative AI and machine-learning techniques for journalism. Previously co-founded Quartz, the global business news organization, serving as CEO, editor-in-chief, and chief product officer over more than a decade. His team has visited nearly half of the NYT's 2,000 journalists across 40 desks to introduce AI tools and workflows. Developed Echo, an internal summarization assistant for journalists. Formerly a reporter at The Wall S

name: Yossi Matias
notes_head: Vice President of Engineering and Research at Google, Head of Google Research. Leads global teams in foundational ML, quantum computing, AI for health, climate, education, and conversational AI. Frames AI as 'an amplifier of human ingenuity' that empowers scientists, healthcare workers, and educators. On AI and employment, argues AI will create more research jobs, not fewer — cites AlphaFold as freeing researchers to tackle bigger questions. Previously a professor at Tel Aviv University. Israeli-American computer scientist with expertise in streaming algorithms and data mining.

name: Maria Basso
notes_head: Head of AI Applications and Impact at the World Economic Forum's Centre for AI Excellence (CAIE), appointed January 2025. Over six years at WEF; previously AI and Digital Technologies Portfolio Manager at the Centre for the Fourth Industrial Revolution. Background in mechanical and energy engineering (Politecnico di Torino, University of Illinois). Former research associate at UC Berkeley and business analyst at McKinsey. Leads WEF's work on real-world AI adoption, focusing on how organizations embed AI into everyday economic systems and maximize transformative potential.

name: Cathy Li
notes_head: Head of the Centre for AI Excellence and Member of the Executive Committee at the World Economic Forum. Oversees the AI Governance Alliance (AIGA), a flagship initiative convening leaders from business, government, academia, and civil society to prepare for the 'intelligent era.' Argues AI leadership depends not on infrastructure volume but deployment effectiveness. Warns most organizations 'fundamentally misunderstand AI's transformative potential' and underestimate the mindset shift required. Champions responsible, transparent, and inclusive AI system design. Focuses on building frameworks,

name: Peter Lee
notes_head: Peter Lee is President of Microsoft Science (formerly President of Microsoft Research), overseeing AI research strategy and scientific discovery initiatives. Under his leadership, Microsoft Research has focused on responsible AI, including tools for detecting hallucinations and improving LLM fact-checking. Lee champions AI as a collaborative partner in scientific discovery, envisioning AI lab assistants that suggest and run experiments. He has emphasized that the true value of AI "hasn't been invented" yet and advocates for deployment infrastructure to deliver AI globally. Spoke at EmTech AI 2

name: Ashok Elluswamy
notes_head: Ashok Elluswamy is VP of AI at Tesla, leading both the Autopilot/FSD (Full Self-Driving) and Optimus humanoid robot programs. He champions Tesla's end-to-end neural network approach to autonomous driving, which trains a single network for perception, planning, and control — contrasting with the modular, sensor-heavy systems used by competitors. Told internal teams that 2026 would be "the most demanding year of their careers." Predicts AGI could become reality by ~2035 based on deep learning advances. The same neural network architecture powering FSD is being extended to Optimus.

name: Larry Fink
notes_head: CEO and Chairman of BlackRock, world's largest asset manager (~$11.5T AUM). Strong AI investment advocate. 2026 chairman's letter warned AI will widen wealth gap unless investing access expands. Leads BlackRock's Global AI Infrastructure Investment Partnership (with MGX, Microsoft). Views AI infrastructure buildout as $7T+ opportunity. Identifies power/energy as key bottleneck, not chips. Predicts some AI companies will go bankrupt but sees that as healthy capitalism.

name: Rob Goldstein
notes_head: COO of BlackRock. Oversees Aladdin platform, technology, and operations. Called AI "the most significant technology, innovation, evolution, revolution of my 30-year career." Launched Asimov, BlackRock's AI research platform (Jun 2025) that scans filings, research notes, and emails to generate portfolio insights. ~25% of BlackRock employees are technologists under his purview. Advocates aggressive AI adoption across investment operations.

name: Nimar Arora
notes_head: AI researcher and entrepreneur specializing in probabilistic programming and Bayesian inference. Co-founder of Bayesian Logic Inc. (2012), which builds Bayesian models for complex physical domains, particularly seismology. Invented the Bean Machine Probabilistic Programming Language and the NET-VISA probabilistic model for detecting nuclear explosions (used by CTBTO). PhD from UC Berkeley (2006-2012) under Stuart Russell and Erik Sudderth. Received the Mitchell Prize in 2014 from the American Statistical Association. Previously Research Scientist at Facebook (designed a new PPL), Head of AI an

name: Ylli Bajraktari
notes_head: President and CEO of the Special Competitive Studies Project (SCSP), founded by Eric Schmidt in 2021 to strengthen U.S. competitiveness in AI and emerging technologies. Previously served as Executive Director of the National Security Commission on Artificial Intelligence (NSCAI, 2019-2021), chaired by Eric Schmidt, where he assembled 130+ staff to produce the landmark NSCAI final report. Career national security official: Chief of Staff to National Security Advisor H.R. McMaster, leadership roles under Deputy SecDef Robert Work, Special Assistant to CJCS Gen. Dempsey. Born in Kosovo, emigrated

name: Jean-François Gagné
notes_head: Co-founder and former CEO of Element AI, a Montreal-based applied AI research lab co-founded with Yoshua Bengio and Nicolas Chapados. Element AI was acquired by ServiceNow in 2020 for ~$500M. Subsequently served as Head of AI Product Management and Strategy at ServiceNow. As of 2025-2026, working on a stealth AI startup based in Montreal. Recognized as one of Fast Company's Most Creative People in Business (2019). Active voice in Canada's AI ecosystem, advocating for AI commercialization and startup development. Element AI was a notable early attempt to bridge academic AI research and enterpri

name: Mark Nitzberg
notes_head: Executive Director of the Center for Human-Compatible AI (CHAI) at UC Berkeley since 2016, and Interim Executive Director of IASEAI. Also head of strategic outreach for Berkeley AI Research (BAIR). Former CEO of The Blindsight Corporation (acquired by Amazon A9).

name: Mohammed bin Salman
notes_head: Crown Prince and Prime Minister of Saudi Arabia. Architect of Vision 2030, which places AI as central pillar of economic diversification. Chairs Humain (PIF-backed AI company). Established Saudi Data & AI Authority (SDAIA) in 2019. National Strategy for Data & AI targets 20K AI specialists, 300+ AI startups, $20B investment by 2030. Designated 2026 as Year of Artificial Intelligence. Oversaw $40B+ in AI infrastructure commitments.

name: Mark Chen
notes_head: Chief Research Officer at OpenAI. Joined 2018 after working as quantitative trader at Jane Street Capital. Led development of Codex (adapting GPT-3 for code generation), co-led vision team for GPT-4 (image recognition integration), contributed to DALL-E. Advocates focusing on "main quest" of AGI over short-term product competition. Views math/coding proficiency as "bedrock for a far more general form of intelligence." Background in competitive programming.

name: Greg Brockman
notes_head: Co-founder and President of OpenAI. Took a sabbatical Aug–Nov 2024, then returned. In 2025 led OpenAI's Stargate infrastructure buildout and co-founded the $100M 'Leading the Future' super PAC opposing state-level AI regulation.

name: Ben Mann
notes_head: Co-founder of Anthropic. Previously architect of GPT-3 at OpenAI. Left OpenAI in 2020 with seven colleagues to found Anthropic because safety was not the top priority. Advocates layered safety approach ("Swiss Cheese Model of Defense"). Key contributor to Constitutional AI methodology. Estimates 50th percentile chance of superintelligence by ~2028. Describes alignment challenge as "how do we keep God in a box."

name: Clay Bavor
notes_head: Co-founder of Sierra AI (with Bret Taylor), an enterprise AI agent platform valued at $10B. Former Google VP (18 years), led Google Labs, AR/VR, Project Starline, Google Lens. Sierra hit $100M ARR in 7 quarters. AI optimist focused on applied/enterprise AI.

name: Shane Legg
notes_head: Co-founder and Chief AGI Scientist at Google DeepMind, leading its Technical AGI Safety team. Has predicted a 50% chance of human-level AGI by 2028 since 2009, reaffirmed publicly in 2025. Advocates for deliberative, human-auditable AI safety mechanisms to address alignment and misuse risks.

name: Mustafa Suleyman
notes_head: Mustafa Suleyman co-founded DeepMind (2010) and Inflection AI (2022), and became CEO of Microsoft AI in 2024. He argues AI is 'already superhuman' and predicts human-level task performance within 5-7 years. He advocates for humanist superintelligence with strong human control, global regulations, and has pledged to halt development if AI poses existential risk.

name: Joe Biden
notes_head: 46th U.S. President (2021-2025). Issued Executive Order 14110 in October 2023, the most comprehensive U.S. AI governance action to that point, requiring safety reporting from frontier AI developers and coordinating federal agency standards. Also released the Blueprint for an AI Bill of Rights in 2022.

name: Justin Johnson
notes_head: Co-founder of World Labs (spatial intelligence startup, founded 2024 with Fei-Fei Li, Christoph Lassner, Ben Mildenhall). Assistant Professor of CS at University of Michigan. PhD from Stanford (advised by Fei-Fei Li). Key contributor to early PyTorch development. Created CLEVR visual reasoning benchmark. NSF CAREER Award recipient. World Labs raised $1B+ at $5B valuation, building Large World Models for 3D perception.

name: Christoph Lassner
notes_head: Co-founder of World Labs (spatial intelligence startup, founded 2024 with Fei-Fei Li, Justin Johnson, Ben Mildenhall). PhD from University of Tubingen. Previously led research teams at Epic Games and Meta Reality Labs. Created Pulsar differentiable renderer (precursor to Gaussian Splatting). Built human pose estimation for Amazon Halo (via Bodylabs acquisition). Specialist in 3D computer vision and graphics.

name: Ben Mildenhall
notes_head: Co-founder of World Labs (spatial intelligence startup, founded 2024 with Fei-Fei Li, Justin Johnson, Christoph Lassner). Co-creator of NeRF (Neural Radiance Fields), ECCV 2020 honorable mention for best paper. PhD from UC Berkeley (advised by Ren Ng), Hertz Fellow. Former Google Research scientist (2021-2023). Research spans computational photography, 3D reconstruction, and text-to-3D generation.

name: Alex LeBrun
notes_head: CEO of AMI Labs (Advanced Machine Intelligence), co-founded with Yann LeCun (2025). Serial French AI entrepreneur: founded VirtuOz (acquired by Nuance), Wit.ai (acquired by Facebook 2015 after 21 months), and Nabla (healthcare AI, remains chairman). Worked under LeCun at Meta FAIR after Wit.ai acquisition. 24 years building AI products spanning chatbots, NLU, virtual assistants, healthcare AI. Graduate of École Polytechnique.

name: President Biden
notes_head: DUPLICATE of entity 1376 (Joe Biden). Same person — '46th U.S. President (2021-2025).' Issued Executive Order 14110 (Oct 2023), the most comprehensive U.S. AI governance action, requiring safety reporting from frontier AI developers and coordinating federal agency standards. Released Blueprint for an AI Bill of Rights (2022). Also issued EO on AI Infrastructure (Jan 2025). EO 14110 was rescinded by President Trump on Jan 20, 2025. Recommend merging edges to entity 1376 and soft-deleting this entity.

name: Brian Long
notes_head: CEO and Co-Founder of Adaptive Security, an AI-native cybersecurity startup simulating deepfake and GenAI attacks for corporate defense training. Serial entrepreneur: previously co-founded Attentive (SMS/email marketing platform, grew to $500M+ ARR, 8,000+ customers, 1,000+ employees) and TapCommerce (acquired by Twitter, where he served as Senior Director leading mobile teams for 19 months). Raised ~$900M+ in venture capital across ventures from Sequoia Capital and other top-tier firms. Under his leadership Adaptive Security raised $136M+ from OpenAI and Andreessen Horowitz. University of Pen

name: Matt Fredrikson
notes_head: Associate Professor, Carnegie Mellon School of Computer Science (ECE). Co-founder and CEO of Gray Swan AI. Research: secure, fair, reliable ML — adversarial robustness, privacy, formal methods. Pioneered methods to identify LLM vulnerabilities. Ran largest public red-teaming competition: 22 frontier AI agents, 44 deployment scenarios, 1.8M prompt-injection attacks, 60K+ successful policy violations. Found limited correlation between agent robustness and model size/capability. Work on representation engineering addresses honesty, harmlessness, and power-seeking. Multiple best paper awards at se

name: Zico Kolter
notes_head: Professor of Computer Science and Head of Machine Learning Department at Carnegie Mellon University. Co-founder and Chief Scientist of Gray Swan AI. Serves on OpenAI Board of Directors and chairs Safety & Security Committee — has authority to delay or block unsafe model releases. CA and DE regulators made Kolter's safety oversight a key condition of OpenAI's restructuring. Also on Qualcomm board. PhD Stanford (2010), postdoc MIT (2010-2012). Developed first methods for deep learning with guaranteed robustness. Takes broad view of AI safety: 'not just existential concerns' but 'the entire swath

name: Rob Jenks
notes_head: Chief Strategy Officer at Gray Swan AI (appointed January 2026). Previously SVP of Strategy & Innovation at Tanium — led product innovation, strategic partnerships, technology alliances for Fortune 2000 enterprises. Before Tanium, led product and go-to-market at C3.ai, an early enterprise AI leader. PhD in Physics from Harvard (used cloud computing and ML to study brain function). Based in Seattle. At Gray Swan, leads global AI security market expansion, bringing enterprise go-to-market expertise to AI safety/security startup.

name: Nick Winter
notes_head: VP Product & Growth at Gray Swan AI (since Jan 2025), focusing on AI red-teaming and safety evaluation tools. Previously co-founded CodeCombat (Y Combinator, 2013), a gamified platform that taught CS to 20M+ learners, and Skritter (2007), a language-learning app. Co-authored D-REX benchmark paper on detecting deceptive reasoning in LLMs (2024). Background in software engineering and ed-tech entrepreneurship.

name: Claire Zabel
notes_head: Managing Director, Short Timelines Special Projects at Coefficient Giving (formerly Open Philanthropy). Leads a team researching and implementing initiatives on the assumption transformative AI may arrive soon. Previously ran Global Catastrophic Risks Capacity Building (2018-2024) and oversaw Biosecurity & Pandemic Preparedness (2018-2019, 2023-2024). Former Research Analyst at GiveWell. B.S. and M.S. in Earth Systems from Stanford University. Key grantmaker shaping EA/AI safety funding landscape.

name: Buck Shlegeris
notes_head: CEO of Redwood Research, an AI safety nonprofit focused on AI control — ensuring safe deployment of potentially misaligned AI. Co-founder of Redwood and board member at ARC. Argues that robust control techniques may be humanity's best near-term safeguard given insufficient industry investment in alignment.

name: Nate Thomas
notes_head: Co-founder and CEO of Redwood Research, a nonprofit AI safety lab in Berkeley founded in 2021. Also co-founded Constellation, a shared office space for longtermist organizations fostering cross-org collaboration. Physics PhD with ML publications. Led Redwood's research on AI control, interpretability, and alignment techniques. Redwood is known for applied alignment research including activation patching and AI control evaluations.

name: Bill Zito
notes_head: Co-founder and former COO of Redwood Research (2021-2023), an AI safety nonprofit. Currently Associate Director at the RAND Center on AI, Security, and Technology (RAND CAST) and the RAND Meselson Center (since Feb 2025), working on AI, cybersecurity, and biosecurity policy. Bridges the gap between technical AI safety research and national security policy. Previously helped build Redwood's operations and co-created the Constellation shared workspace.

name: Tristan Cook
notes_head: Managing Director at the Center on Long-Term Risk (CLR), leading work on reducing suffering risks (s-risks) from powerful AI systems. Succeeded Jesse Clifton as MD. Holds a Master of Advanced Study (MASt) in Mathematics from the University of Cambridge (2020-2021). Based in London. CLR focuses on worst-case outcomes from advanced AI including multi-agent conflict scenarios. Oversees both empirical and conceptual research programs.

name: Eric Ho
notes_head: Co-founder and CEO of Goodfire, a leading AI interpretability research company founded in June 2024. Goodfire develops mechanistic interpretability tools to understand, audit, and edit neural networks at the feature level. Co-founded with Dan Balsam (CTO) and Tom McGrath (Chief Scientist, ex-DeepMind interpretability). Raised $50M Series A led by Menlo Ventures, then $150M Series B at $1.25B valuation led by B Capital. Team includes Lee Sharkey (pioneer of sparse autoencoders, co-founded Apollo Research) and Nick Cammarata (started OpenAI's interpretability team). Partners include Mayo Clinic

name: Sarah Friar
notes_head: CFO of OpenAI since June 2024 -- the company's first CFO. Born 1972 in Sion Mills, Northern Ireland. Studied metallurgy, economics, and management at Oxford; MBA from Stanford (Arjay Miller scholar). Career: McKinsey analyst in South Africa, Goldman Sachs equity research MD, Salesforce SVP Finance & Strategy, Square CFO (led 2015 IPO, added $30B market cap), Nextdoor CEO (led 2021 SPAC IPO). Board member at Walmart and ConsenSys. Co-chairs Stanford's Digital Economy Lab. Co-founded Ladies Who Launch (nonprofit for female entrepreneurs). OBE from Queen Elizabeth II (2019). Honorary doctorate fr

name: Ankesh Chandaria
notes_head: CEO of the AI Safety Foundation, which increases awareness and scientific understanding of catastrophic AI risks through education and research. Background spans law, governance, and AI policy -- previously practiced as a litigation and regulatory investigations lawyer. Founded theConcept.ai. Affiliated with Periscope Lab at the University of Toronto. Currently pursuing a Master's in AI Ethics and Society with the Leverhulme Centre for the Future of Intelligence at the University of Cambridge. Published on Canadian AI governance and AI creativity. Served on boards including the Art Gallery of

name: Richard Phillips
notes_head: Chair of the Board at the AI Safety Foundation (AISF), a Canadian registered charity focused on catastrophic AI risk education and research. Former Deputy Chairman of CIBC World Markets with decades of experience in investment management and capital markets. Founded his own investment firm. Active in non-profit governance across poverty reduction, education, arts, and financial regulation.

name: Meg Sintzel
notes_head: Director at the AI Safety Foundation (AISF). Former Managing Director of Marketing and Communications at Accenture Canada, where she worked for 36 years before stepping down in 2025. Previously held roles at Toronto Region Board of Trade and National Public Relations. Brings corporate communications and technology industry expertise to AISF board governance.

name: Bill Morris
notes_head: Director at the AI Safety Foundation (AISF). Former CEO of Accenture Canada with over 35 years at the firm, holding leadership roles across North America and globally. Serves on several corporate and non-profit boards and is active in the business technology space. Brings executive and governance experience to AISF.

name: Lila Ibrahim
notes_head: COO of Google DeepMind (joined 2018 as COO of DeepMind, now Chief AI Readiness Officer per LinkedIn). Leads operations, governance & ethics, policy, communications, and people & culture. Previously VP/GM at Coursera and spent 18 years at Intel. Signed the CAIS statement that "mitigating the risk of extinction from AI should be a global priority." Member of UK AI Council and Council on Foreign Relations. Named 2025 CNBC Changemaker. Advocates for international coordination and proactive governance; believes the tech industry is at a critical juncture for responsible AI.

name: Emmett Shear
notes_head: Co-founder and former CEO of Twitch (2011-2023). Briefly served as interim CEO of OpenAI (Nov 19-21, 2023) during the Sam Altman firing crisis. Co-founder and CEO of Softmax, a 10-person AI startup focused on "organic alignment" — teaching AIs to cooperate with humans using principles from biology and collective intelligence. Backed by a16z. Previously co-founded Stem AI (incorporated June 2023). Estimates his P(doom) at 5-50%. Believes AI will improve so rapidly it could overwhelm creators, but generally favors limited regulation except for AGI-level systems. Advocates for alignment research

name: Colleen Mckenzie
notes_head: Executive Director of the AI Objectives Institute (AOI), the nonprofit co-founded by the late Peter Eckersley. Researcher, designer, and product strategist. Co-founded the Median Group, a scientific research nonprofit exploring technological trends and societal impacts. Past roles include Product Manager and Software Engineer at Google, Chief of Staff at the Center for Humane Technology, and Head of Product at distributed computing startup Kalix Systems. B.A. in Computer Science and Neuroscience from Columbia University. Presented on scaling deliberation at Foresight Institute.

name: Stacey Svetlichnaya
notes_head: Chief Technology Officer at the AI Objectives Institute (AOI). Research interests in climate change and responsible AI. Previously a deep learning engineer at Weights & Biases, building developer tools for ML visualization, explainability, reproducibility, and collaboration. Earlier work on image search, productionizing ML systems, and discovery/recommendation at Flickr. Collaborating with AOI since spring 2024. Works at the intersection of ML research, application, and UX for vision and language models.

name: Brittney G
notes_head: Vice President and Board Chair at the AI Objectives Institute (AOI), which she co-founded with Peter Eckersley in 2021. Storyteller and creator focused on human interaction with technology. Interviewed technologists and thought-leaders for Digital Village, one of the oldest public radio shows in Los Angeles. Also builds and manages large-scale software systems for media companies. AOI was created with the hope of building AI tools that enhance human agency.

name: Maria de la Lama
notes_head: Head of Operations at Epoch AI and member of its Board of Directors (alongside Tom Davidson, Ajeya Cotra, and Jaime Sevilla). Previously Special Projects Coordinator at Rethink Priorities and Co-Founder of Contexto Estudio. Holds MSc in Philosophy of the Social Sciences from LSE. Plays a key operational and governance role at Epoch AI, which produces datasets and analysis used by policymakers worldwide for AI governance decisions.

name: Jonas Vollmer
notes_head: EA-aligned executive and co-founder of Center on Long-Term Risk. Held senior roles at EA Funds and Centre for Effective Altruism before joining Macroscopic Ventures as CFO and AI Futures Project as COO. Focuses on scenario forecasting and strategic grantmaking to improve the trajectory of advanced AI development.

name: David Norman
notes_head: Managing Director of the Cooperative AI Foundation (CAIF) since November 2023. Previously held roles at London Initiative for Safe AI, Global Insight Consulting, Saferworld, and SABMiller. Was Director of Campaigns / Director of External Affairs at WWF UK (2008-2013). Brings experience from advocacy, policy campaigns, and international NGO management to CAIF leadership.

name: Allison Duettmann
notes_head: President and CEO of Foresight Institute, a San Francisco nonprofit advancing beneficial frontier technologies. Co-initiated Foresight's AI Safety Grant Program. Focuses on existential risk reduction, AI governance, and longtermist coordination. Co-authored 'Gaming the Future' and 'Superintelligence: Coordination & Strategy'.

name: Siméon Campos
notes_head: Founder and Executive Director of SaferAI, a French nonprofit focused on AI risk management and governance. Technical expert at ISO/IEC and CEN-CENELEC standards bodies. Previously co-founded EffiSciences, training hundreds across France/Europe in responsible AI. SaferAI developed AI risk management ratings for frontier labs (featured in TIME, Euractiv, Le Monde). Active in EU AI Act Code of Practice, NIST US AISIC, and OECD G7 Hiroshima Process.

name: Henry Papadatos
notes_head: Managing Director of SaferAI. Created AI risk management ratings for frontier developers (featured twice in TIME, the Guardian, Euractiv). Member of EU AI Act Code of Practice expert working group on risk taxonomy for GPAI models. Joined OECD expert group on AI Risk & Accountability. Previously conducted LLM alignment research at CHAI (Center for Human-Compatible AI) at UC Berkeley. Published framework integrating AI practices with traditional risk management. Current research on quantitative AI-enabled cyber risk modeling.

name: Amba Kak
notes_head: Co-Executive Director of AI Now Institute. TIME 100 Most Influential People in AI 2024. Indian-born, US-based technology policy expert. Joined AI Now in 2019 as Director of Global Policy; became Co-ED in 2022. Senior advisor on AI to the FTC (2021). Testified before Congress (Oct 2023) and Senate Commerce Committee (Jul 2024) on AI and data privacy. Spoke before UN General Assembly on AI governance. Central thesis: AI concentrates power in Big Tech; advocates antitrust enforcement, industrial policy for alternative AI pathways, and redistributive taxation. Editor of "Regulating Biometrics" col

name: Kate Brennan
notes_head: Associate Director at AI Now Institute, spearheading policy and research programs. Yale Law School graduate. Prior roles in tech including product marketing at Google and digital marketing for progressive nonprofits. Advocates strong antitrust enforcement in AI; testified before NYC Council Committee on Technology (Sep 2024). Published on Google Search monopoly remedies and risks of weak AI regulation. Argues Big Tech dominance over AI infrastructure threatens public interest.

name: Rudolf Laine
notes_head: Co-founder of Workshop Labs PBC, a public benefit corporation focused on preventing human disempowerment from AI. Oxford-educated, London-based. Workshop Labs builds privacy-preserving AI infrastructure (Silo stack for cloud-based training/inference of trillion-parameter models). Active in London AI safety ecosystem, sponsoring AI security hackathons with BlueDot Impact and Entrepreneurs First. Previously involved with AI safety research community.

name: Anthony Armstrong
notes_head: Former CFO of xAI, appointed October 2025, departed April 2026 amid wave of senior exits. Previously global head of technology M&A at Morgan Stanley beginning 2015, where he advised Elon Musk during the 2022 Twitter acquisition. Served as senior advisor to the Office of Personnel Management during DOGE (Department of Government Efficiency). At xAI, oversaw finances of both xAI and X (merged April 2025), responsible for steering X back to financial stability after advertiser exodus.

name: Peter McIntyre
notes_head: Co-founder of Trajectory Labs PBC, a stealth AI security startup building safety evaluations and reinforcement learning environments for frontier AI labs. Co-founded with Ben West. Previously CEO of Non-Trivial (grew to 40,000+ applicants). Prior experience at Centre for the Governance of AI (GovAI), Longview Philanthropy, and 80,000 Hours. Note: Trajectory Labs PBC (the security startup) is a separate entity from Trajectory Labs (the Toronto AI safety coworking nonprofit). AI safety background spans policy, philanthropic funding, and now technical evaluation work.

name: Daniel Gross
notes_head: Israeli-American entrepreneur who co-founded Cue (acquired by Apple 2013), was a YC partner, and launched Pioneer. Co-founded Safe Superintelligence Inc. with Ilya Sutskever in 2024; left as CEO in July 2025 to join Meta Superintelligence Labs.

name: Daniel Levy
notes_head: Co-founder and President of Safe Superintelligence Inc. (SSI), alongside Ilya Sutskever and Daniel Gross. PhD in Computer Science from Stanford. Former AI researcher/engineer at OpenAI with strong safety focus. Based in Palo Alto. SSI raised $1B Series A (Sept 2024, $5B valuation) and subsequently $2B (2025, $32B valuation) from investors including Andreessen Horowitz, Sequoia, DST Global, SV Angel, Greenoaks Capital, Alphabet, and Nvidia. SSI is focused solely on safely developing superintelligent AI systems. Levy became president following Daniel Gross departure; Sutskever took CEO role.

name: Jesse Hoogland
notes_head: Co-founder and Executive Director of Timaeus, an AI safety research organization applying Singular Learning Theory (SLT) to AI alignment and interpretability. MSc in theoretical physics from University of Amsterdam. Previously co-founded a health-tech startup before pivoting to AI safety. Timaeus develops "developmental interpretability" — using the geometry of loss landscapes to understand neural network structure. MATS mentor stream with Daniel Murfet. Research published on Alignment Forum and academic venues. Dutch-born.

name: Max Dalton
notes_head: Co-founder and Executive Director of Forethought, an AI macrostrategy research group (launched mid-2024) co-founded with Will MacAskill, Tom Davidson, and Amrit Sidhu-Brar. Previously Executive Director of the Centre for Effective Altruism (CEA). First-class degree in Philosophy, Politics, and Economics from Oxford; MSc in Economics specializing in economics of technological change. Forethought aims to navigate the transition to superintelligent AI systems, tackling the most important questions unrestricted by the current Overton window. Built out of the old Forethought Foundation for Global P

name: Andrea Miotti
notes_head: Founder and CEO of ControlAI, a nonprofit dedicated to mitigating catastrophic AI risks. Lead author of "A Narrow Path," a comprehensive policy plan proposing a 20-year ban on superintelligence development, an IAEA-like international AI authority, and prohibition of dangerous AI capabilities such as automated AI research. Previously Head of Strategy and Governance at Conjecture. Has built a coalition of 100+ cross-party UK parliamentarians demanding binding regulation of powerful AI systems. Has given written evidence to UK Parliament and testified before Canadian parliamentary committee on AI

name: Adam Gleave
notes_head: CEO and co-founder of FAR.AI, an AI safety research nonprofit based in Berkeley focused on adversarial robustness, model evaluation, interpretability, and alignment. PhD from UC Berkeley under Stuart Russell; previously at Google DeepMind with Jan Leike and Geoffrey Irving. Board member of Safe AI Forum, London Initiative for Safe AI, and METR. AI2050 Fellow at Schmidt Sciences. Challenges the innovation-vs-safety dichotomy, arguing that technical innovation in safety evaluations and verification can overcome the binary. Organized a 2025 DC event with 150+ technical experts and policymakers on

name: Karl Berzins
notes_head: Co-founder and COO (later President) of FAR.AI, an AI safety research nonprofit founded in 2022 with Adam Gleave. Manages operations for the organization which conducts technical research in adversarial robustness, model evaluation, interpretability, and alignment. Previously Head of Strategy at Advanced Navigation (Australian robotics company), spent 3 years at Swire, and was Chief of Staff at a Berlin-based software automation company. Holds Bachelor of Engineering and Bachelor of Commerce from UNSW, Australia.

name: Marc Warner
notes_head: CEO and co-founder of Faculty AI, a UK-based AI company providing software, consulting, and services to government and enterprise. Faculty was acquired by Accenture in January 2026 for over 600 million GBP; Warner became Accenture CTO. Former member of the UK Prime Ministers AI Council and Digital Economic Advisory Council. Helped the NHS use AI during the pandemic. PhD in physics; Marie Curie Fellow at Harvard specializing in quantum sensing. Lab partner at Imperial College with Elena Andreicheva, inspiring the Intelligence Rising documentary. Advocates domain-specific AI regulation, differen

name: Jenny Marron
notes_head: Executive Director of the Institute for AI Policy and Strategy (IAPS), a nonpartisan think tank producing policy research on implications of AI from today's advanced models to potential AGI and superintelligence. Joined IAPS as Director of Policy and Engagement in October 2024 before being appointed Executive Director. Leads IAPS mission translating AI research into practical guidance for government, industry, and civil society. Extensive background in foreign policy, national security, and bipartisan engagement — previously served at the White House National Security Council (NSC) and the Dep

name: Peter Wildeford
notes_head: Co-founder and Chief Strategy Officer at IAPS; co-founded Rethink Priorities (served as Co-CEO 2018-2024). Head of Policy at The AI Policy Network. Board member of Metaculus. Avid forecaster (top-20 on Metaculus). Advocates evidence-based AI policy: transparency, third-party verification, whistleblower protections. Supports targeted regulation including reliability standards, incident reporting, autonomous weapons oversight, and AI surveillance limits. Criticized use of Defense Production Act against AI companies as regulatory overreach.

name: Amanda El-Dakhakhni
notes_head: Director at IAPS leading the compute policy team with increasing focus on policy engagement. Previously advised emerging technology companies including Uber, Cruise (GM), and other Fortune 500 firms and tech startups on policy. Held policy and legal roles in U.S. Senate, U.S. Attorney's Office, and the White House. J.D. from Yale Law School, B.A. from University of Pennsylvania. Brings legal and policy expertise to AI governance at the intersection of technology and public policy.

name: Zoe Williams
notes_head: Managing Director at IAPS overseeing the organization's three research teams and driving organizational planning and strategic execution.
