Previously worked at Rethink Priorities and Trade Me. Manages day-to-day operations and the Frontier Security research program. Provides organization-wide leadership for IAPS's nonpartisan AI policy research.

name: Connor Axiotes
notes_head: UK-based AI policy and communications expert. Director of Communications and Lead on Resilience & Risk at the Adam Smith Institute; former Strategic Communications Lead at Conjecture. Authored ASI paper 'Tipping Point: On the Edge of Superintelligence' (2023). Executive producer of 'Making God', a feature documentary on AGI risks. Affiliated with Leaf Courses' 'Dilemmas and Dangers in AI' program for students aged 15-19. Also involved with Control AI campaign against AI-generated deepfakes.

name: Rob Fergus
notes_head: Rob Fergus is a computer scientist and Director of AI Research / Head of FAIR (Fundamental AI Research) at Meta. He co-founded FAIR with Yann LeCun in 2013 and co-founded the CILVR Lab at NYU in 2009. Fergus left Meta in 2020 to join Google DeepMind as a research director, then returned to Meta in May 2025 to lead FAIR, replacing Joelle Pineau. His research spans computer vision, deep learning, and large-scale AI systems. Under his leadership, FAIR pursues long-term AI research including robotics, audio generation, image understanding, and frontier capabilities. Meta's AI strategy emphasizes o

name: Serkan Piantino
notes_head: Serkan Piantino is a technology executive who co-founded Facebook AI Research (FAIR) in 2013 with Yann LeCun. At Facebook, he led development of the Big Sur open-source GPU server (with Quanta and Nvidia) that doubled AI research speed, directed Timeline's redesign, and established Facebook's New York engineering office. He left Facebook in 2016 and founded Spell in 2017, a cloud platform for AI processing hardware. Reddit acquired Spell in June 2022 and Piantino joined as VP of Foundational Product, later becoming VP Product at Reddit. His edge listing as 'Founder' of Meta AI reflects his 201

name: Jérôme Pesenti
notes_head: Former VP of AI at Meta/Facebook (2018–2022), where he led AI research and applied AI teams overseeing hundreds of scientists and engineers. Co-chaired an independent AI review commissioned by the UK government that led to a sector deal. Previously at IBM (Watson). In 2023 founded Sizzle AI, an AI learning platform used by 1.7M+ people. Sizzle was acquired by Campus (Series B edtech backed by Founders Fund, Sam Altman, Shaquille O'Neal) in Oct 2025; Pesenti became CTO. Has publicly called out AI bias risks (e.g., GPT-3 toxic output) and advocated for legal frameworks that protect vulnerable po

name: David Singleton
notes_head: Former CTO of Stripe (7 years) and VP of Engineering at Google (11 years, Android). In 2024 co-founded Dreamer (with Hugo Barra, Nicholas Jitkoff, Ficus Kirkpatrick), an AI agent OS startup that raised $56M seed from Founders Fund and others. Dreamer was acqui-hired by Meta in March 2026; Singleton and team joined Meta's Superintelligence Labs under Alexandr Wang to build personalized AI agents across Meta's apps and wearables. Focus is on making AI agents mainstream through new UI patterns, privacy models, and developer platforms.

name: Hugo Barra
notes_head: Former VP at Meta Reality Labs (2017–2021) overseeing Oculus/VR hardware. Previously VP of Android at Google and VP of International at Xiaomi. Co-founded Dreamer in 2024 with David Singleton, Nicholas Jitkoff, and Ficus Kirkpatrick. In March 2026 returned to Meta via acqui-hire of Dreamer, joining Superintelligence Labs under Alexandr Wang. Focus is on autonomous AI agents that integrate across Meta's apps and wearable devices (AI glasses). His return signals Meta's strategic pivot from VR to AI.

name: Nicholas Jitkoff
notes_head: Designer and technologist. Former Senior Design Director at Figma; principal designer on Google Chrome and Chrome OS; held senior design roles at Dropbox. Co-founded Dreamer in 2024 as Chief Design Officer, building an AI agent operating system. In March 2026, Dreamer was acqui-hired by Meta; Jitkoff joined Meta's Superintelligence Labs under Alexandr Wang to work on AI agent UX across Meta's apps and wearables. Dreamer licensed its technology to Meta as part of the deal.

name: Judd Rosenblatt
notes_head: Founder and CEO of AE Studio (est. 2016), a bootstrapped 150+ person company focused on software development, brain-computer interfaces, and AI alignment research. Left a food delivery startup to work on ensuring AI safety. Engages with National Security Council, White House OSTP, congressional staff, and frontier AI lab founders on AI policy. Leads AE Studio's 'neglected approaches' alignment agenda including BCI-OS and self-other overlap research. Active in Effective Altruism community. Yale alum.

name: Marc Carauleanu
notes_head: AI Safety Researcher at AE Studio (since 2023). First-class honors in Artificial Intelligence from Oxford Brookes University. Previously at Stanford Existential Risks Initiative researching strategies to reduce AI existential threats. Pioneered 'self-other overlap' fine-tuning technique inspired by cognitive neuroscience to reduce LLM deception. Co-authored AE Studio's alignment research roadmap. Work on neural self-other overlap demonstrated measurable reduction in deceptive behaviors in RL agents.

name: Arthur Mensch
notes_head: CEO and co-founder of Mistral AI, the leading European frontier AI company. Former research scientist at Meta FAIR (Facebook AI Research), where he worked on large language models. Co-founded Mistral in April 2023 with Guillaume Lample and Timothée Lacroix. Strong advocate for open-source AI models and European AI sovereignty. Skeptical of existential risk narratives, arguing they lack scientific evidence and are conflated with national security concerns. Advocates regulating AI applications rather than base models. Proposed a revenue-based content levy on AI companies in Europe to compensate

name: Jacob Hilton
notes_head: President of the Alignment Research Center (ARC), a nonprofit pursuing theoretical research on mechanistic interpretability of neural networks. Previously at OpenAI where he co-led development of InstructGPT (predecessor to ChatGPT), pioneering RLHF techniques. Was among 12 former OpenAI employees in a Harvard law filing advocating for whistleblower protections at AI labs. Estimates ~5-10% chance AI causes existential catastrophe by 2100; believes only about half of existential risk from AI comes from misalignment, with misuse also important. PhD in mathematics (set theory).

name: Nick Beckstead
notes_head: Co-Founder & CEO of Secure AI Project, which develops and advocates for pragmatic policies to reduce risks of severe harm from advanced AI. Former CEO of the FTX Future Fund, early employee and Program Officer at Open Philanthropy (science philanthropy and ML), Policy Lead at Center for AI Safety, Research Fellow at Oxford Future of Humanity Institute. PhD in Philosophy from Rutgers, BA in Mathematics and Philosophy from University of Minnesota. Major EA figure. Praised California SB 53 as showing "what's possible when policymakers act with foresight."

name: Lily Sands
notes_head: Chief of Staff at Secure AI Project. Background as Foreign Affairs Officer at U.S. Department of State and former Naval Officer (Anti-Submarine Warfare Officer, Electronic Warfare Officer). MA from Georgetown Walsh School of Foreign Service. At State Department, analyzed export control licensing trends related to Missile Technology Control Regime and researched visa applicants for proliferation concerns. National security background.

name: Paul Scharre
notes_head: Executive Vice President and Director of Studies at CNAS. Leading expert on AI, autonomous weapons, and defense technology policy. Led the DoD working group that drafted Directive 3000.09, establishing U.S. policies on autonomy in weapon systems. Previously served in the Office of the Secretary of Defense on unmanned/autonomous systems and emerging weapons technologies. Named to TIME100/AI list (2023). Author of "Army of None: Autonomous Weapons and the Future of War" (2019 Colby Award, Bill Gates top-5 2018) and "Four Battlegrounds: Power in the Age of Artificial Intelligence." Testified befo

name: Aidan Gomez
title: Co-founder and CEO, Cohere
notes_head: Aidan N. Gomez is co-founder and CEO of Cohere. Previously a research intern and then researcher affiliated with Google Brain, where he was one of the eight co-authors of the landmark 2017 paper 'Attention Is All You Need' introducing the Transformer architecture. Also co-founded the non-profit research organisation FOR.ai. Canadian; completed his DPhil in computer science at the University of Oxford.

name: Nick Frosst
title: Co-founder, Cohere
notes_head: Nick Frosst is a co-founder of Cohere (2019). Previously a research scientist at Google Brain, where he worked with Geoffrey Hinton on capsule networks and other deep-learning architectures. Canadian; also the lead singer of the indie band Good Kid.

name: Ivan Zhang
title: Co-founder, Cohere
notes_head: Ivan Zhang is a co-founder of Cohere (2019), where he has led engineering and infrastructure. Previously worked with Aidan Gomez at FOR.ai, the research community that preceded Cohere. Canadian; dropped out of the University of Toronto to start the company.

name: Swami Sivasubramanian
title: Vice President, AWS Agentic AI
notes_head: Swami Sivasubramanian leads AWS's Agentic AI division, elevated to this role in the January 2026 AWS leadership reshuffle under CEO Matt Garman. A 20+ year Amazon veteran, he previously led AWS's Database, Analytics and Machine Learning organization (Bedrock, SageMaker, DynamoDB). Public-facing leader at AWS Summits and in enterprise AI policy discussions.

name: Peter DeSantis
title: Senior Vice President, Amazon AGI
notes_head: Peter DeSantis is Senior Vice President leading Amazon's reorganized AGI group, taking over from Rohit Prasad at the end of 2025. A 27-year Amazon veteran previously serving as SVP in the AWS cloud unit (overseeing infrastructure, silicon, and networking). Now holds the foundation-model mandate (Nova family) following Prasad's departure.

name: Ned Finkle
title: Vice President, Government Affairs, NVIDIA
notes_head: Ned Finkle has served as NVIDIA's Vice President of Government Affairs since April 2015. NVIDIA's primary public voice on AI chip export controls; authored the company's January 2025 rebuke of the Biden administration's proposed 'AI Diffusion' rule (characterizing it as a '200+ page regulatory morass'). Leads NVIDIA's Washington engagement on AI policy, chip controls, and sanctions.

name: Koray Kavukcuoglu
title: CTO, Google DeepMind; Chief AI Architect, Google
notes_head: Koray Kavukcuoglu was elevated in June 2025 to a dual role as CTO of Google DeepMind and Chief AI Architect of Google (SVP reporting to Sundar Pichai), spanning all of Google/Alphabet's AI strategy. Principal technical architect of Gemini 3. Turkish-origin researcher; joined DeepMind in 2012 and led major research advances prior to this dual leadership role.

name: James Manyika
title: SVP, Research, Labs, Technology & Society, Google-Alphabet
notes_head: James Manyika is Senior Vice President of Research, Labs, Technology & Society at Google-Alphabet, a role he has held since 2023 (previously Google's first head of Tech & Society, 2022). A principal public-policy voice at Google. Former Vice Chair of the U.S. National AI Advisory Committee (NAIAC) and co-chair of the UN Secretary-General's AI Advisory Body. Rhodes Scholar; Oxford DPhil in AI/robotics; long-time McKinsey Global Institute chair.

name: Joel Kaplan
title: Chief Global Affairs Officer, Meta
notes_head: Joel Kaplan is Meta's Chief Global Affairs Officer, having replaced Nick Clegg in early 2025. Meta's top Washington executive and a politically prominent Republican — former Deputy Chief of Staff for Policy under George W. Bush. Led Meta's public positioning around the Davos 2026 $600B U.S. AI infrastructure pledge. Has been a key architect of Meta's political realignment in the Trump era. Joined Meta (then Facebook) in 2011.

name: Jacob Andreou
title: EVP, Copilot, Microsoft
notes_head: Jacob Andreou is EVP of Copilot at Microsoft, named in the March 2026 Microsoft AI reorganization and reporting directly to Satya Nadella. Leads a unified consumer + commercial Copilot organization, freeing Mustafa Suleyman to focus on frontier-model and superintelligence work. Former Snap executive before joining Microsoft.

name: Joelle Pineau
title: Chief AI Officer, Cohere
notes_head: Joelle Pineau is Chief AI Officer of Cohere, joining from Meta in 2025 where she had been VP of AI Research leading FAIR (Fundamental AI Research). Computer Science Professor at McGill University and affiliate of Mila (Quebec AI institute). One of the most senior Canadian AI-research voices and a longtime advocate for reproducibility and open science in ML.

name: Sean White
title: CEO, Inflection AI
notes_head: Sean White became CEO of Inflection AI in 2024, succeeding Mustafa Suleyman following the Inflection-to-Microsoft transition. Has led Inflection's pivot from consumer AI (the 'Pi' assistant) to a commercial AI-studio / enterprise focus including emotional AI for business applications. Previously SVP of R&D at Mozilla; background in UX/AR.

name: Kevin Weil
title: VP, OpenAI for Science (formerly Chief Product Officer, 2024–2025)
notes_head: Kevin Weil joined OpenAI as Chief Product Officer in 2024 and, as of early 2026, is listed on LinkedIn and organizational trackers as VP of 'OpenAI for Science' — OpenAI's push to apply frontier models to scientific research. Previously President of Planet Labs; earlier Head of Product at Instagram and Twitter and co-creator of Facebook's Libra project. Lieutenant Colonel in the U.S. Army Reserve.

name: Marc Andreessen
title: Co-Founder & General Partner, Andreessen Horowitz (a16z)
notes_head: Marc Andreessen is co-founder of Netscape and co-founder of the venture capital firm Andreessen Horowitz (a16z). He is described as a founder, investor, and co-founder of Netscape, as well as co-founder of the venture capital firm Andreessen Horowitz (a16z). According to Wikipedia, he is an American businessman, venture capitalist, and former software engineer who is the co-author of Mosaic, the first web browser to display inline images. His net worth is estimated at $1.9 billion as of January 2025.

name: Peter Thiel
title: Co-founder and Chairman, Palantir Technologies; Managing Partner, Founders Fund
notes_head: Peter Thiel is the billionaire co-founder of PayPal and chairman of Palantir. He argues that artificial intelligence is targeting 'math people' - coders, engineers, quants, data scientists, and algorithmic specialists - rather than creative writers, artists, or storytellers first. In a 2019 talk, Thiel suggested AI itself might be inherently authoritarian, calling AI 'communist' and warning it could bring back a world of 'a few large companies, a few large governments, a few large computers that controlled everything'. He has been giving lectures on the Antichrist, first in San Francisco in Se

name: Holden Karnofsky
title: Member of Technical Staff - Anthropic
notes_head: Holden Karnofsky cofounded both the charity evaluator GiveWell and the philanthropic foundation Open Philanthropy (now called Coefficient Giving), which has now directed over $4 billion in grants. He led Open Phil for seven years, during which he also served on the board of OpenAI for four years. Reflecting his growing focus on AGI, he recently joined the frontier AI company Anthropic, which his wife Daniela Amodei helped cofound back in 2021. Karnofsky joined Anthropic in January 2025 as member of the technical staff, working on Anthropic's responsible scaling policies and other safety planni

name: Joshua Kushner
title: Founder and Managing Partner, Thrive Capital
notes_head: Joshua Kushner is the founder and managing partner of Thrive Capital, a venture capital firm. He co-founded Oscar Health, a health insurance startup that went public in 2021. Kushner's firm has been one of OpenAI's major investors, with Thrive Capital investing roughly $1 billion in OpenAI at a $285 billion valuation in December. He is married to supermodel Karlie Kloss and they have two sons together.

name: Joi Ito
title: Former Director, MIT Media Lab (2011-2019); President, Chiba Institute of Technology
notes_head: Joichi 'Joi' Ito is a Japanese entrepreneur and venture capitalist who serves as president of Chiba Institute of Technology. He was director of the MIT Media Lab from 2011-2019, resigning in September 2019 following revelations about financial ties to Jeffrey Epstein. Ito founded multiple tech companies including PSINet Japan, Infoseek Japan, and Digital Garage, and was an early investor in Twitter, Kickstarter, and Flickr. He earned a PhD in Media and Governance from Keio University in 2018.

name: Dustin Moskovitz
title: Co-founder and Chairman, Asana; Co-founder, Coefficient Giving (formerly Open Philanthropy)
notes_head: Dustin Moskovitz is a co-founder of Facebook and Asana, with an estimated net worth of $17.4 billion as of May 2025 according to Forbes. He co-founded the philanthropic organization Good Ventures with his wife Cari Tuna in 2011. Moskovitz and Tuna are major funders of AI safety research, having donated $1 million to Future of Life Institute and $30 million to OpenAI's non-profit in 2017. Open Philanthropy, which is financed primarily by Moskovitz and Tuna, funds AI fellows across congressional offices, federal agencies and think tanks.

name: Nat Friedman
title: Co-leader, Meta Superintelligence Labs; Investor and Co-founder, AI Grant/NFDG
notes_head: Led $15M seed round for AIUC in July 2025, developing AI agent insurance and safety standards. Co-founded $1.1B NFDG fund that achieved 4x returns in two years, investing in AI infrastructure and safety companies like Safe Superintelligence Inc. Joined Meta Superintelligence Labs in June 2025 to develop advanced AI systems. Previously launched GitHub Copilot as CEO, transforming developer productivity with AI-powered code assistance.

name: Masayoshi Son
title: Founder, Chairman, and CEO, SoftBank Group Corp.; Chairman, Arm Holdings; Chairman, Stargate LLC
notes_head: Son has committed over $40 billion to OpenAI and leads the $500 billion Stargate AI infrastructure project with Oracle and OpenAI. He predicts AGI will arrive 'much sooner' than 2-3 years and ASI will be '10,000 times smarter' than humans by 2035. SoftBank established comprehensive AI ethics policies in 2022 applying to 88 group companies and created an AI Ethics Committee in 2024.

name: Jaan Tallinn
title: Co-founder, Skype & Kazaa; Co-founder, Centre for the Study of Existential Risk; Co-founder, Future of Life Institute
notes_head: Co-founded Future of Life Institute which published the 6-month pause letter on frontier AI experiments. Has P(doom) estimate of 33-50% according to EA Forum survey. Early investor in DeepMind and Anthropic while simultaneously funding AI safety research, describing this as having 'street cred' in both communities to facilitate dialogue. Serves on UN Secretary General's AI Council.

name: Reid Hoffman
title: Co-founder, LinkedIn; Partner, Greylock; Co-founder, Inflection AI & Manas AI; Author, Impromptu and Superagency
notes_head: Wrote 'Impromptu' (2023) co-authored with GPT-4 and 'Superagency' (2025) on AI's positive potential. Major investor in OpenAI and Anthropic through Greylock. Publicly defended Anthropic as 'one of the good guys' in January 2025 clash with Trump administration AI czar David Sacks. Advocates 'iterative deployment' approach and warns that society opting out of AI engagement would be 'catastrophic.'

name: Vinod Khosla
title: Founder, Khosla Ventures; Vocal on AI Regulation
notes_head: Early OpenAI investor with $50M at $1B valuation, achieving 2,500x return with Juniper Networks. Predicts 80% of jobs will be AI-capable by 2030 but views this as ultimately positive for humanity. Called California Senator Scott Wiener 'clueless' about real AI dangers, focusing instead on national security concerns about China gaining AI leadership.

name: Martin Casado
title: General Partner, Andreessen Horowitz (Infrastructure Practice Leader); Former VMware SVP/GM, Nicira Co-founder
notes_head: Co-founded Nicira (sold to VMware for $1.26B), scaled VMware's networking business to $600M run-rate. At a16z since 2016, leads $1.25B infrastructure practice investing in AI startups like OpenAI, World Labs, Cursor, Ideogram. Advocates for open-source AI models for US competitiveness and warns against premature regulation stifling innovation. Active board member on 15+ portfolio companies.

name: Anjney Midha
title: General Partner, Andreessen Horowitz; Venture Partner (as of Oct 2024); Founder, AMP (Autonomous Machine Partners)
notes_head: Controls access to a16z's 20,000+ GPU cluster worth over $500M through the Oxygen program, making him a critical gatekeeper for AI startup compute access. Early angel investor in Anthropic when founded by ex-OpenAI researchers. Founded AMP in October 2024 to provide 'compute and capital to frontier AI teams' after leaving his GP role at a16z. Advocates for US-allied 'sovereign AI' development and open-source models to compete with China.

name: Sonya Huang
title: Partner, Sequoia Capital; Co-host, Training Data podcast; Co-author, State of AI Reports
notes_head: Leads Sequoia's AI application layer thesis with over $1.5B invested across Harvey, Glean, LangChain, Mercury, and Gong. Co-authored 'Generative AI's Act Two' (2023) and '2026: This is AGI' defining AI investment strategy. Hosts influential Training Data podcast and annual AI Ascent conference shaping industry discourse. Self-describes as 'very AGI-pilled' and believes we've reached the 'event horizon' for AGI.

name: Seth Rosenberg
title: Partner, Greylock Partners; Enterprise AI and Fintech Investor
notes_head: Greylock partner specializing in AI and fintech with '80% domain/20% AI' investment thesis. Led investments in companies like Pine (AI mortgage origination), Resolve AI ($35M seed), and serves on boards of multiple AI-enabled fintech companies. Advocates for AI as business accelerant rather than replacement, focusing on companies where AI enhances existing domain expertise rather than pure AI plays.

name: Elad Gil
title: Investor & Advisor; Author, High Growth Handbook; Former Twitter VP; Managing ~$3B fund
notes_head: Manages ~$3B fund and has invested in 40+ unicorns including Airbnb, Stripe, Coinbase, Figma. Early AI investor since 2021 with portfolio including OpenAI, Mistral, Perplexity, Harvey, Character.AI. Co-hosts 'No Priors' AI podcast. Distinguishes between 'technology-level' vs 'species-level' AI risks, viewing the transition to self-replicating AI with robotics as potentially existential. Author of influential blog posts on AI safety and market dynamics.

name: Eric Schmidt
title: Former CEO & Chairman, Google; Co-founder, Schmidt Futures; Former Chair, NSCAI (2019-2021); CEO, Relativity Space (2025-present)
notes_head: Testified before Congress in 2021 urging $40B AI investment and warned China poses existential threat to US tech leadership. Co-authored 'Genesis' with Henry Kissinger exploring AI's civilizational risks. Recently warned about 'superintelligence strategy' and proposed 'Mutual Assured AI Malfunction' (MAIM) deterrence framework. Led NSCAI from 2019-2021, significantly influencing US AI policy and defense strategy.

name: Josh Wolfe
title: Co-founder & Managing Partner, Lux Capital; Defense Tech & National Security AI Investor
notes_head: Warns portfolio companies about market risks and 'the bubble of AI' while remaining bullish on AI investment. Key investments include Anduril, Hugging Face, Palantir, and other defense/AI companies. Advocates for stapling visas to foreign AI graduates and tripling NSF funding. Member of bipartisan Hilland Valley Forum connecting technologists with policymakers.

name: Joe Lonsdale
title: Co-founder, Palantir & 8VC; Founding Chairman, University of Austin
notes_head: Founded Leading the Future super PAC with $125M to elect candidates favoring lighter AI oversight, directly opposing Anthropic's pro-regulation super PAC. Published comprehensive healthcare AI policy framework in December 2025 proposing three levels of AI integration. Co-founded Palantir in 2003 and manages $6B+ through 8VC, investing in defense tech companies like Anduril and healthcare AI applications.

name: Sam Lessin
title: General Partner, Slow Ventures; Former VP Product, Facebook
notes_head: Argues that AI seed investing has been 'a washout for most' and that AI is fundamentally reorganizing financial markets rather than creating traditional venture returns. Believes the VC 'factory model' is over and AI impact on capital markets may be more profound than the technology itself. Co-founded Meritfirst with Joe Lonsdale and made early investments in Venmo and Solana.

name: Matt Clifford
title: Co-founder & CEO, Entrepreneur First; Chair, ARIA; Prime Minister's Adviser on AI Opportunities; UK AI Safety Summit Representative
notes_head: Authored the UK's AI Opportunities Action Plan in January 2025, with all 50 recommendations accepted by the government. Has financial interests in nearly 500 tech companies through Entrepreneur First, creating potential conflicts of interest. Organized the 2023 Bletchley Park AI Safety Summit and helped establish the UK's AI Safety Institute. Awarded CBE for services to AI in 2024.

name: Ian Hogarth
title: Chair, UK AI Security Institute (formerly AI Safety Institute); General Partner, Plural; Co-author, State of AI Report
notes_head: Authored viral 2023 Financial Times essay 'We Must Slow Down the Race to God-like AI' warning of extinction risks. Led UK's AI Safety Institute (now AI Security Institute) from June 2023 to February 2025 with £100 million budget. Has invested in 50+ AI companies including Anthropic but divested from foundation model companies due to conflicts of interest. Co-authored annual State of AI report since 2018 and previously founded music startup Songkick.

name: Bill Gates
title: Co-chair, Gates Foundation; Co-founder, Microsoft; Founder, Breakthrough Energy
notes_head: Gates Foundation portfolio exceeds $41.8 billion with 25.55% allocated to Microsoft, reflecting concentrated bet on AI infrastructure. Led $11.8 billion OpenAI investment through Microsoft. Portfolio includes Berkshire Hathaway (26%), Waste Management (19%), and Canadian National Railway (15%). In Q3 2025, sold $8.67 billion in Microsoft shares for portfolio rebalancing. Co-founded Giving Pledge in 2010 with Warren Buffett. Awarded Presidential Medal of Freedom (2016), Padma Bhushan (2015), and Honorary Knight Commander of the British Empire (2005).

name: Laurene Powell Jobs
title: Founder and President, Emerson Collective
notes_head: Widow of Apple co-founder Steve Jobs with estimated net worth of $11.5 billion (Bloomberg Billionaires Index). Founded Emerson Collective in 2011, which has invested in at least nine AI startups since 2022 with funding rounds totaling over $1 billion. Early investor and founding backer of Jony Ive and Sam Altman's AI hardware device venture (io/OpenAI partnership). Earned BA in political science and BS in economics from University of Pennsylvania (1985), MBA from Stanford (1991). Received Service to America Medal Spirit of Service Award (2021) and honorary doctorate from Chiba Institute of Tec

name: Naval Ravikant
title: Co-founder and Chairman, AngelList
notes_head: Co-founded AngelList (2010) which has facilitated over $127M in annual deal flow. Angel investor in 200+ companies including early investments in Twitter and Uber. Previously co-founded Epinions (1999) and sued Benchmark Capital in high-profile case. Author of viral 'How to Get Rich (Without Getting Lucky)' tweetstorm (2018). Born in New Delhi (1974), graduated from Stuyvesant High School and Dartmouth College.

name: Ben Horowitz
notes_head: Ben Horowitz is a cofounder and general partner at the venture capital firm Andreessen Horowitz. He previously co-founded and served as president and chief executive officer of the enterprise software company Opsware, which Hewlett-Packard acquired in 2007. Andreessen Horowitz recently raised more than $15 billion across five new funds, with Horowitz stating that at this moment of profound technological opportunity, it is fundamentally important for humanity that America wins. He has authored books including The Hard Thing About Hard Things: Building a Business When There Are No Easy Answers.

name: Ruairí Donnelly
notes_head: President of the Board of Macroscopic Ventures, a Swiss nonprofit (est. 2019) that makes grants and investments (~$10M in grants in 2024) to projects reducing catastrophic and existential AI risk. Oversees programs and strategy. Also serves on the Board of the Center for AI Safety (CAIS). Previously worked at AI safety organizations and in the cryptocurrency industry. Macroscopic has funded the NYU Mind, Ethics, and Policy Program; Center on Long-Term Risk; Cooperative AI Foundation; and Institute for Law and AI.

name: Arden Berg
notes_head: Investor at Macroscopic Ventures, a Swiss nonprofit (est. 2019) focused on grants and investments to reduce catastrophic and existential AI risk. Background in economics; previously worked on investigating the trajectory of AI. Macroscopic gave ~$10M in grants in 2024 to organizations including the NYU Mind, Ethics, and Policy Program, Center on Long-Term Risk, Cooperative AI Foundation, and Institute for Law and AI. The organization focuses specifically on AI governance, compute governance, and information security to prevent existential misuse of AI.

name: Vishal Maini
notes_head: Founder and Managing Partner of Mythos Ventures, a San Francisco seed-stage VC firm focused on AI. Former Google DeepMind researcher. Closed a $14M inaugural fund in 2023 targeting pro-social AI startups. Also a board member at Manifund.

name: Anish Acharya
notes_head: Entrepreneur and General Partner at Andreessen Horowitz (a16z), leading consumer and fintech investing at Series A. Prior to a16z (joined 2019), founded Snowball (universal messaging inbox, acquired by Credit Karma 2015) and served as GM of Core Consumer and GM of Cards at Credit Karma. Co-authored a16z "Roadmap for Federal AI Legislation" (Dec 2025) advocating protecting people while empowering builders. Board member at Clutch, Deel, HappyRobot, Mosaic, Method, The Coterie, Titan.

name: Alex Immerman
notes_head: General Partner on the Growth investing team at Andreessen Horowitz (a16z), promoted Jan 2026. Focuses on B2C and B2B AI, application software, and fintech. Previously at General Atlantic investing in growth-stage tech. Led investments in Kalshi, EliseAI, Revolut; partnered with Waymo, Stripe, ElevenLabs, Hebbia, Roblox, Anduril, Flock Safety. Writes about "Service-as-a-Software" thesis — AI agents replacing knowledge workers, not just assisting them.

name: Santiago Rodriguez
notes_head: Partner on the Growth investing team at Andreessen Horowitz (a16z). Focuses on fintech, consumer, and crypto technology companies. Previously at Bain Capital investing in later-stage consumer, financial services, and industrial businesses. Contributed to a16z investments in hyperexponential, Kavak, Kalshi, Hebbia, and other growth-stage companies.

name: Patrick Collison
notes_head: Co-founder and CEO of Stripe, the global payments infrastructure company. Irish-born tech entrepreneur and one of the most prominent figures in Silicon Valley. Invested in Conjecture (AI alignment startup) alongside his brother John. Vocal advocate for reducing regulatory burden in Europe, co-signing open letters calling for 'major regulatory reform and simplification.' Co-founded Fast Grants to accelerate scientific research funding. On AI, pushes back on doom narratives and sees AI as a driver of Stripe's growth, with Stripe serving many top AI companies (OpenAI, Anthropic, Mistral, etc.).

name: John Collison
notes_head: Co-founder and President of Stripe alongside his brother Patrick. Invested in Conjecture (AI alignment startup). Sees AI as powering a 'good old-fashioned tech race' and expects a 'torrent' of AI agent commerce. Stripe under his leadership has integrated AI-driven fraud detection and stablecoin payments for AI agents. Shares Patrick's pro-innovation, anti-overregulation stance on European tech policy. Active infrastructure investor with a focus on long-term, technically deep companies.

name: Arthur Breitman
notes_head: Co-founder of Tezos, a proof-of-stake blockchain platform. Invested in Conjecture (AI alignment startup) alongside Patrick and John Collison, Nat Friedman, and Andrej Karpathy. Background in quantitative finance (Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley) and as a research engineer at Google/Waymo working on AI and self-driving cars before founding Tezos. Connection to Conjecture runs deeper through Gabriel Alfour, whose firm Marigold performs core Tezos development. Has spoken publicly about intersection of blockchain and AI.

name: Cari Tuna
title: Co-Founder and Chair, Good Ventures and Coefficient Giving (formerly Open Philanthropy)
notes_head: Cari Tuna is an American philanthropist and former Wall Street Journal reporter who co-founded Good Ventures and Open Philanthropy (now Coefficient Giving) with her husband Dustin Moskovitz. Open Philanthropy was an early backer of AI safety, giving more than $580 million toward building a field of researchers studying the potential catastrophic risks of AI. She was included in TIME's 100 Most Influential People in AI 2024.

name: Casey Newton
title: Founder of Platformer and Co-host of Hard Fork podcast
notes_head: Casey Newton is the founder and editor of Platformer, an independent publication devoted to exploring the intersection of technology and democracy. He co-hosts Hard Fork, a podcast about the future that is already here, alongside a colleague at The New York Times. Prior to founding Platformer in 2020, Casey spent 10 years covering Silicon Valley for The Verge, CNET, and the San Francisco Chronicle. In 2020, Casey was a finalist for the National Magazine Award for his series of investigative reports about content moderation that lead to a $52 million settlement for Facebook moderators who devel

name: Michel Justen
title: AI Grants Lead at Tarbell Center for AI Journalism
notes_head: Michel Justen works as AI Grants Lead at Tarbell Center for AI Journalism, which is funded by Coefficient Giving (formerly Open Philanthropy). He co-founded EA UW in Fall 2020 alongside his twin Lenni and graduated from UW-Madison in 2022 with degrees in Neurobiology and Psychology. He previously worked as an AI Governance Fellow at Pivotal Research and helped run events at the Centre for Effective Altruism. Open Philanthropy recommended a grant of $13,000 to Michel Justen to support him as he explores career options that could be highly impactful.

name: Dan Wang
title: Research Fellow at Hoover Institution, Technology Analyst and Writer
notes_head: There are multiple individuals named Dan Wang who are journalists or writers. One Dan Wang is a Canadian technology analyst and writer specializing in contemporary China, who has been an analyst at Gavekal Dragonomics and a research fellow at the Hoover Institution. Another Dan Wang is Lambert Family Professor of Social Enterprise at Columbia Business School and creator of CAiSEY, a voice-powered AI discussion partner. A third Dan Wang is a director on Eurasia Group's China team, previously serving as chief economist at Hang Seng Bank China.

name: Ezra Klein
title: NYT columnist and podcast host
notes_head: Ezra Klein is a New York Times columnist, best-selling author, and host of The Ezra Klein Show podcast. He is a co-founder of Vox and was formerly the website's editor-at-large. He has written two books: Why We're Polarized in January 2020, and Abundance, cowritten with Derek Thompson, in March 2025. Klein is married to Annie Lowrey, who writes about economics at The Atlantic, and they have two children.

name: Dan Kagan-Kans
title: Writer covering AI, science, and ideas for publications including Transformer, Wall Street Journal, American Scholar
notes_head: Dan Kagan-Kans is described as a writer on AI, science, and ideas for publications like Transformer, the Wall Street Journal, and American Scholar. He was previously a program officer at the Tikvah Fund and lives in New York. He has written articles including "The Left Is Missing Out on AI" for Transformer and "Baby Shoggoth Is Listening" for The American Scholar.

name: Karen Hao
title: Award-winning AI journalist, author of 'Empire of AI', former senior AI editor at MIT Technology Review
notes_head: Karen Hao is an American journalist who currently freelances for publications like The Atlantic and co-hosts the BBC podcast The Interface. She is the author of the 2025 bestselling book 'Empire of AI: Dreams and Nightmares in Sam Altman's OpenAI' which came out May 20, 2025 and was an instant NYT bestseller. She previously worked as a reporter for The Wall Street Journal covering American and Chinese tech companies, and as a senior AI editor at MIT Technology Review. Time magazine honored her on its TIME100 AI list in 2025.

name: Cade Metz
title: Technology Reporter, The New York Times; Author of 'Genius Makers: The Mavericks Who Brought AI to Google, Facebook, and the World'
notes_head: Broke major AI stories including Geoffrey Hinton leaving Google in 2023 to warn about AI dangers. His 2021 book 'Genius Makers' chronicled the history of deep learning pioneers like Hinton, Yann LeCun, and Demis Hassabis. Has conducted over 400 interviews with AI researchers and industry leaders over 8+ years covering the field. Recently faced criticism from rationalist community members for characterizing AI risk concerns as 'near-religious' in his August 2025 article on Lighthaven.

name: Kevin Roose
title: Technology Columnist, The New York Times; Co-host, Hard Fork Podcast; Author
notes_head: Author of 'Futureproof: 9 Rules for Humans in the Age of Automation' (2021) and upcoming 'The AGI Chronicles' (2026). Known for viral 2023 conversation with Bing's Sydney chatbot that sparked global AI safety debate. Currently documenting the 'AGI scene' in San Francisco, believing AGI arrival is imminent based on insider consensus. Previously wrote 'Young Money' (2014 NYT bestseller) and 'The Unlikely Disciple' (2009).

name: Will Knight
title: Senior Writer, Wired; AI & Robotics Coverage; Author of AI Lab Newsletter
notes_head: Veteran AI journalist with over a decade covering the field, writing for major publications since before the current AI boom. Has extensively covered military AI applications, AI governance, and the technical development of systems like ChatGPT. Writes the weekly AI Lab newsletter for Wired and has been featured on podcasts discussing AI policy and safety issues.

name: James Vincent
title: Senior Reporter, The Verge; AI & Science Coverage; Author of 'Beyond Measure'
notes_head: Author of 'Beyond Measure: The Hidden History of Measurement' (New Yorker and Economist book of the year, NY Times critics' pick). Has extensively covered humanoid robotics, AI chatbots, and automation's impact on labor. Featured in Harper's December 2025 cover story on humanoid robots and wrote influential LRB piece 'Horny Robot Baby Voice' on AI companion risks. Warns specifically about billionaire-led automation displacing workers rather than robot uprising scenarios.

name: Melissa Heikkilä
title: AI Correspondent, Financial Times (formerly Senior Reporter, MIT Technology Review)
notes_head: Named one of 100 Brilliant Women in AI Ethics (2024) and Forbes 30 Under 30 in Media (2020). Known for immersive journalism including creating deepfakes of herself for stories. Co-wrote 'Is AI Hitting a Wall?' analysis in Financial Times (August 2025). Frequently quoted expert on AI developments and policy implications.

name: Parmy Olson
title: Bloomberg Opinion Columnist covering technology, AI and regulation; Author of 'Supremacy: AI, ChatGPT, and the Race That Will Change the World'
notes_head: Won 2024 Financial Times and Schroders Business Book of the Year Award for 'Supremacy.' Previously authored 'We Are Anonymous' (2012) about hacker collective. Named Digital Journalist of the Year 2023 by PRCA and listed as Top 100 People in UK Tech 2019 by Business Insider. Covers AI development since 2016, emphasizing the need for corporate accountability and transparent governance structures.

name: Madhumita Murgia
title: AI Editor, Financial Times; Author of Code Dependent: Living in the Shadow of AI
notes_head: First AI Editor at Financial Times (appointed January 2023). Won UK Press Awards for Tech/Science Journalist of the Year in 2024 and 2025. Code Dependent was shortlisted for Women's Prize for Non-Fiction 2024. Has academic background in biology and science journalism from Oxford and NYU, with previous research on HIV vaccine development.

name: Matt O'Brien
title: Technology Writer, Associated Press; Covers AI policy, copyright issues, and tech regulation
notes_head: Leading AP technology reporter covering major AI developments including the $1.5 billion Anthropic copyright settlement, Pentagon-Anthropic disputes over military AI use, and AI's impact on software engineering jobs. His reporting frequently examines the intersection of AI advancement with legal, regulatory, and societal challenges. Covers stories from Providence, Rhode Island and has extensive bylines across major publications.

name: Nitasha Tiku
title: Technology Culture Reporter, The Washington Post; covers AI ethics, labor impacts, and Silicon Valley culture
notes_head: Tiku has extensively covered AI safety movements, broke major stories about AI training data practices including Anthropic's book scanning project, and reported on AI chatbot harms to teenagers including suicide cases. Her 2022 story about Google engineer Blake Lemoine's claims of AI sentience sparked global debate about AI consciousness and corporate AI development practices.

name: Kara Swisher
title: Host, On with Kara Swisher & Pivot Podcasts; Technology Journalist and Author
notes_head: In February 2024 interviews, called for government dialogue with tech companies about AI regulation, comparing it to regulation in other industries. Argued that AI creators should not receive blanket free speech protections and advocated for legal liability for harmful AI-generated content like deepfakes. Has been covering tech industry since 1990s and regularly interviews major AI company CEOs including Sam Altman.

name: Lex Fridman
title: Host, Lex Fridman Podcast; Research Scientist, MIT Laboratory for Information and Decision Systems (LIDS)
notes_head: Hosts one of the most influential AI podcasts with 4.88M YouTube subscribers and 855M views. Rose to prominence in 2019 after Elon Musk praised his non-peer-reviewed Tesla Autopilot study, which was later criticized and removed from MIT's website. Transitioned from MIT AgeLab to unpaid role in 2019, currently research scientist at MIT LIDS since 2022. His podcast features extensive conversations with AI researchers, policymakers, and tech leaders, making him a key narrator in AI discourse.

name: Dwarkesh Patel
title: Host, Dwarkesh Podcast; AI Policy Commentator and Writer
notes_head: Conducts in-depth interviews with AI leaders including Dario Amodei, Elon Musk, and other prominent figures. Named one of TIME's Most Influential People in AI. Argues current RL scaling contradicts short AGI timelines and that economic diffusion lag is 'cope for missing capabilities.' Believes continual learning is the key bottleneck to true AGI.

name: Craig Smith
title: Host, Eye on AI Podcast; Former Foreign Correspondent, The New York Times
notes_head: Former NYT foreign correspondent who covered 40+ countries including conflicts in Afghanistan, Iraq, and Israeli-Lebanese war. Started Eye on AI podcast after interviewing Geoff Hinton in 2017, which awakened his interest in AI. Served on National Security Commission on AI until October 2021. Eye on AI podcast ranked #2-3 among AI podcasts by Feedspot.

name: Nathaniel Whittemore
title: Host, The AI Daily Brief Podcast; Founder and CEO, Superintelligent
notes_head: Hosts daily AI news analysis podcast The AI Daily Brief (formerly The AI Breakdown) since April 2023. Founded Superintelligent, an AI planning platform for enterprises that helps with agent deployment and AI transformation. Previously founded The Breakdown Network and hosted crypto podcast acquired by Blockworks. Known for analytical approach bridging technical developments with business implications.

name: Zeynep Tufekci
title: Henry G. Bryan Professor of Sociology and Public Affairs at Princeton University; New York Times Opinion Columnist; Author, Twitter and Tear Gas
notes_head: Delivered keynote 'Are We Having the Wrong Nightmares About AI?' at NeurIPS 2025, arguing against AGI fixation in favor of addressing 'Artificial Good-Enough Intelligence' that breaks societal trust mechanisms. Co-authored 2025 paper on dimensional governance for AI systems. Known for early accurate predictions on technology impacts, including COVID-19 mask effectiveness and social media's democratic risks.

name: Kashmir Hill
title: Technology Reporter specializing in privacy, surveillance, and AI
notes_head: Technology reporter at The New York Times since July 2019, specializing in privacy and surveillance. Author of 'Your Face Belongs to Us: A Secretive Startup's Quest to End Privacy as We Know It' investigating Clearview AI. Broke major story in January 2020 exposing Clearview AI's existence and police client base. Has extensively covered 'AI psychosis' phenomenon and people developing romantic relationships with chatbots. Conducted experiment in 2024 letting AI make all her decisions for a week. Previously worked at Gizmodo, Fusion, and Forbes covering technology and privacy issues. Graduate of

name: Billy Perrigo
notes_head: Correspondent at TIME magazine based in London, covering the tech industry with a focus on AI and social media. Known for investigative reporting on AI labor exploitation: broke the story that OpenAI used outsourced Kenyan workers earning less than $2/hour to detoxify ChatGPT. Also reported that OpenAI lobbied to water down the EU AI Act. His investigation "Inside Facebook's African Sweatshop" was a finalist for the 2022 Orwell Prize. Reporting focuses on how tech companies restructure power, with attention to impacts on marginalized communities, workers, and postcolonial states.

name: Rubina Fillion
notes_head: Rubina Madan Fillion is the Associate Editorial Director of A.I. Initiatives at The New York Times, working alongside Zach Seward on a cross-disciplinary team leveraging generative AI and machine learning for journalism while ensuring responsible use. Previously Director of Strategy for Times Opinion, founding Director of Audience Engagement at The Intercept, and social media editor at The Wall Street Journal. She has taught graduate-level digital journalism at Columbia University and NYU. Served on the ONA Board of Directors (2019–2024). Active in exploring how AI can power investigative jour

name: Leah Harrison
notes_head: Partnerships Director at the Tarbell Center for AI Journalism, where she helps build relationships between the center and news organizations. Previously spent over two decades at Bloomberg in roles including newsroom trainer, reporter, and managing editor of QuickTake. Received Journalist of the Year 2025 award for AI reporting from One Young World. The Tarbell Center supports journalism covering AI's societal impacts, offering fellowships, grants ($350K in 2025), and newsroom placements (Guardian, Bloomberg, MIT Tech Review, TIME, The Verge).

name: Kylie Robison
title: Independent AI journalist (KylieBytes); formerly Senior Correspondent, Wired
notes_head: Kylie Robison covered the business of AI as Senior Correspondent at Wired from 2024 through late 2025, including reporting on OpenAI, Anthropic, and Dario Amodei's positions on state AI regulation. Departed Wired to write independently (KylieBytes) in late 2025. Well-sourced in Silicon Valley AI-business circles.

name: Hayden Field
title: Senior AI Reporter (Lead), The Verge
notes_head: Hayden Field leads The Verge's AI coverage as Senior AI Reporter, having joined from CNBC in June 2025. Anchors Verge reporting on OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Meta, and Apple's AI efforts. Appears on the Decoder podcast discussing AI monetization and lab IPO timelines.

name: Deepa Seetharaman
title: AI Reporter, Reuters
notes_head: Deepa Seetharaman covers AI at Reuters, having joined in April 2025 after a decade at the Wall Street Journal where she was a lead tech reporter (including on the 'Facebook Files' investigation). One of the most experienced AI accountability journalists in U.S. media.

name: Berber Jin
title: Reporter, AI / Startups / VC, Wall Street Journal
notes_head: Berber Jin is a Wall Street Journal reporter covering AI, startups and venture capital. Broke major OpenAI scoops in 2025–26 including the 'code red' leadership dynamics, the Sora shutdown controversy, Sam Altman investment conflicts, and the OpenAI/Anthropic IPO race.

name: Alex Heath
title: Founder, Sources; Co-host, 'Access' podcast (Vox Media)
notes_head: Alex Heath left The Verge in September 2025 to launch Sources, an independent publication chronicling how AI is reshaping Silicon Valley. Also co-hosts the Access podcast (Vox Media) with interviews of tech CEOs (Mark Zuckerberg, Dylan Field). Known for AI talent-war scoops and CEO-access interviews during his Verge tenure.

name: Nilay Patel
title: Editor-in-Chief, The Verge; Host, 'Decoder' podcast
notes_head: Nilay Patel is Editor-in-Chief of The Verge and host of the Decoder podcast, one of the most influential tech-policy interview shows. Decoder episodes in 2025–26 have interviewed AI-lab and enterprise leaders, and Patel's editorial direction shapes Verge coverage of OpenAI, Anthropic, and Pentagon AI procurement.

name: Reed Albergotti
title: Technology Editor, Semafor
notes_head: Reed Albergotti is Technology Editor at Semafor, authoring its twice-weekly AI-focused newsletter read by an estimated 30,000+ tech executives. Covers AI infrastructure (GPU shortages, data-center capacity), frontier labs, and Big Tech AI strategy. Editor of Semafor Tech's 2026 predictions package.

name: Shirin Ghaffary
title: AI Reporter, Bloomberg News; author, 'Q&AI' newsletter
notes_head: Shirin Ghaffary is Bloomberg's lead AI reporter on the West Coast, covering major frontier-lab funding rounds (including the reported OpenAI $110B raise in 2025) and writing the award-winning 'Q&AI' newsletter. Previously at Recode/Vox.

name: Anna Tong
title: AI Reporter, Forbes
notes_head: Anna Tong joined Forbes in October 2025 to cover AI and help curate the AI 50 list, after previously breaking OpenAI governance scoops at Reuters. One of the more prominent AI-business beat journalists moving to a major U.S. business outlet with a dedicated AI franchise.

name: Ben Thompson
title: Founder and Author, Stratechery
notes_head: Ben Thompson is founder and author of Stratechery, a subscription tech-strategy analysis newsletter widely read by executives and policymakers. Thompson's 2025–26 AI analysis (including 'Checking In on AI and the Big Five', 'OpenAI, Microsoft and The State of AI', and DeepSeek and 'AI slop era' coverage) has set framing for how industry leaders think about AI strategy and platform dynamics.

name: Sigal Samuel
title: Senior Reporter, Future Perfect, Vox
notes_head: Sigal Samuel is a Senior Reporter for Vox's Future Perfect vertical, covering AI consciousness, AI ethics, and frontier-AI moral philosophy questions substantively — a niche few mainstream outlets address. Co-hosts the Future Perfect podcast. One of the most thoughtful public voices on AI welfare/consciousness questions in U.S. media.

name: Garrison Lovely
title: Freelance AI journalist; publisher, 'Obsolete' Substack; author
notes_head: Garrison Lovely is a freelance AI journalist whose 2024–25 work has appeared in the New York Times, Bloomberg, TIME, Nature, and MIT Technology Review (including a 2025 Bloomberg piece on AI 'scheming'). Publishes the 'Obsolete' Substack and is author of the forthcoming (Spring 2026) book 'Obsolete'. Reporter-in-Residence at Omidyar Network.

name: Jennifer Pahlka
title: Author of 'Recoding America', Co-founder and Board Chair of Recoding America Fund, Former US Deputy Chief Technology Officer
notes_head: Jennifer Pahlka is the founder of Code for America and author of 'Recoding America: Why Government Is Failing in the Digital Age and How We Can Do Better'. She served as U.S. Deputy Chief Technology Officer under President Obama from 2013-2014, where she helped found the United States Digital Service. She is currently a senior fellow at the Niskanen Center and the Federation of American Scientists. In October 2025, she announced the launch of the Recoding America Fund, a pooled philanthropic effort that will raise and deploy at least $120 million over six years.

name: Timnit Gebru
title: Founder and Executive Director
notes_head: Timnit Gebru is the founder and executive director of The Distributed Artificial Intelligence Research (DAIR) Institute. She was co-lead of the Ethical AI research team at Google until her firing in 2020 (along with her co-lead) for writing a paper warning about the dangers of large language models and raising issues of discrimination in the workplace. She co-founded Black in AI, a nonprofit that works to increase the presence, inclusion, visibility and health of Black people in the field of AI. She is the recipient of the 2025 Miles Conrad Award, NISO's lifetime achievement award for those wo

name: Fran Drescher
title: President of SAG-AFTRA
notes_head: Fran Drescher is the SAG-AFTRA President who led the union during a 118-day strike and warned about AI threats to the entertainment industry. She became one of the prominent faces of the strike alongside WGA West President Meredith Stiehm and WGA East President Lisa Takeuchi Cullen. Drescher took office as president on October 15, 2021, and led the union during the five-month actors' strike that began on July 14, 2023. She has been vocal about protecting actors from AI technology and securing proper compensation and consent for use of their likeness.

name: Ed Newton-Rex
title: CEO, Fairly Trained
notes_head: Ed Newton-Rex is the CEO of Fairly Trained, a non-profit he founded in 2024 that certifies generative AI companies for fairer training data practices. He previously founded Jukedeck in 2010, one of the first AI music generation startups, which was acquired by ByteDance in 2019. He was named in TIME Magazine's list of the 100 most influential people in artificial intelligence in 2025. He organized the Statement on AI Training, an open letter rejecting unfair generative AI training practices signed by more than 50,000 people, and 'Is This What We Want?', a protest album co-written by 1,000 UK mu

name: Duncan Crabtree-Ireland
title: National Executive Director and Chief Negotiator, SAG-AFTRA
notes_head: Duncan Crabtree-Ireland is the National Executive Director and Chief Negotiator of SAG-AFTRA, overseeing the world's largest entertainment union with more than 160,000 members worldwide. He was the lead negotiator during the 2023 SAG-AFTRA strike and has been a key advocate for AI protections in the entertainment industry. He previously served as SAG-AFTRA's Chief Operating Officer and General Counsel, and was a key participant in the successful merger between SAG and AFTRA. He co-hosts the SAG-AFTRA Podcast and SAG-AFTRA Podcast en Español.

name: Milagros Miceli
title: Founder, Data Workers' Inquiry; Research Lead, Distributed AI Research Institute (DAIR); Head of Data, Algorithmic Systems, and Ethics research unit, Weizenbaum Institute; Lecturer, TU Berlin
notes_head: Milagros Miceli is a sociologist and computer scientist who founded the Data Workers' Inquiry, an academic project that puts power in the hands of AI data labelers to publish research by and about themselves. She is based at the Weizenbaum Institute in Germany and was named to TIME's 2025 TIME100 AI list. Miceli is 41 years old and is described as an Argentine researcher who has lived in Berlin for more than 16 years. She does not use ChatGPT, stating it would feel like "a betrayal" because she works closely with people who worked on ChatGPT training and "it has destroyed their lives".

name: Meredith Whittaker
title: President of Signal Foundation
notes_head: Meredith Whittaker is the president of the Signal Foundation and serves on its board of directors. She was formerly the Minderoo Research Professor at New York University (NYU), and chief advisor and former faculty director & co founder of the AI Now Institute. She was employed at Google for 13 years, where she founded Google's Open Research group and co-founded the M-Lab. In 2018, she was a core organizer of the Google Walkouts and resigned from the company in July 2019.

name: Randi Weingarten
title: President, American Federation of Teachers
notes_head: Randi Weingarten is president of the 1.8 million-member American Federation of Teachers (AFT), which represents teachers, paraprofessionals, higher education faculty, nurses, government employees, and early childhood educators. She was elected AFT president in 2008, after serving 11 years as president of the United Federation of Teachers in New York City. She taught history at Clara Barton High School in Brooklyn from 1991 to 1997 and holds degrees from Cornell University's School of Industrial and Labor Relations and the Cardozo School of Law. When elected AFT president in 2008, she became th

name: Elliston Berry
title: Deepfake victim turned AI safety advocate
notes_head: Elliston Berry was 14 years old when a classmate made and shared deepfake nude images of her, which she discovered in October 2023. She helped create an online training course to teach students, parents and school staff about non-consensual, explicit deepfake image abuse, in partnership with cybersecurity firm Adaptive Security and Pathos Consulting Group. Berry advocated for the Take It Down Act, which was signed into law by President Donald Trump and makes it a crime to share nonconsensual, explicit images. She appeared at a Capitol Hill press conference with Sen. Ted Cruz and spoke at the W

name: Ana Helena Ulbrich
title: Co-founder and Director, NoHarm; Pharmacist and AI Safety Advocate
notes_head: Named to TIME's 100 Most Influential People in AI 2025 list alongside Elon Musk and Sam Altman. Co-founded NoHarm in 2019 as a nonprofit AI tool that reviews medical prescriptions and flags potential dangers, now used by 200+ Brazilian hospitals analyzing 5 million prescriptions monthly. The platform is provided free to Brazil's public healthcare system (SUS) while private hospitals pay fees, and has received funding from Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation, Google, Amazon, and BNDES.

name: Sneha Revanur
title: Founder & President, Encode Justice; Gen Z AI Policy Advocate
notes_head: Founded Encode Justice at age 15 in 2020, initially opposing California Proposition 25's algorithmic cash bail system. Named to TIME's inaugural list of 100 most influential people in AI (2023) as the youngest person. Served as reviewer for Future of Life Institute's AI Safety Index evaluating major AI companies. Co-sponsored California SB 53 requiring transparency and safety protocols from frontier AI companies.

name: Mary Wareham
title: Deputy Director, Crisis, Conflict and Arms Division, Human Rights Watch; Former Coordinator, Campaign to Stop Killer Robots (2013-2021)
notes_head: Led the global Campaign to Stop Killer Robots from 2013-2021, coordinating 270+ civil society organizations across 65+ countries. Co-authored major Human Rights Watch reports including 'A Hazard to Human Rights: Autonomous Weapons Systems and Digital Decision-Making' (2025). Previously coordinated successful campaigns for the Mine Ban Treaty and Convention on Cluster Munitions. Currently advocating for UN Secretary-General Guterres' call for legally binding agreement on autonomous weapons by 2026.

name: Rebecca Finlay
title: CEO, Partnership on AI
notes_head: Leads Partnership on AI, a nonprofit with 141 partners across 19 countries focused on responsible AI development. Previously founded CIFAR's AI & Society program, one of the first international multi-stakeholder AI initiatives. Regularly speaks at major AI policy venues including UK AI Safety Summit, OECD, and FTC. Has been featured in Financial Times, The Guardian, and Nature Machine Intelligence on AI governance issues.

name: Mark Surman
title: President & Executive Director, Mozilla Foundation
notes_head: Founded Mozilla AI with $30M investment in 2023 and launched Mozilla Ventures as impact venture fund. Led creation of Mozilla's 'Public AI' vision advocating for parallel public ecosystem to counter big tech dominance. Instrumental in Mozilla's pivot from web browser advocacy to AI policy leadership, emphasizing open-source as critical for innovation and competition. Co-signed 2023 letter on open-source AI safety with over 1,500 others.

name: Alex Hanna
title: Director of Research, Distributed AI Research Institute (DAIR); Former Senior Research Scientist, Google AI Ethics; Co-author of 'The AI Con'
notes_head: Co-authored 'The AI Con: How to Fight Big Tech's Hype and Create the Future We Want' (2025) with Emily Bender, critiquing AI as primarily advanced autocomplete systems. Former Google AI ethicist who left in 2022 following controversies around AI ethics research. Focuses on data bias, racial inequality in AI systems, and advocates for community-owned technological alternatives like Te Hiku Media in New Zealand.

name: Liz Shuler
title: President, AFL-CIO
notes_head: Leads the AFL-CIO's "Workers First Initiative on AI" launched in fall 2024. Has partnered with Microsoft on AI and labor issues while maintaining advocacy for worker protections. Testified that "70% of workers worry about being replaced by AI or automation" and warns AI decisions made now will "shape the next 50 years." Represents nearly 15 million workers across 63 unions.

name: Cindy Cohn
title: Executive Director, Electronic Frontier Foundation
notes_head: Led landmark Bernstein v. Department of Justice case establishing encryption as protected First Amendment speech. Stepping down as EFF Executive Director in mid-2026 after 25+ years with the organization. Published 'Privacy's Defender: My Thirty-Year Fight Against Digital Surveillance' (MIT Press, March 2026). Co-hosts EFF's 'How to Fix the Internet' podcast and has built EFF into a 125-person organization focused on digital rights.

name: Corynne McSherry
title: Legal Director, Electronic Frontier Foundation
notes_head: EFF Legal Director since before 2024, specializing in AI policy, copyright, and free speech. Testified before Congress on Section 230 and DMCA issues. Co-signed Mozilla's joint statement on AI safety and openness. Consistently argues that context matters in AI regulation and warns against rushing to broad regulatory solutions that could harm beneficial uses.

name: Ben Wizner
title: Director, ACLU Speech, Privacy & Technology Project; Attorney for Edward Snowden
notes_head: Has been Edward Snowden's attorney and gained prominence in surveillance law. In 2017, wrote extensively about AI surveillance threats in partnership with AI Now initiative. Warns that automated surveillance systems threaten constitutional rights and could create a society where people feel watched 'all the time.' Focuses primarily on civil liberties implications rather than technical AI safety.

name: Janet Haven
title: Executive Director, Data & Society Research Institute
notes_head: Chaired the Rights, Trust, and Safety working group of the National AI Advisory Committee from 2022-2025. Previously spent over a decade at Open Society Foundations overseeing technology and human rights grantmaking. Co-authored op-eds criticizing industry self-regulation and advocating for stronger government oversight of AI systems. Focuses extensively on sociotechnical research approaches and worker participation in AI governance.

name: Alexandra Reeve Givens
title: President & CEO, Center for Democracy & Technology
notes_head: Testified before House Education and Workforce Committee in January 2026 on 'Building an AI-Ready America.' Criticized narrow focus on long-term risks at 2023 Bletchley Park AI Safety Summit, advocating for attention to current AI harms. Daughter of actor Christopher Reeve, serves on foundation board focused on spinal cord injury research using AI technologies.

name: Tristan Harris
title: Co-founder and President
notes_head: Former Google Design Ethicist who authored viral internal presentation 'A Call to Minimize Distraction & Respect Users' Attention' (2013). Featured prominently in Netflix's 'The Social Dilemma' documentary (2020). Named to Time 100 Next Leaders Shaping 2021 and Rolling Stone's 25 People Changing the Future. Delivered major TED Talk 'Why AI is Our Ultimate Test and Greatest Invitation' (April 2025). Co-hosts 'Your Undivided Attention' podcast. Studied Computer Science at Stanford under Terry Winograd with focus on Human-Computer Interaction.

name: Aza Raskin
title: Co-founder and President
influence_type: Organizer/advocate, Narrator, Researcher/analyst, Builder, Advisor/strategist, Connector/convener
notes_head: Co-founder of Center for Humane Technology (2018) and Earth Species Project (2017), focusing on AI-animal communication translation. Subject and architect of Emmy-winning Netflix documentary 'The Social Dilemma' (2020). Created infinite scroll technology while at Mozilla. Trained as mathematician and dark matter physicist at University of Chicago (BS) and Caltech (PhD program). Son of Macintosh co-creator Jef Raskin. Co-hosts 'Your Undivided Attention' podcast. Featured on Oprah's AI special (2024). Former head of UX at Mozilla Labs, founded multiple companies including Humanized (acquired by

name: Chris Smalls
title: President (Former)
notes_head: Founded first successful Amazon warehouse union in U.S. history (ALU, 2021-2024) after being fired for organizing COVID-19 safety walkout in March 2020. Named Time Magazine's 100 Most Influential People (2022). Served as ALU president until July 2024 when replaced by Connor Spence. Participated in Gaza Freedom Flotilla (July 2024), physically assaulted by Israeli forces during ship interception. Born July 4, 1988, former rapper who toured with Meek Mill before warehouse work.

name: Børge Brende
notes_head: President and CEO of the World Economic Forum since 2020. Former Norwegian Minister of Foreign Affairs (2013-2017) and Minister of Trade and Industry (2004-2005). Under his leadership, WEF launched the AI Governance Alliance and Global Regulatory Innovation Platform (GRIP, July 2025) to co-develop frameworks for agile, inclusive AI governance. Emphasizes that AI challenges 'cannot be addressed in silos' and require 'co-creation' over competition and 'collaboration' over fragmentation. Optimistic about AI's economic productivity potential but acknowledges overvaluation risks. Advocates multi-st

name: Francisco Betti
notes_head: Head of the Global Industries Team and Member of the Executive Committee at the World Economic Forum. Previously led WEF's Centre for Shaping the Future of Advanced Manufacturing and Supply Chains. Brings together 20+ industry communities to help companies and sectors anticipate trends and accelerate responsible industrial transformation. Advocates for AI deployment at scale in manufacturing through the Network of Centres for the Fourth Industrial Revolution. Joined WEF in May 2015 after management consulting at PricewaterhouseCoopers in Geneva working with international organizations.

name: Andrew Carney
notes_head: Joint DARPA/ARPA-H program manager. Leads the AI Cyber Challenge (AIxCC) at DARPA -- a two-year, $8.5M competition to develop AI systems that can find and fix vulnerabilities in critical infrastructure software. Also leads health cybersecurity programs at ARPA-H. Previously principal researcher at HSBC Cybersecurity Science and Analytics. Background in competitive hacking (CTF). MS in Computer Science from Johns Hopkins University.

name: Amir Banifatemi
notes_head: Chief Responsible AI Officer at Cognizant. Former GM of Innovation at XPRIZE, led IBM Watson AI XPRIZE. Co-founded AI Commons collective. Co-created AI for Good Global Summit with ITU/UN. Elected to GPAI Multistakeholder Expert Group, co-chairs Responsible AI working group. 25+ years in emerging tech, prior roles at European Space Agency, Airbus, European Commission. Managed two venture capital funds.

name: Meredith Stiehm
notes_head: President of WGA West (2021-2025). TV showrunner (The Rookie, Code Black, Cold Case, NYPD Blue). Named TIME 100 Most Influential People in AI 2024. Led WGA during historic 148-day strike in 2023 that secured precedent-setting AI protections for writers. Called on studios to sue AI companies for using copyrighted work to train models. Strong advocate that AI cannot replace human creative experience.

name: Lisa Takeuchi Cullen
notes_head: President of WGA East (elected Sept 2023). First person of color to hold the position. TV writer/producer (Empire, Five Days at Memorial). Participated in April 2024 White House roundtable on AI and Workers Rights with Acting Secretary of Labor Julie Su. Launched WGA East AI Task Force to track AI's effect on writing professions. Strong enforcement of 2023 MBA AI protections for film/TV/streaming writers.

name: Sean McGarvey
notes_head: President of North America's Building Trades Unions (NABTU), an alliance of 14 national/international unions representing 3+ million skilled craft professionals in US and Canada. Glazier by trade — began career in 1981 with Glaziers Local Union 252 in Philadelphia, worked up through IUPAT leadership. Key figure at AI-labor intersection: partnered with Sam Altman/OpenAI (March 2026) to ensure AI data center infrastructure construction supports union careers and registered apprenticeship programs. NABTU invests $2.5B annually in workforce training. Positions unions as essential builders of AI in

name: Anna McAdams
notes_head: Mother of Elliston Berry and co-advocate for deepfake child safety legislation. After her 14-year-old daughter was targeted by a classmate who used AI to create non-consensual deepfake nude images (Aledo, Texas, fall 2023), McAdams gave emotional testimony before the Texas Senate Committee on Criminal Justice and took the story public nationally. Met with lawmakers in Washington including Sen. Ted Cruz and First Lady Melania Trump to push for federal legislation. Their advocacy was instrumental in shaping the TAKE IT DOWN Act (signed May 2025), which criminalizes sharing non-consensual explici

name: Peter Favaloro
notes_head: Program Director, Technical AI Safety at Coefficient Giving (formerly Open Philanthropy). Leads a team of six overseeing ~$150M/year in technical AI safety grantmaking, including the $40M Technical AI Safety RFP. Previously oversaw global catastrophic risks cause prioritization research and advised leadership on resource allocation strategy. One of the largest funders of technical alignment research globally.

name: Eli Rose
notes_head: Program Director, Global Catastrophic Risks Capacity Building at Coefficient Giving (formerly Open Philanthropy). Joined in June 2020. Oversees grantmaking for GCR capacity building and supports work on AI Governance & Policy and Technical AI Safety. Previously Director of Engineering at Mentor Collective, a Boston ed-tech company. Funds programs, events, and fellowships that build the pipeline of talent working on AI safety and other GCR topics.

name: Stefan Torges
notes_head: Head of Strategic Initiatives at Forethought, an AI safety research nonprofit focused on explosive AI progress scenarios. Previously Director of Operations at the Center on Long-Term Risk, where he built their fellowship program and oversaw AI safety grants. Also served as policy/research affiliate at GovAI.

name: Otto Barten
notes_head: Founder and president of the Existential Risk Observatory (est. May 2021, Amsterdam). Background as a physicist, sustainable energy engineer, data scientist, and entrepreneur -- previously founded and sold an EV smart-charging startup. Pivoted to existential risk communication after concluding x-risk is more important than climate. The Observatory aims to reduce human extinction risk by informing public debate. Co-authored the Conditional AI Safety Treaty proposal published in TIME. Active in the Effective Altruism community.

name: Alexandra Narin
notes_head: Strategic Delivery lead at Geodesic Research and Co-founder of UK AI Forum. Background in neuroscience research at UCL. Previously served as Head of Grants for a biotech company, where she secured and managed over $5M in private and government funding. Now applies operations and fundraising experience to scaling Geodesic Research. Connects AI safety research community in the UK through the UK AI Forum.

name: Peter Eckersley
notes_head: DECEASED (September 2, 2022, age 43). Australian computer scientist, security researcher, and internet privacy activist. Co-founder of Let's Encrypt (2012), which has validated certificates for 290M+ domains. Worked at EFF from 2006-2018 as Technology Projects Director, Chief Computer Scientist, and Head of AI Policy. Created Privacy Badger, Certbot, HTTPS Everywhere, SSL Observatory, and Panopticlick. Director of Research at Partnership on AI (2018-2020). Co-founded the AI Objectives Institute (AOI) in 2021 with Brittney Gallagher, focused on aligning AI objectives with societal interests. In

name: Adam Schumacher
notes_head: Program Director at the AI Objectives Institute (AOI). Based in Saint James, NY. Background in international development: previously served as Deputy Director in USAID's Office of East Asian Affairs, managing daily operations for over $800M in development programs across East Asia and the Pacific. Also worked at the East-West Management Institute. Brings international development and program management expertise to AOI's mission of aligning AI with human objectives.

name: Zoey Tseng
notes_head: APAC Partnerships at the AI Objectives Institute and independent researcher specializing in public goods and self-sovereign identity. Active in Taiwan's g0v civic tech community. Collaborated with Taiwan's Ministry of Digital Affairs (MoDA) on web3 and AI-related projects, including deploying AOI's Talk to the City tool. Has facilitated deliberation workshops in Taiwan on the impact and development of LLMs. Researches new government funding mechanisms for open-source projects. Bridges AI governance discourse between Asia-Pacific and Western contexts.

name: Jesse Clifton
notes_head: AI safety researcher focused on cooperative AI, game theory, and conflict prevention in multi-agent systems. Co-founded Center on Long-Term Risk and served as its Executive Director; now Grantmaking Officer at Macroscopic Ventures and board trustee of the Cooperative AI Foundation. PhD student in statistics at NC State.

name: Cecilia Elena Tilli
notes_head: Associate Director (Research and Grants) at the Cooperative AI Foundation (CAIF). Manages grantmaking and research initiatives focused on multi-agent AI safety. Background in strategy consulting for research-heavy startups, previously led a foundation focused on preventing antibiotic resistance (PAR Foundation), and studied environmental engineering. CAIF is backed by a $15M philanthropic commitment from Macroscopic Ventures and focuses on improving cooperative intelligence of advanced AI systems.

name: Goda Mockutė
notes_head: Programme Manager at the Cooperative AI Foundation (CAIF). Supports research aimed at improving cooperative intelligence of advanced AI. Background from the University of Oxford, based in the Netherlands. CAIF organized a Cooperative AI retreat bringing together researchers from industry, academia, and non-profits working across technical AI research, AI ethics, and AI governance.

name: Gergő Gáspár
notes_head: Founded AI Safety Hungary in 2022. Served as part-time Director of the European Network for AI Safety (ENAIS) for two years, focusing on coordination and capacity-building across European AI safety communities. Now incoming Executive Director of EA UK (Effective Altruism UK). Transitioned to ENAIS board to oversee hiring of next director. Background in psychology. Also co-founded Amplify Reason, providing marketing support for local EA/AI safety groups.

name: Jonathan Claybrough
notes_head: Board member of the European Network for AI Safety (ENAIS) and EffiSciences. Software engineer who transitioned to AI safety research and education. Involved in ENAIS governance and European AI safety coordination. Speaker at FOSDEM 2024. Part of the founding cohort that established ENAIS to connect AI safety researchers and community organizers across Europe.

name: Fynn Heide
notes_head: Co-founder and Executive Director of the Safe AI Forum (SAIF), a 501(c)(3) non-profit advancing global action on extreme AI risks. SAIF started as a FAR.AI fiscally-sponsored project, became independent nonprofit in 2025. Main program is International Dialogues on AI Safety (IDAIS), bringing together senior computer scientists and AI governance experts for international collaboration. Previously research scholar at Centre for the Governance of AI (GovAI) studying AI progress and China AI policy. Earlier researched Chinese data/tech policy at Sinolytics, Trivium China, and Mercator Institute fo

name: Conor McGurk
notes_head: Co-founded and served as Managing Director of the Safe AI Forum (SAIF), an AI safety non-profit focused on the International Dialogues on AI Safety (IDAIS). Joined Open Philanthropy (now Coefficient Giving) in August 2025 as Chief of Staff, Biosecurity. Previously led a team of engineers focused on data transparency and academic partnerships at Meta, and worked as a technology strategy consultant at Deloitte. BSc in Philosophy and Computer Science from University of British Columbia.

name: Mario Gibney
notes_head: Executive Director of Trajectory Labs, an Open Philanthropy-funded AI safety coworking and community space in downtown Toronto. Manages shared office for ~30 AI safety practitioners with weekly meetups, presentations, and hackathons. Mission is to catalyze Toronto as an AI safety research hub. Operational since January 2025. Focuses on reducing catastrophic risk from advanced AI through community building and mentorship.

name: Juliana Eberschlag
notes_head: Operations Generalist at Trajectory Labs, an Open Philanthropy-funded AI safety coworking and community space in downtown Toronto. Co-manages the space alongside Executive Director Mario Gibney. Supports workspace (~30 AI safety practitioners), events programming (meetups, hackathons, presentations), and peer network cultivation. Active in the LessWrong community.

name: Robert Kralisch
notes_head: Research coordinator and organizer at AI Safety Camp since AISC10. Independent conceptual/theoretical alignment researcher with a background in cognitive science.
