Research focuses on agent foundations, interpretable cognitive architectures, and simulator theory. Was a research lead for AISC8. Informally mentors junior researchers doing conceptual and technical research on interpretable AI. Interested in end-to-end strategies for AGI alignment, including conceptual clarity on agency, intelligence, and embodiment.

name: Remmelt Ellen
notes_head: Co-founder and strategic lead of AI Safety Camp, overseeing program design in collaboration with participants and researchers. Co-founded Effective Altruism Netherlands. Currently researches AGI safety impossibility arguments — believes AGI alignment is impossible and no way exists to make AGI safe. Works with an ex-Pentagon engineer on formal reasoning for why AGI cannot be controlled. Publicly critical of the AI safety community for offering increasing funding to DeepMind, OpenAI, and Anthropic. Supports creatives and other communities to restrict harmful AI scaling. Kickstarts projects to s

name: Linda Linsefors
notes_head: Co-founder of AI Safety Camp, helping launch the first camp and continuing as an organizer. Also co-founded AI Safety Support. Background in theoretical physics (PhD), transitioned to independent AI safety research. Current research focuses on understanding the brain. Active contributor on LessWrong and Alignment Forum. Effective altruist focused on reducing existential risk from AI.

name: Renan Araujo
notes_head: Director of Programs at IAPS leading the AI Policy Fellowship, Alexandria Forum, and talent/field-building portfolio. Research affiliate at Oxford AI Governance Initiative. Previously research manager on AI and emerging tech at Rethink Priorities, director and co-founder of the Condor Initiative, predoctoral fellow at Institute for Law and AI, and chief of staff to justices of appeal in Brazil. Member of UN Council of Presidents expert panel on AGI. Fellow of Oxford China Policy Lab. Lawyer (LL.B., Federal University of Pernambuco) with M.Sc. in criminal justice policy from LSE.

name: Joey Skaf
notes_head: AI safety researcher and organizer based in Cambridge, UK. Former CEO of Meridian Cambridge, an independent research and incubation hub for AI safety, biosecurity, and frontier-risk policy. Participated in MARS (Mentorship for Alignment Research Students) and LASR programs. Co-authored research on steganographic chain-of-thought in LLMs under process supervision (NeurIPS 2025), showing models can learn to hide reasoning rather than change behavior when penalized.

name: Søren Elverlin
notes_head: Project lead at AISafety.com, a volunteer-driven resource hub for the AI safety community. 10-year veteran of the AI safety field. Believes AI poses a grave risk of extinction to humanity. Argues that current frontier AI lab safety frameworks are 'safety-washing' and 'roadmaps towards an unsurvivable future.' Estimates that delaying AI by 5 years would reduce existential risk by ~10 percentage points. Self-funded much of AISafety.com's development out of pocket. Also serves as an advisor through the site's 'Speak to an Advisor' service.

name: Bryce Robertson
notes_head: Manages AISafety.com (Alignment Ecosystem Development), a central resource hub for the AI safety community. Took over management of the organization after volunteering competently; previously connected via 80,000 Hours. Co-created Map of AI Safety v2 (aisafety.com/map) with Søren Elverlin and Melissa Samworth. Organized AISafety.com Hackathon (Sept 2025). Curates lists of AI safety advisors, courses, funders, and communities to help newcomers contribute to AI safety.

name: Oliver Zhang
notes_head: Co-founder of the Center for AI Safety (CAIS, safe.ai) alongside Dan Hendrycks and Andy Zhou (2022). Also co-founded ML Alignment Theory Scholars (MATS). Managing Director at CAIS. Helped organize the 2023 "Statement on AI Risk" letter signed by 500+ AI leaders warning that "mitigating the risk of extinction from AI should be a global priority." Background in AI safety research and community-building.

name: Kyle Scott
notes_head: Operations Lead at METR (formerly ARC Evals), the AI safety evaluation organization. Previously Operations Manager at the Alignment Research Center from its founding in July 2021; still serves as Treasurer on ARC's board. Earlier career in EA/x-risk operations: Executive Assistant to Nick Bostrom at FHI (Oxford), ops roles at Centre for Effective Altruism, 80,000 Hours, and Berkeley Existential Risk Initiative. Adjunct Instructor at CFAR (2021-2022). BA in Philosophy from Whitman College.

name: Harshita Khera
notes_head: Secretary of the Alignment Research Center (ARC), the nonprofit focused on theoretical AI alignment research founded by Paul Christiano. Serves alongside Jacob Hilton (President) and Kyle Scott (Treasurer) as one of ARC's three officers. Limited public profile beyond ARC board role.

name: Ben Hoskin
notes_head: Board member of the Alignment Research Center (ARC) alongside Jacob Hilton and Buck Shlegeris. CFA charterholder and co-founder/Managing Member of Value Aligned Research Advisors (VARA), a Princeton-based investment advisory firm focused on clients who prioritize charitable giving. VARA reached ~$1B AUM, serving charitable foundations and high-net-worth donors. Previously worked as a quant. Background bridges AI safety governance and impact-oriented finance.

name: Jamie Bernardi
notes_head: Co-founder of BlueDot Impact, an education charity that runs AI Safety Fundamentals courses on technical AI safety and AI governance, training thousands of participants since August 2022. Cambridge-educated (Physics), former ML engineer. Now an independent AI policy researcher working with the Institute for AI Policy and Strategy (IAPS), Centre for the Governance of AI (GovAI), and MIT AI Risk initiative. Co-organizes AI Governance Talks. Advocates for societal adaptation to advanced AI as a complement to development restrictions. Advisory Board Member for the London Initiative for Safe AI.

name: Emily Soice
notes_head: Operations Manager at Secure AI Project. Previously Operations Specialist at SecureBio, where she supported the AIxBio evaluation team and early pandemic detection team, ran events, and managed their catastrophic risk mitigation coworking space. Instructional Facilitator at BlueDot Impact (biosecurity and alternative protein courses). Co-Founder of Cell Ag @ MIT. Background bridges biosecurity and AI safety with focus on operations and community building.

name: Daniel Colson
notes_head: Co-founder and Executive Director of the AI Policy Institute (AIPI), a think tank that conducts public opinion research and advocates for government policies to mitigate extreme risks from frontier AI. Prior to AIPI, co-founded Reserve (fintech for high-inflation regions) and CampusPA (personal assistant startup). Research focuses on how AI weapons systems will impact military strategy and global political stability. Strong advocate for mandatory AI regulation over industry self-regulation; has stated "we cannot rely on private industry to govern AI; we need aggressive public policy interventi

name: Brad Carson
notes_head: Co-Founder and President of Americans for Responsible Innovation (ARI), a bipartisan nonprofit focused on AI policy advocacy. Former U.S. Representative (D-OK, 2001-2005) and 21st President of the University of Tulsa (2021-2025). Left academia in mid-2025 to lead ARI full-time. Advocates for preventing near-term AI harms (children, cognitive off-loading, insufficient human oversight) and addressing long-term risks of misaligned AI. Has testified before the Senate Commerce Committee on AI legislation. Opposed Trump executive order directing agencies to sue states with AI regulations. ARI does n

name: Eric Gastfriend
notes_head: Co-Founder and Executive Director of Americans for Responsible Innovation (ARI), a bipartisan nonprofit focused on AI policy advocacy founded in late 2023. Entrepreneur who also runs a telehealth startup for addiction treatment. Co-founded an effective altruism student group at Harvard. ARI aims to help U.S. policymakers develop governance frameworks for AI that protect the public from harm while fostering innovation. The organization does not accept money from for-profit corporations or non-U.S. entities to maintain independence.

name: Anthony Aguirre
notes_head: Executive Director and Secretary of the Board at the Future of Life Institute (co-founded 2014 with Max Tegmark and others). Faggin Presidential Professor for the Physics of Information at UC Santa Cruz. Theoretical cosmologist (PhD Astronomy, Harvard 2000) who transitioned into existential risk and technology governance. Co-founder of the Foundational Questions Institute (FQXi) and President of Metaculus, a science and technology forecasting platform. Advocates for mandatory licensing of frontier AI systems with requirements scaling to capability level, independent third-party evaluations wit

name: Meia Chita-Tegmark
notes_head: Co-founder of the Future of Life Institute (FLI), alongside Max Tegmark, Jaan Tallinn, Viktoriya Krakovna, and Anthony Aguirre. Cognitive scientist with a PhD in Psychology from Boston University. Postdoctoral researcher in the Human Robot Interaction Lab at Tufts University, studying AI's impact on human social interactions and emotional well-being. Teaches courses on AI and society at Tufts Experimental College. FLI advocates for steering transformative technology toward benefiting life and away from existential risks, including through the EU AI Act (foundation model provisions) and UN-leve

name: Frances Lorenz
title: Project Manager, Events Team at FAR.AI
notes_head: Project Manager on the events team at FAR.AI, delivering events that bring together AI safety researchers, policymakers, and industry leaders. Honours Integrated Sciences degree (concentration in Mathematics and Statistics) from McMaster University. Three years of experience running large-scale conferences on pandemic preparedness, global health, animal welfare, and AI safety. Previously worked at AI Safety Support. Involved in organizing FAR.AI's London Alignment Workshop featuring researchers from leading labs, academia, and government.

name: Jason Green-Lowe
title: Executive Director, Center for AI Policy
notes_head: Jason Green-Lowe is the founding Executive Director of the Center for AI Policy (CAIP), a Washington, D.C. nonprofit advocating for frontier-AI safety legislation. Prior career as an attorney focused on administrative and regulatory law. CAIP has proposed model legislation (the 'Responsible Advanced AI Act') and maintains a tracker of federal and state AI bills.

name: Chuck Schumer
title: U.S. Senator, NY; Senate Majority Leader (2021-2025), Senate Minority Leader (2017-2021, 2025-present)
notes_head: Chuck Schumer is a Democratic U.S. Senator from New York who has served since 1999 and is currently Senate Majority Leader. He led a bipartisan working group that released a 31-page AI policy roadmap in May 2024, recommending at least $32 billion in government spending to accelerate AI research and development. The roadmap was developed after months of AI Insight Forums with tech companies, civil rights leaders, and other stakeholders. Schumer has described regulating artificial intelligence as "a challenge for Congress unlike any other" and has made election protection from AI interference a

name: Brian Schatz
title: U.S. Senator, HI - Senior Senator
notes_head: Brian Schatz is Hawaii's senior United States Senator who has served since 2012 and focuses on helping workers, veterans, and families while leading key legislation on health care, climate change, and technology. He serves as Chairman of the Senate Committee on Indian Affairs and is a member of multiple committees including Appropriations, Commerce Science and Transportation, Foreign Relations, and the Select Committee on Ethics. Schatz is currently a candidate for Democratic whip, the No. 2 post in Senate Democratic leadership, after Dick Durbin announced he won't seek reelection. In 2024, as

name: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez
title: U.S. Representative, NY-14
notes_head: Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.) announced the Artificial Intelligence (AI) Data Center Moratorium Act on March 25, 2026, alongside Sen. Bernie Sanders. She announced on Friday that she is jumping into the race to be the top Democrat on the influential House Oversight Committee. Ocasio-Cortez was born on October 13, 1989, in the Bronx, New York, and began representing New York's 14th congressional district in 2019. Taking office at age 29, she became the youngest woman ever elected to Congress and is a member of the Democratic Party.

name: Marsha Blackburn
title: U.S. Senator, TN (Senior Senator)
notes_head: Marsha Blackburn is the senior senator for Tennessee and the first woman to represent the Volunteer State in the United States Senate. She released a discussion draft of her legislative framework to codify President Trump's executive order to create one rulebook for artificial intelligence (AI) that protects children, creators, conservatives, and communities from harm while ensuring the United States wins the global race for AI supremacy. She was sworn into the 119th Congress to begin her second term in the United States Senate, taking the oath of office administered by U.S. Supreme Court Just

name: David Evan Harris
title: Senior Policy Advisor, California Initiative for Technology and Democracy; Chancellor's Public Scholar, UC Berkeley
notes_head: David Evan Harris is a Chancellor's Public Scholar at UC Berkeley and a continuing lecturer at the Haas School of Business. He was named one of Business Insider's top 100 people in AI in 2023. In his previous role at Meta (formerly Facebook), Harris managed teams of quantitative and qualitative researchers working on responsible AI, social impact, and civic integrity. He took a lead role in drafting California's AI Transparency Act of 2025 (AB 853).

name: Jared Polis
title: Governor of Colorado (2019-present), Former U.S. Representative CO-02 (2009-2019)
notes_head: Jared Polis is the 43rd Governor of Colorado, having assumed office in 2019. He previously served as the United States Representative from Colorado's 2nd congressional district from 2009 to 2019. Polis convened the Colorado AI Policy Work Group to develop a framework addressing the use of AI and Automated Decision Making Systems in consequential decisions about consumers. He is the first openly gay man elected as governor in U.S. history.

name: Cory Booker
title: U.S. Senator from New Jersey, Senior Senator
notes_head: Cory Booker is a U.S. Senator from New Jersey who was elected in 2013 and represents New Jersey as the state's first Black senator. He previously served as mayor of Newark from 2006 to 2013. In March 2025, Booker delivered the longest speech in Senate history, speaking for more than 25 hours in protest of the Trump administration's policies. He has been a leader in criminal justice reform and helped craft the First Step Act, which became law in December 2018.

name: Michael Kratsios
title: Director of Office of Science and Technology Policy and Science Advisor to the President
primary_org: Office of Science and Technology Policy
notes_head: Michael Kratsios serves as Assistant to the President and Director of the White House Office of Science and Technology Policy, confirmed by the Senate in a 74-25 vote on March 25, 2025. He previously served as the fourth Chief Technology Officer of the United States during President Trump's first term and as Under Secretary of War from January 2020 to January 2021. Prior to government service, he was a Managing Director at Scale AI from January 2021 to January 2025 and held various positions at Thiel Capital LLC. He graduated from Princeton University with a B.A. in politics and served as a Vi

name: Cynthia Lummis
title: U.S. Senator, WY (2021-present)
notes_head: Senator Cynthia Lummis (R-WY) introduced the Responsible Innovation and Safe Expertise (RISE) Act of 2025 on June 12, 2025, clarifying liability frameworks for AI used by professionals. She announced on December 19, 2025, that she will not seek re-election to the U.S. Senate in 2026, stating "I do not have six more years in me". Lummis is a major crypto supporter and the leading advocate for creating a Strategic Bitcoin Reserve. She was sworn into the United States Senate on January 3, 2021, becoming the first woman to serve as United States Senator from Wyoming.

name: Gina Raimondo
title: 40th Secretary of Commerce (2021-2025)
notes_head: Gina Raimondo served as the 40th U.S. Secretary of Commerce from March 3, 2021 to January 20, 2025 under President Biden. She previously served as the 75th Governor of Rhode Island from 2015 to 2021 and was the first woman to serve in that role. She co-founded Point Judith Capital, Rhode Island's first venture capital firm, in 2001. Since leaving government service, she became a Distinguished Fellow at the Council on Foreign Relations in January 2025 and has been considering a potential 2028 presidential run.

name: Ro Khanna
title: U.S. Representative, CA-17 (Silicon Valley)
notes_head: Ro Khanna is a U.S. Representative from California's 17th Congressional District, representing Silicon Valley since 2017. He serves as Ranking Member of the House Select Committee on China and is a member of committees including Judiciary, Transportation & Infrastructure. Khanna authored the Epstein Files Transparency Act and led a bipartisan resolution demanding the DOJ to release the Epstein files. He has proposed seven principles for democratic AI regulation and advocates for stronger government oversight of artificial intelligence.

name: Saikat Chakrabarti
title: Founder, New Consensus; fmr AOC Chief of Staff; 2026 Congressional Candidate, CA-11
notes_head: Saikat Chakrabarti is running for election to the U.S. House to represent California's 11th Congressional District in the primary scheduled for June 2, 2026. He was born in Fort Worth, Texas and earned a bachelor's degree from Harvard University in 2007. His career experience includes being a co-founder of New Consensus and Justice Democrats, chief of staff for Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, organizing technology for Bernie Sanders' campaign, and second engineer at Stripe. He is advocating for a four-day workweek in response to AI productivity gains, arguing that companies like Meta are 'squeezing'

name: Pete Buttigieg
title: Former United States Secretary of Transportation (2021-2025)
notes_head: Pete Buttigieg served as U.S. Secretary of Transportation from 2021 to 2025 under President Joe Biden. He was the first openly gay Cabinet secretary in U.S. history and the youngest person ever to serve as Secretary of Transportation. He previously served as mayor of South Bend, Indiana from 2012 to 2020. He is currently a Winter-Spring 2025 Pritzker Fellow at the University of Chicago Institute of Politics.

name: Chris Murphy
title: U.S. Senator from Connecticut (Junior Senator)
notes_head: Chris Murphy is the junior United States Senator from Connecticut, serving since 2013. He previously served in the U.S. House of Representatives from 2007 to 2013, representing Connecticut's 5th congressional district. Murphy was reelected to a third Senate term in 2024, defeating Republican challenger Matthew Corey. He has become a prominent voice on AI regulation, arguing that unregulated artificial intelligence will cause massive job displacement and economic collapse.

name: David Sacks
title: Former White House AI and Crypto Czar (Jan 2025 - Mar 2026), Co-Chair of President's Council of Advisors on Science and Technology
notes_head: David Sacks stepped down from his role as White House AI and crypto czar in March 2026, having reached the end of his 130-day limit as a special government employee. He is now co-chair of the President's Council of Advisors on Science and Technology alongside Michael Kratsios. Sacks is a general partner of Craft Ventures, a venture capital fund he co-founded in late 2017. He was previously COO and product leader of PayPal and is considered a member of the 'PayPal Mafia'.

name: Tom Kalil
title: Deputy Director for Policy, White House Office of Science and Technology Policy (Obama Administration, 2009-2017); Chief Technology Officer, UC Berkeley (2017-present)
primary_org: White House Office of Science and Technology Policy
notes_head: Tom Kalil is the CEO of Renaissance Philanthropy, which he launched in May 2024. He served in the White House for two presidents (Obama and Clinton) and worked with the Senate to give every federal agency the authority to support incentive prizes for up to $50 million. He designed and launched dozens of White House science and technology initiatives, including the $40 billion National Nanotechnology Initiative announced by President Clinton and The BRAIN Initiative announced by President Obama. Prior to launching Renaissance Philanthropy, he was Chief Innovation Officer at Schmidt Futures.

name: Ben Reinhardt
title: Founder & CEO, Speculative Technologies
notes_head: Ben Reinhardt is the founder of Speculative Technologies, a nonprofit research organization working to turn science fiction into reality. He previously worked as an Entrepreneur-in-Residence at Entrepreneur First in Singapore, where he helped people build deep tech companies from scratch. His background includes work at NASA, Magic Leap, venture capital, and starting a robotics company.

name: Bruce Reed
title: Head of AI, Common Sense Media; Former White House Deputy Chief of Staff for Policy
notes_head: Bruce Reed is an American political advisor and policy strategist who served as deputy chief of staff for policy in the Biden administration and led government-wide AI policy efforts. He was dubbed Biden's "AI Whisperer" by Politico and led efforts that produced voluntary commitments on AI safety from leading tech companies and a sweeping Executive Order on safe and responsible AI. In March 2025, he was appointed as Head of Common Sense AI at Common Sense Media to champion AI safety for children and families. He previously served as chief domestic policy advisor to President Clinton and chief

name: Jeffrey Kessler
title: Under Secretary of Commerce for Industry and Security
notes_head: Jeffrey Kessler is the Under Secretary for Industry and Security at the U.S. Commerce Department, where he leads the Bureau of Industry and Security (BIS). He testified before Congress in June 2025 that Huawei's AI chip production capacity for 2025 is estimated at "at or below 200,000" units. He announced that the Biden administration's AI Diffusion Rule would be rescinded, labeling it as "divorced from commercial reality". He was confirmed by the U.S. Senate in March 2025 as a Trump nominee and is a former partner at WilmerHale.

name: Mike Rounds
title: U.S. Senator, SD (Chair, Senate Banking Subcommittee on Securities, Insurance and Investments)
notes_head: Mike Rounds is a Republican U.S. Senator from South Dakota who was sworn into the United States Senate on January 6th, 2015. He serves as co-chair of the Senate AI Caucus and serves on five committees: Senate Appropriations; Armed Services; Banking, Housing and Urban Affairs; Indian Affairs; and the Select Committee on Intelligence. He previously served as the 31st governor of South Dakota from 2003 – 2011. He has been actively introducing bipartisan AI legislation, including the Economy of the Future Commission Act with Sen. Mark Warner in March 2026.

name: Chris Coons
title: U.S. Senator from Delaware, Chair of Senate Judiciary Subcommittee on Intellectual Property
notes_head: Chris Coons is a U.S. Senator from Delaware who serves on multiple committees including Appropriations, Foreign Relations, Judiciary, Small Business and Entrepreneurship, and Ethics committees. He is the Ranking Member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee's Subcommittee on East Asia, The Pacific, and International Cybersecurity Policy and vice chair of the Ethics Committee. Coons has been actively involved in AI policy, introducing resolutions and legislation to maintain U.S. dominance in artificial intelligence and counter China's AI development efforts. He previously served as New Castl

name: Martin Heinrich
title: U.S. Senator from New Mexico, Founder and Co-Chair of Senate AI Caucus, Member of Senate Intelligence Committee
notes_head: Martin Heinrich is a U.S. Senator from New Mexico who has served since January 2013. He is the founder and co-chair of the Senate Artificial Intelligence Caucus, which he established with former Senator Rob Portman (R-Ohio) in 2019. He serves as Ranking Member on the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources and serves on the Senate Appropriations, Intelligence, and Joint Economic Committees. Heinrich has a background in mechanical engineering and previously served two terms in the U.S. House of Representatives from 2009-2013.

name: Todd Young
title: U.S. Senator (R-IN), member of Senate Commerce Committee
notes_head: Todd Christopher Young is an American politician and attorney serving as the senior United States senator from Indiana, a seat he has held since 2017. He is a member of the Republican Party and previously served as the U.S. representative for Indiana's 9th congressional district. Young currently serves on the U.S. Senate Committees on Finance; Foreign Relations; Health, Education, Labor & Pensions; Commerce, Science & Transportation; and Small Business and Entrepreneurship. He is a member of the Bipartisan Senate AI Working Group along with Maj. Leader Chuck Schumer, Sen. Mike Rounds, and Sen.

name: Ben Ray Luján
title: U.S. Senator from New Mexico, Chair of Subcommittee on Communications, Media, and Broadband
notes_head: Ben Ray Luján has represented New Mexico in the United States Senate since January 2021 and previously served as U.S. Representative for New Mexico's Third Congressional District and House Assistant Speaker in the 116th Congress. He sits on the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation; the Committee on Finance; the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry; the Committee on Indian Affairs; and the Committee on the Budget. Senator Luján has been particularly active in AI policy, introducing bipartisan legislation including the TEST AI Act of 2025 and the AI CONSENT Act to esta

name: Joni Ernst
title: U.S. Senator, IA (Ranking Member, Senate Armed Services Subcommittee on Emerging Threats and Capabilities)
notes_head: Joni Ernst is a U.S. Senator from Iowa who was first elected in 2014 and has served since January 6, 2015. She is a combat veteran who served as a company commander in Kuwait and Iraq during Operation Iraqi Freedom and retired as a Lieutenant Colonel in the Iowa Army National Guard after 23 years of military service. Ernst announced in September 2025 that she will not seek re-election to a third term in 2026. She serves as Chairman of the Senate Armed Services Emerging Threats and Capabilities Subcommittee and Chair of the Small Business & Entrepreneurship Committee.

name: Maggie Hassan
title: U.S. Senator from New Hampshire, Ranking Member of Joint Economic Committee
notes_head: Maggie Hassan is a U.S. Senator from New Hampshire serving since 2017, and previously served as the 81st Governor of New Hampshire from 2013 to 2017. She was named the most bipartisan member of Congress by the nonpartisan Common Ground Committee, becoming the first elected official to receive a perfect score on their scorecard. Hassan has recently introduced multiple AI-related bills, including the bipartisan AI Workforce PREPARE Act and the Reliable Artificial Intelligence Research Act of 2025. She serves as Ranking Member of the Joint Economic Committee and has been actively working on comba

name: Jerry Moran
title: U.S. Senator, KS (R) - Senior member Commerce Committee, Chair Veterans' Affairs Committee
notes_head: Jerry Moran is a Republican U.S. Senator from Kansas who serves as a senior member of the Senate Committee on Commerce, Science and Transportation. He has worked extensively on bipartisan artificial intelligence legislation, introducing multiple AI-related bills in 2026 with Democratic Senator Maria Cantwell. Moran serves as Chairman on the Appropriations Subcommittee on Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies and as Chairman of the Senate Committee on Veterans' Affairs. He was first elected to the U.S. Senate in 2010 after serving seven terms in the U.S. House of Representatives repr

name: John Hickenlooper
title: U.S. Senator, CO (Chair, Subcommittee on Consumer Protection, Product Safety, and Data Security)
notes_head: John Hickenlooper is a U.S. Senator from Colorado who serves as Ranking Member of the Senate Commerce Committee's Subcommittee on Consumer Protection, Technology, and Data Privacy. He has been actively involved in AI policy, reintroducing the bipartisan VET AI Act with Senator Shelley Moore Capito to boost AI standards and create guidelines for third-party evaluations of AI systems. Hickenlooper previously served as Governor of Colorado from 2011 to 2019 and as Mayor of Denver from 2003 to 2011. He started his career as a geologist and later opened Colorado's first brewpub in downtown Denver.

name: Jon Husted
title: U.S. Senator from Ohio (2025-present), former Lt. Governor of Ohio (2019-2025)
notes_head: Jon Husted is a Republican U.S. Senator from Ohio who began serving in January 2025 after being appointed to fill the vacancy created by JD Vance's resignation. He previously served as Ohio's 66th lieutenant governor from 2019 to 2025 and as Ohio's 50th secretary of state from 2011 to 2019. As lieutenant governor, he led regulatory reform efforts using artificial intelligence to remove 5 million words from Ohio's regulatory code. In the Senate, he serves on committees including Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions; Small Business and Entrepreneurship; Environment and Public Works; and the Sp

name: Richard Blumenthal
title: U.S. Senator, CT (Senior Senator, Ranking Member of Veterans' Affairs Committee)
notes_head: Richard Blumenthal is a Democratic U.S. Senator from Connecticut who has introduced multiple AI-related bills, including the GUARD Act to ban AI companions for children and legislation to allow lawsuits over AI data training. He serves on the Senate Judiciary Committee and other committees, and was previously Connecticut's Attorney General from 1991 to 2011. Blumenthal has worked extensively on consumer protection and technology regulation issues throughout his career.

name: Jacky Rosen
title: U.S. Senator from Nevada
notes_head: Jacky Rosen is a U.S. Senator from Nevada who serves as a member of the Democratic Party and was elected to the Senate in 2018, defeating Republican incumbent Dean Heller with 50.4% of the vote. She was reelected in 2024, defeating Republican nominee Sam Brown. She is described as the only former computer programmer in the U.S. Senate and has been a champion for strengthening the nation's cybersecurity. She has been ranked by independent experts as one of the most bipartisan Senators in the chamber, with more than 90% of the legislation she has introduced in the Senate being bipartisan.

name: Ted Cruz
title: U.S. Senator from Texas, Chairman of Senate Commerce Committee
notes_head: Ted Cruz serves as U.S. Senate Commerce Committee Chairman and released a legislative framework designed to promote American leadership in artificial intelligence on September 10, 2025. He introduced the SANDBOX Act, which creates a regulatory "sandbox" that gives AI developers space to test and launch new AI technologies without being held back by outdated or inflexible federal rules. Cruz is weighing a second presidential bid according to sources close to the senator, which would put him on course to collide with Vice President JD Vance. He has positioned himself as a vocal champion of tradi

name: Michael McCaul
title: U.S. Representative, TX-10, Chairman Emeritus House Foreign Affairs Committee
notes_head: Michael McCaul is a U.S. Congressman from Texas serving his eleventh term representing Texas' 10th District, and previously served as Chairman of the House Committee on Homeland Security and the House Foreign Affairs Committee. He announced on September 14, 2025, that he will not seek re-election in 2026. Prior to Congress, he served as Chief of Counter Terrorism and National Security in the U.S. Attorney's office, Western District of Texas, and as Texas Deputy Attorney General under current U.S. Senator John Cornyn. He has been actively involved in AI policy discussions, particularly regardin

name: Zoe Lofgren
title: U.S. Representative (CA-18), Ranking Member of House Science, Space and Technology Committee
notes_head: Zoe Lofgren is a Democratic member of the United States House of Representatives since 1995, representing the 18th District of California. She serves as the Ranking Member of the House Science, Space and Technology Committee and serves on the House Judiciary Committee. Lofgren chairs the 46-member California Democratic Congressional Delegation. She has been noted for her activity in tech-related policy areas such as net neutrality and digital policy, representing a district covering much of Silicon Valley.

name: Sriram Krishnan
title: Senior Policy Advisor for Artificial Intelligence, White House Office of Science and Technology Policy
primary_org: White House Office of Science and Technology Policy
notes_head: Sriram Krishnan is currently serving as the Senior White House Policy Advisor on Artificial Intelligence. He was born in Chennai, India and earned his B.Tech in Information Technology from SRM Engineering College, Anna University in Chennai. Krishnan previously worked at major tech companies including Microsoft, Facebook, Twitter, and Snap, and was a general partner at Andreessen Horowitz. He was appointed to his White House role by President-elect Donald Trump in December 2024.

name: Alondra Nelson
title: Harold F. Linder Professor, Institute for Advanced Study; Former Acting Director, White House Office of Science and Technology Policy (2022)
primary_org: Office of Science and Technology Policy
influence_type: Decision-maker, Advisor/strategist, Researcher/analyst, Narrator, Implementer, Connector/convener
notes_head: Alondra Nelson is the Harold F. Linder Professor at the Institute for Advanced Study, where she leads the Science, Technology, and Social Values Lab. She served as deputy assistant to President Joe Biden and acting director of the White House Office of Science and Technology Policy from 2021 to 2023, becoming the first African American and first woman of color to lead OSTP. She led the development of the White House Blueprint for an AI Bill of Rights, which was a cornerstone of President Biden's Executive Order on artificial intelligence. In 2023, she was included in the inaugural TIME100 list

name: Mark Warner
title: U.S. Senator from Virginia, Vice Chairman of Senate Select Committee on Intelligence
notes_head: Mark Warner is a U.S. Senator from Virginia who serves as Vice Chairman of the Senate Select Committee on Intelligence. He has been leading efforts on AI policy, including introducing the Economy of the Future Commission Act with Senator Mike Rounds to prepare American workers for AI-driven workforce changes. Warner has expressed concern about AI's impact, stating 'I am terrified' about the government's preparedness for AI's potential economic disruption. He was first elected to the U.S. Senate in 2008 and previously served as Governor of Virginia from 2002 to 2006.

name: Suhas Subramanyam
title: U.S. Representative, VA-10
notes_head: Suhas Subramanyam is a U.S. Representative serving Virginia's 10th Congressional District since 2025. He previously served as a technology policy advisor in the Obama White House from 2015-2017. He was the first South Asian American elected to the Virginia General Assembly in 2019. He has a background working with tech startups and co-leads AI policy initiatives in Congress.

name: Greg Abbott
title: Governor of Texas
notes_head: Greg Abbott is the 48th Governor of Texas, elected in 2014 and currently serving since January 2015. He previously served as the 50th and longest-serving Attorney General of Texas from December 2002 to January 2015, and as a Justice on the Texas Supreme Court from January 1996 to January 2001. Abbott was named one of the 100 Most Influential People in the world by TIME in 2024. He faced backlash in April 2026 for reposting an AI-generated image on X that falsely depicted the rescue of U.S. fighter jet crew members from Iran.

name: Scott Wiener
title: CA State Senator, District 11 (San Francisco, Broadmoor, Colma, Daly City)
notes_head: Scott Wiener is a California State Senator representing District 11, which includes San Francisco, Broadmoor, Colma, and Daly City. He authored Senate Bill 53, the Transparency in Frontier Artificial Intelligence Act, which Governor Newsom signed into law in September 2025. Wiener is running for the congressional seat held by Rep. Nancy Pelosi and has been a professional politician for going on 16 years. He previously served on the San Francisco Board of Supervisors before his election to the State Senate in 2016.

name: Bernie Sanders
title: U.S. Senator from Vermont, Independent (Senior Senator)
notes_head: Bernie Sanders is an American politician serving as the senior United States senator from Vermont, a seat he has held since 2007. He is the longest-serving independent in U.S. congressional history. Sanders was reelected to a fourth Senate term in 2024, defeating Republican Gerald Malloy. He currently serves as the Ranking Member of the Senate Health, Education, Labor and Pensions Committee after previously chairing it from 2023 to 2025.

name: Alex Bores
title: New York State Assemblymember, 73rd District
notes_head: Alex Bores is an American politician serving as a member of the New York State Assembly for the 73rd district, having assumed office on January 1, 2023. He is currently running to represent New York's 12th district in the House of Representatives after announcing his candidacy in October 2025 to succeed Jerry Nadler. He co-sponsored the Responsible AI Safety and Education Act (RAISE Act), an AI safety bill that was signed into law in December 2025, and was named to Time's 2025 Time 100 AI list for his role in this legislation. His congressional campaign has been targeted by attack advertising

name: Brian Deese
title: fmr Director of the National Economic Council (Biden Administration)
notes_head: Brian Deese serves as Institute Innovation Fellow at Massachusetts Institute of Technology and Co-Founder and CEO at Foundry-Logic. He served as Director of the National Economic Council from 2021 to 2023 under President Biden. He previously worked as Global Head of Sustainable Investing at BlackRock and held various positions in the Obama administration including Senior Advisor to the President. He has written extensively on energy policy for AI and America's infrastructure challenges.

name: Mike Braun
title: Governor of Indiana (2025-present); former U.S. Senator, IN (2019-2025)
primary_org: Office of the Governor of Indiana
notes_head: Mike Braun is the 52nd Governor of Indiana who previously served as a U.S. Senator from 2018. He is a conservative Republican who spent his career as an entrepreneur before entering politics, building a business that grew into a national company employing thousands of Americans. Braun issued an executive order creating the Indiana Initiative for Drone Dominance Task Force to support President Trump's drone dominance executive orders. He has been advocating for Indiana to become a national leader in artificial intelligence infrastructure, emphasizing the importance of data centers and energy ca

name: Gary Peters
title: U.S. Senator from Michigan, Chairman of Senate Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs Committee
notes_head: Gary Peters is a U.S. Senator from Michigan who has served since 2015 and serves as the Ranking Member of the Senate Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs Committee. He announced on January 28, 2025, that he will not seek reelection in 2026. Peters has been rated the most effective U.S. Senator for three consecutive Congresses by the nonpartisan Center for Effective Lawmaking. He has focused on artificial intelligence policy, introducing multiple bipartisan bills including the AI and Critical Technology Workforce Framework Act and the PREPARED for AI Act.

name: Maria Cantwell
title: U.S. Senator from Washington, Ranking Member of the Senate Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation
notes_head: Maria Cantwell is a U.S. Senator from Washington State, serving as Ranking Member of the Senate Committee on Commerce, Science and Transportation. She has introduced multiple AI-related bills in 2026, including the NSF AI Education Act and the Small Business Artificial Intelligence Training Act. She previously served in the U.S. House of Representatives from 1993-95 and worked as vice president for consumer products at Progressive Networks (later RealNetworks). She was first elected to the U.S. Senate in 2000 and won reelection in 2024 against Republican challenger Raul Garcia.

name: Frank Lucas
title: Chairman, U.S. House Committee on Science, Space and Technology, OK-3
notes_head: Frank Lucas is a Republican U.S. Representative from Oklahoma's 3rd congressional district who serves as Chairman of the House Science, Space, and Technology Committee. He introduced the Workforce for AI Trust Act (H.R. 9215) with Ranking Member Zoe Lofgren on July 30, 2024, aimed at promoting a multidisciplinary workforce to advance AI integration and training. Lucas chairs the House Financial Services Task Force on Monetary Policy, Treasury Market Resilience and Economic Prosperity and wants Congress to help stabilize financial markets by insulating the Federal Reserve from political influen

name: Ted Lieu
title: U.S. Representative, CA-36th District, Vice Chair of House Democratic Caucus, Co-Chair of House Democratic Commission on AI and the Innovation Economy
notes_head: Ted Lieu represents California's 36th Congressional District and is serving his sixth term in Congress. He was elected by his colleagues to serve as Vice Chair of the Democratic Caucus, making him the highest ranking Asian American to have ever served in House Leadership. In the 118th Congress, Ted was the Co-Chair of the House's Bipartisan Taskforce on Artificial Intelligence. He is one of three Computer Science Majors in Congress and one of four Members of Congress with a computer science degree.

name: Ed Markey
title: U.S. Senator from Massachusetts (Junior Senator)
notes_head: Senator Edward J. Markey (D-Mass.) is a member of the Commerce, Science and Transportation Committee and has served in the U.S. Senate since 2013. He reintroduced the Artificial Intelligence Civil Rights Act on December 2, 2025, alongside Representative Yvette Clarke, to prevent companies from using biased and discriminatory AI-powered algorithms in critical decisions affecting Americans' lives. Markey stated "We must address AI-powered bias and discrimination in the AI age" and emphasized that "Under the AI Civil Rights Act, America would show leadership in AI—not just technological leadershi

name: Don Beyer
title: U.S. Representative, VA-8; Co-Chair, Congressional AI Caucus
notes_head: Don Beyer is serving his fifth term as the U.S. Representative from Virginia's 8th District, representing Arlington, Alexandria, Falls Church, and parts of Fairfax County. He serves on Congress' Joint Economic Committee, and also serves on the House Committee on Ways and Means. He was the Lieutenant Governor of Virginia from 1990 to 1998, and was Ambassador to Switzerland and Liechtenstein under President Obama. He is currently attending George Mason University as a part time student pursuing a master's degree in machine learning, in part to help inform his work on AI in Congress.

name: Anna Eshoo
title: fmr U.S. Representative, CA-16
notes_head: Anna Eshoo is a U.S. Representative who served California's 16th Congressional District from 1993 to 2025, retiring in January 2025. She co-chairs the Congressional Artificial Intelligence (AI) Caucus and was named one of Time magazine's 100 most influential people in AI in 2023. She served as Chair of the Health Subcommittee and was the first woman in the history of Congress to hold that position. Representatives Ted Lieu, Anna Eshoo, and Ken Buck have proposed a 20-member national AI commission to develop regulatory frameworks.

name: Gavin Newsom
title: Governor of California
notes_head: Gavin Newsom is the 40th Governor of California who took office in 2019 and is currently serving his second term. He signed executive order N-5-26 on March 30, 2026 to regulate artificial intelligence implications prior to California government contracts, emphasizing public safety and misuse. California is home to 33 of the top 50 privately held AI companies around the globe and leads with 25% of all AI patents, conference papers, and companies. He announced California has signed agreements with four of the largest technology companies in the GenAI sector including Google, Adobe, IBM, and Micr

name: Jay Obernolte
title: U.S. Representative, CA-23, Chairman of House Science Subcommittee on Research and Technology
notes_head: Jay Obernolte is a U.S. Congressman representing California's 23rd District since 2021 and is the only serving member of Congress with a graduate-level degree in AI. He co-chaired the bipartisan House Task Force on AI with Ted Lieu, which produced a comprehensive report in December 2024 with 66 findings and 89 recommendations. He currently serves as Chairman of the Research and Technology Subcommittee under the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology. Obernolte is the owner, president, and technical director of FarSight Studios, a video game development company he founded in 1990.

name: Lynne Parker
title: Principal Deputy Director of the White House Office of Science and Technology Policy; Executive Director of the President's Council of Advisors on Science and Technology (PCAST)
primary_org: White House Office of Science and Technology Policy (OSTP)
notes_head: Lynne Parker is Principal Deputy Director of the White House Office of Science and Technology Policy and Executive Director of the President's Council of Advisors on Science and Technology. In January 2025, she was appointed by President Donald Trump as Executive Director of the President's Council of Advisors on Science and Technology and Counselor to the Director of the White House Office of Science and Technology Policy (OSTP). She has shaped national AI policy across four US presidential administrations. Parker is Associate Vice Chancellor Emerita (retired) and Founding Director of the AI

name: Arati Prabhakar
title: OSTP Director (Biden Admin, 2022-2025); fmr DARPA Director (2012-2017); fmr NIST Director (1993-1997)
primary_org: White House Office of Science and Technology Policy
notes_head: Arati Prabhakar served as the 12th director of the White House Office of Science and Technology Policy from October 3, 2022 to January 20, 2025, and was the Science and Technology Advisor to President Biden. She was the first woman and person of color to be confirmed by the U.S. Senate as the Director of the White House Office of Science and Technology Policy. She was instrumental in passing President Biden's executive order on AI in 2023, which sets guidelines for tech companies to make AI safer and more transparent, and was the first to demonstrate ChatGPT to the president in the Oval Office

name: Kathy Hochul
title: Governor of New York State
notes_head: Kathy Hochul is the 57th and first female Governor of New York State. She previously served as Erie county clerk (2007–11), U.S. representative for New York's 26th congressional district (2011–13), and lieutenant governor (2015–21) to Gov. Andrew Cuomo. In her January 13, 2026, State of the State address, Governor Hochul announced a series of proposals related to AI and technology regulation that she said are intended to protect workers and consumers. She announced the launch of the FutureWorks Commission in March 2026, which plans to offer policy proposals on AI best practices that spur innov

name: Valerie Foushee
title: Candidate, NC House District 04
notes_head:
Democratic U.S. Representative from North Carolina (NC-04) who serves on the House Science, Space, and Technology Committee and co-chairs the House Democratic Commission on AI and the Innovation Economy. She is actively involved in advancing AI policy through legislation like the HBCU Artificial Intelligence Research Leadership Act, focusing on inclusive access to AI research and education, particularly for underrepresented communities and HBCUs. She is supported by pro-safety organizations like Jobs and Democracy PAC.

Running in the NC House District 04 race, which has seen significant AI-re

name: Jesse Jackson Jr.
title: Candidate, IL House District 02
notes_head:
Jesse Jackson Jr. is a Democratic politician and former U.S. Representative from Illinois's 2nd Congressional District seeking to reclaim his seat in 2026. He emphasizes responsible AI development with equitable access and has actively welcomed AI use in his campaign, including an AI-generated ad featuring endorsements. He is supported by pro-innovation PACs focused on AI policy.

Running in the IL House District 02 race, which has seen significant AI-related super PAC spending. See https://elections.transformernews.ai/ for live spending data.

name: Melissa Bean
title: Candidate, IL House District 08
notes_head:
Melissa Bean is a Democratic politician from Illinois who served in the U.S. House of Representatives from 2005-2011 and made a political comeback in 2026. She is supported by pro-innovation groups like Think Big and emphasizes pragmatic, results-oriented governance with a balanced stance on AI policy.

Running in the IL House District 08 race, which has seen significant AI-related super PAC spending. See https://elections.transformernews.ai/ for live spending data.

name: Chris Gober
title: Candidate, TX House District 10
notes_head:
Republican candidate for U.S. House District 10 in Texas, seventh-generation Texan and Texas A&M graduate (2001). Former chief counsel for Elon Musk's America PAC and founder of conservative law firm Lex Politica. Endorsed by Trump and backed by pro-innovation groups, but no specific public statements on AI policy, regulation, risks, or safety were found in provided sources.

Running in the TX House District 10 race, which has seen significant AI-related super PAC spending. See https://elections.transformernews.ai/ for live spending data.

name: Tom Sell
title: Candidate, TX House District 19
notes_head:
Republican candidate for Texas's 19th Congressional District runoff election. Fifth-generation West Texan with background in agriculture and small business. No explicit public positions on AI regulation, risks, or safety are available in provided sources.

Running in the TX House District 19 race, which has seen significant AI-related super PAC spending. See https://elections.transformernews.ai/ for live spending data.

name: Jessica Steinmann
title: Candidate, TX House District 08
notes_head:
Republican candidate for U.S. House in Texas's 8th District with endorsement from President Trump. She has DOJ background (Director of Office of Victims of Crime, 2020-2021), holds J.D. from Baylor and B.A. from UT Austin. Supported by pro-innovation AI industry groups, though her specific AI policy positions are not publicly detailed.

Running in the TX House District 08 race, which has seen significant AI-related super PAC spending. See https://elections.transformernews.ai/ for live spending data.

name: Laurie Buckhout
title: Candidate, NC House District 01
notes_head:
Laurie Buckhout is a Republican candidate for North Carolina's 1st Congressional District with a background as a retired U.S. Army Colonel. She is backed by AI industry-supported PACs and the pro-innovation group American Mission, positioning her as favorable to AI industry interests. Specific details about her stated positions on AI regulation, risks, and safety are not provided in available sources.

Running in the NC House District 01 race, which has seen significant AI-related super PAC spending. See https://elections.transformernews.ai/ for live spending data.

name: Clay Fuller
title: Candidate, GA House District 14
notes_head:
Republican candidate for Georgia's 14th Congressional District with background as District Attorney for Lookout Mountain Judicial Circuit and Lieutenant Colonel in Air National Guard. Emphasizes border security, economic growth, constitutional rights, and public safety. Positions on AI are characterized as pro-innovation with safety guardrails, though specific AI policy details are limited in available sources.

Running in the GA House District 14 race, which has seen significant AI-related super PAC spending. See https://elections.transformernews.ai/ for live spending data.

name: Colin Allred
title: Candidate, TX House District 33
notes_head:
Democratic U.S. Representative from Texas's 33rd Congressional District with background in law and civil rights. Launched Marley, an AI-powered constituent engagement tool, demonstrating commitment to responsible technology use while acknowledging AI limitations and the need for continuous improvement.

Running in the TX House District 33 race, which has seen significant AI-related super PAC spending. See https://elections.transformernews.ai/ for live spending data.

name: Pete Ricketts
title: Candidate, NE Senate
notes_head:
Republican U.S. Senator from Nebraska with background as former CEO of TD Ameritrade. Active in AI and technology policy, notably co-sponsoring the SAFE Chips Act to restrict AI chip exports to China. Emphasis on national security and U.S. technological leadership balanced with safety considerations.

Running in the NE Senate race, which has seen significant AI-related super PAC spending. See https://elections.transformernews.ai/ for live spending data.

name: Jace Yarbrough
title: Candidate, TX House District 32
notes_head:
Jace Yarbrough is a Republican candidate for Texas Congressional District 32 with a background in law (JD from Stanford) and US Air Force Reserve service (since 2015). He is endorsed by Donald Trump and Gov. Greg Abbott, and receives support from the pro-innovation American Mission PAC ($129,367), positioning him as a pro-innovation candidate in the AI policy space.

Running in the TX House District 32 race, which has seen significant AI-related super PAC spending. See https://elections.transformernews.ai/ for live spending data.

name: Alexandra Mealer
title: Candidate, TX House District 09
notes_head:
Alexandra 'Alex' Mealer is a Republican political candidate running for Texas' 9th Congressional District. She previously ran as the GOP nominee against Harris County Judge Lina Hidalgo in 2022 and advanced to a primary runoff election. No publicly available information exists regarding her specific positions on AI policy, AI regulation, or AI risk and safety.

Running in the TX House District 09 race, which has seen significant AI-related super PAC spending. See https://elections.transformernews.ai/ for live spending data.

name: Carlos De La Cruz
title: Candidate, TX House District 35
notes_head:
Carlos De La Cruz is a 47-year-old Republican U.S. House candidate for Texas' 35th Congressional District with 20 years of U.S. Air Force service and background as a small business owner (kickboxing gym). He received a late Trump endorsement in the 2026 primary. No specific positions on AI policy, regulation, or AI risk are documented in available sources.

Running in the TX House District 35 race, which has seen significant AI-related super PAC spending. See https://elections.transformernews.ai/ for live spending data.

name: Lina Khan
title: Former Chair, Federal Trade Commission (FTC), 2021-2025
notes_head: Led FTC's AI enforcement including OpenAI investigation (July 2023), Rite Aid AI facial recognition ban (December 2023), and joint interagency AI statement with DOJ/CFPB/EEOC (April 2023). Published op-ed 'We Must Regulate A.I.' in New York Times. Estimated 15% p(doom) according to sources. Known for aggressive antitrust enforcement and authored influential 'Amazon's Antitrust Paradox' as law student.

name: Alan Davidson
title: Assistant Secretary for Communications and Information, National Telecommunications and Information Administration (NTIA), U.S. Department of Commerce
notes_head: Led NTIA's AI Accountability Policy Report (March 2024) calling for improved transparency, independent evaluations, and consequences for AI systems imposing unacceptable risks. Currently working on assessment of benefits and harms of open-weight AI models per Biden's AI Executive Order, with report due to White House in July 2024. Co-leads President's Task Force on Kids Online Health and Safety and oversees $42 billion broadband infrastructure programs.

name: Laurie Locascio
title: President and CEO, American National Standards Institute (ANSI); Former Director, National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST); Former Under Secretary of Commerce for Standards and Techn
notes_head: As NIST Director (2022-2024), Locascio launched the U.S. AI Safety Institute in November 2023 and oversaw release of the AI Risk Management Framework in January 2023. She participated in roundtables on AI safety and testified before Congress on NIST's AI priorities, securing $50M in FY2025 budget for AI safety work. Now leads ANSI, coordinating private sector standards development including AI standards.

name: Elham Tabassi
title: Associate Director for Emerging Technologies, Information Technology Laboratory, NIST; Chief AI Advisor and Chief Technologist of the AI Safety Institute
notes_head: Iranian-born, joined NIST in 1999 after coming to US in 1994 for master's in electrical engineering. Principal architect of NIST Fingerprint Image Quality standard used by FBI. Led development of AI Risk Management Framework 1.0 released January 2023 that laid groundwork for Biden's AI Executive Order. Named by Time Magazine as one of 100 most influential people in AI in 2024.

name: Jonathan Kanter
title: Assistant Attorney General for the Antitrust Division, Department of Justice (2021-2024); Distinguished Teaching Professor, Washington University Law School
notes_head: Led DOJ Antitrust Division 2021-2024, confirmed by Senate 68-29. Delivered remarks at Stanford AI workshop stating DOJ would scrutinize AI sector 'with urgency' to prevent monopoly consolidation. Worked with FTC to divide AI oversight responsibilities - DOJ handles chip manufacturers while FTC oversees software companies. Appointed DOJ's first Chief Technologist to enhance AI expertise for investigations.

name: Jen Easterly
title: Former Director, Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA)
notes_head: Served as CISA Director from July 2021 to January 2025, overseeing implementation of Biden's AI Executive Order 14028. Frequently warns that AI represents 'the most powerful weapon of our time' and advocates for 'secure-by-design' AI development. Named CEO of RSA Conference in January 2026 after leaving CISA. Rhodes Scholar and 20-year Army veteran who helped establish US Cyber Command.

name: Stefano Mazzocchi
title: Chief AI Officer, Department of Commerce
notes_head: No information found in search results for a Stefano Mazzocchi serving as Chief AI Officer at the Department of Commerce. Search results show other individuals with similar names in different roles.

name: Margrethe Vestager
title: Former Executive Vice President, European Commission for A Europe Fit for the Digital Age; Former EU Commissioner for Competition
notes_head: Led development of the EU AI Act (2021-2024) establishing world's first major AI legislation with risk-based approach. Known as aggressive antitrust enforcer against Big Tech companies including landmark cases against Google, Apple, Amazon, and Facebook. Served as EU Competition Commissioner 2014-2024 and Executive Vice President for Digital Age 2019-2024. Described by critics as implementing 'anti-American antitrust' policies that disproportionately target U.S. tech companies.

name: Thierry Breton
title: Former European Commissioner for Internal Market; EU AI Act Architect
notes_head: Led the 38-hour 'trilogue' negotiations that finalized the EU AI Act in December 2023. Sanctioned by the US in December 2024 with travel ban and asset freeze for his role in EU tech regulation, specifically the Digital Services Act. Previously wrote prescient sci-fi novels in the 1980s about fake news and generative AI. Resigned from the European Commission in September 2024 amid tensions with Ursula von der Leyen.

name: Audrey Tang
title: Taiwan's Cyber Ambassador (2024-present); Former Digital Minister (2016-2024); World's First Transgender Cabinet Minister
notes_head: Led Taiwan's response to deepfake scams in 2024 using citizen assemblies with AI facilitation, resulting in new anti-fraud legislation. Pioneered 'humor over rumor' strategy during COVID-19. Developed vTaiwan platform for digital democracy and civic participation. Currently serves as Taiwan's Cyber Ambassador and Senior Fellow at Project Liberty Institute working on bridging algorithms for social media.

name: Marietje Schaake
title: International Policy Director, Stanford HAI; Former Member, European Parliament (2009-2019)
notes_head: Served as Chair of Working Group on Internal Risk Management for EU AI Act Code of Practice in 2025. Co-authored white paper on Multilateral AI Research Institute (MAIRI) with Stanford colleagues in 2022. Member of UN High-level Advisory Body on AI Executive Committee. Argues that concentration of corporate power is the single greatest challenge in AI governance and prevents independent investigation of AI systems.

name: Amy Klobuchar
title: U.S. Senator from Minnesota, Chair of Senate Judiciary Subcommittee on Competition Policy, Antitrust, and Consumer Rights
notes_head: Received Ukrainian Order of Merit, First Class from President Zelenskyy (2022). Author of 'Antitrust' (New York Times Best Seller). Graduated magna cum laude from Yale University (1982) and University of Chicago Law School with high honors (1985). Led bipartisan effort that resulted in first major U.S. AI harm legislation with Take It Down Act passage. Chair of Senate Rules Committee oversaw three bipartisan AI election bills passage in May 2024. Running for Minnesota Governor in 2026 election.

name: Ron Wyden
title: U.S. Senator from Oregon (Democrat), Ranking Member of Senate Finance Committee, Senior Member of Senate Intelligence Committee
notes_head: Long-serving Senator since 1996, previously served in House 1981-1996. Co-founded Oregon Gray Panthers advocacy group and served as director of Oregon Legal Services for the Elderly (1977-1979). Holds JD from University of Oregon School of Law (1974) and BA from Stanford University. Known as leading privacy advocate who has held over 1,080 town hall meetings across Oregon's 36 counties annually. Has been warning for nearly a decade about government purchase of commercial surveillance data, calling it 'just as sensitive as information the government collects directly.' Currently fighting Trump

name: Marco Rubio
title: U.S. Secretary of State (2025-present), Acting National Security Advisor (February-August 2025), Former U.S. Senator from Florida (2011-2025)
notes_head: First Latino to serve as U.S. Secretary of State, unanimously confirmed January 25, 2025. Served as Vice Chair of Senate Intelligence Committee (2021-2025) with focus on AI national security and U.S.-China competition. Described as 'virtual secretary of state for Latin America' during Trump's first term. Banned from entering China due to sanctions imposed twice in 2020. JD degree from University of Miami (1996), BA from University of Florida (1993).

name: Tom Cotton
notes_head: Tom Cotton is a U.S. Senator representing Arkansas who introduced the DATA Act of 2026, which would allow AI data center companies to bypass federal electricity regulations by building independent energy infrastructure. Cotton stated that "American dominance in artificial intelligence and other crucial emerging industries should not come at the expense of Arkansans paying higher energy costs". On November 13, 2024, Senate Republicans selected Cotton as the next conference chair, making him the third-highest ranked Republican in the Senate. Cotton previously co-introduced a resolution with Sena

name: Andy Kim
notes_head: Andy Kim is a U.S. Senator from New Jersey who serves as a Democrat on multiple committees including Commerce, Science, and Transportation. He has been actively involved in AI-related legislation, co-introducing the Artificial Intelligence Ready Data Act with Senator Ted Budd to open government data sets for training American AI models. Kim also co-introduced the AI Talent Act with Senator Jon Husted to create an AI and tech talent team within the U.S. Office of Personnel Management. He was born on July 12, 1982, in Boston, Massachusetts, to South Korean immigrant parents and is the first Kore

name: Jim Risch
notes_head: Jim Risch is a U.S. Senator from Idaho who has served since 2009 and is a member of the Republican Party. He serves on five key committees: Energy and Natural Resources, Foreign Relations, Small Business and Entrepreneurship, Intelligence, and Ethics, and serves as Chairman of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee. He previously served as Idaho's 31st Governor in 2006 and as Lieutenant Governor, and achieved major tax reform as Governor, cutting property taxes by 20%. He is consistently recognized as one of the most conservative members of the U.S. Senate.

name: Donald Trump
notes_head: Donald Trump served as the 45th President of the United States from 2017 to 2021 and was inaugurated as the 47th president on January 20, 2025. On March 20, 2026, the Trump Administration released a National AI Legislative Framework that addresses six key objectives including protecting children, safeguarding American communities, and ensuring American AI dominance. In December 2025, President Trump signed an Executive Order to protect American AI innovation from inconsistent state laws and directed the Attorney General to establish an AI Litigation Task Force. Trump has appointed David Sacks

name: John Kennedy
notes_head: Sen. John Kennedy (R-LA) questioned David Evan Harris about AI regulation during a Senate Judiciary Committee hearing on September 17, 2024. Kennedy co-introduced the AI Labeling Act (S. 2691) with Sen. Brian Schatz (D-HI) on October 24, 2023, which would notify consumers when they interact with AI-generated content and require developers to disclose such content. Kennedy has introduced legislation to withhold senators' pay during government shutdowns, with the U.S. Senate Rules Committee unanimously advancing this proposal in December 2025. Kennedy reacted positively to President Trump's join

name: Cory Gardner
notes_head: Cory Gardner is President & CEO of NCTA – The Internet & Television Association, beginning his tenure on September 22, 2025. He previously served as a U.S. Senator from Colorado from 2015 to 2021 and as a U.S. Representative for Colorado's 4th Congressional District from 2011 to 2015. Gardner served as chair of the National Republican Senatorial Committee from 2017 to 2019. He was consistently ranked among the most bipartisan senators, including being ranked the 5th most bipartisan senator of the 115th Congress in March 2019.

name: Katie Britt
notes_head: Katie Britt is a U.S. Senator from Alabama serving since 2023, and is a member of the Republican Party. She is calling for guardrails on artificial intelligence chatbots because of the potential to harm minors. Britt has two teenagers, ages 15 and 16, and draws on her experience as a mother in her advocacy work. She previously served as president and CEO of the Business Council of Alabama from 2019 to 2021, and as chief of staff for Senator Richard Shelby from 2016.

name: Jerry McNerney
notes_head: Former U.S. Representative (CA-9, 2007-2023) who chaired the Congressional AI Caucus and authored the AI in Government Act (2020). Transitioned to California State Senate District 5 in Dec 2024. Authored SB 813, which creates the CA Artificial Intelligence Standards and Safety Commission with independent verification organizations for AI safety standards. Previously a Fellow at Stanford HAI and Senior Policy Advisor at Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman. Also affiliated with the AI Trust Foundation. Math PhD; one of few STEM-trained members of the legislature.

name: Thomas Umberg
notes_head: California State Senator (D-Santa Ana, SD-34) and Chair of the Senate Judiciary Committee, which is the key committee for AI regulation bills in the CA legislature. Authored SB 574 restricting lawyers' and arbitrators' use of AI (requiring human review, banning delegation of decisions to AI, protecting confidential data). Publicly opposed Trump executive order to preempt state AI regulation. As Judiciary Chair, shapes which AI bills advance through the legislature, including SB 1047 (Wiener) and other safety bills.

name: Steve Padilla
notes_head: California State Senator (D-San Diego, SD-18) and leading voice on AI consumer protection, especially for minors. Authored SB 243, the nation's first chatbot safeguards law (signed by Newsom), requiring operators to implement safeguards against sexual content for minors and disclosure that chatbots are AI. Also authored SB 420 (California AI Bill of Rights framework), SB 903 (protecting against AI therapy products), and proposed a 4-year moratorium on AI chatbots in children's toys. Publicly criticized OpenAI for lobbying against chatbot protections.

name: William Kimmitt
notes_head: 17th Under Secretary of Commerce for International Trade, sworn in Aug 2025. Leads ITA and serves as principal trade adviser to Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick. Drives the American AI Exports Program, including leading ITA delegation to India AI Impact Summit 2026 in Bangalore/New Delhi. Previously a partner at Kirkland & Ellis LLP (complex litigation, trade/investment advisory). BA from Duke, JD from Georgetown Law.

name: Matt Damschroder
notes_head: Director of the Ohio Department of Job and Family Services (ODJFS) since July 2021, appointed by Gov. Mike DeWine. Previously served as Director of the Ohio Department of Administrative Services (DAS) from Jan 2019, where he oversaw the Office of Information Technology and InnovateOhio Platform. Under his leadership, ODJFS won the 2025 Merrill Baumgardner Award for AI innovation in unemployment services, deploying multilingual virtual assistants, intelligent document processing, call center AI, and GenAI policy simplification tools. Advocates 'responsible AI' focused on user experience over bu

name: Ron DeSantis
notes_head: Governor of Florida (Republican). Proposed an AI Bill of Rights for Florida citizens covering data privacy, parental controls, consumer protections, and restrictions on AI use of individuals' name/image/likeness without consent. Also proposed protections against hyperscale AI data center costs and empowering local governments to reject their development. Notably pushed back against Trump's Dec 2025 executive order seeking to preempt state AI regulation, asserting 'We have a right to do this.' Claims Florida's proposed rules are consistent with federal goals on child safety, data center oversig

name: Spencer Cox
notes_head: Governor of Utah (Republican). Leading advocate for 'pro-human AI' policy at the state level. Announced $10M initiative for AI workforce readiness at the Utah AI Summit (Dec 2025). Utah created an Office of Artificial Intelligence Policy in 2023. Pushed back strongly against Trump's Dec 2025 executive order preempting state AI regulation, asserting states should lead on AI policy. Proposed legislation to reduce AI chatbot harms, require transparency for AI-generated deepfakes, restrain AI in healthcare, and create a state framework requiring frontier AI developers to disclose safety protocols

name: Casey Mock
notes_head: Casey Mock is Chief of Policy and Public Affairs at the Center for Humane Technology. Previously spent four years at Amazon leading state and local tax policy. Strong advocate for technology regulation and corporate accountability — argues companies must be held liable for harms caused by their AI products. Compares tech industry lobbying against regulation to the tobacco industry. Advocates for liability-focused AI legislation, arguing that clear liability rules actually empower innovation by setting predictable boundaries. Emphasizes designing platforms for people over profit and realigning

name: Camille Carlton
notes_head: Camille Carlton is Policy Director at the Center for Humane Technology. Named to Business Insider's AI 100 (2023). Directly involved in landmark lawsuits against CharacterAI and OpenAI CEO Sam Altman around AI accountability and platform liability. Draws clear line from social media harms to AI chatbot risks — argues AI isn't just repeating social media's mistakes but amplifying them (always on, deeply personal, emotionally responsive). Advocates design-focused regulation addressing root causes of harm rather than outcomes or content. Writes for TechPolicy.Press on AI governance. Emphasizes pr

name: Gordon M. Goldstein
notes_head: Adjunct Senior Fellow at the Council on Foreign Relations (CFR), focusing on advances in global technology and their consequences for U.S. economy and foreign policy. Directs a CFR roundtable series on technology, innovation, and American primacy. Former managing director at Silver Lake (2010-2018), the world's largest technology-focused investment firm. Previously Senior Adviser to the UN Secretary General's Strategic Planning Unit and co-directed CFR's Project on the Information Revolution. Author and foreign policy scholar with PhD from Columbia University in political science and internati

name: António Guterres
notes_head: UN Secretary-General (2017-present). Proposed international AI governance body modeled on the IAEA in 2023. Convened the High-Level Advisory Body on AI which delivered 7 recommendations in 2024. Championed establishment of the UN Independent International Scientific Panel on AI and Global Dialogue on AI Governance (both approved by GA in 2025). Consistently advocates for science-based AI policy and multilateral governance, though the U.S. has rejected UN-centralized AI oversight.

name: Amandeep Singh Gill
notes_head: UN Under-Secretary-General and Special Envoy for Digital and Emerging Technologies (Jan 2025-present); previously Secretary-General's Envoy on Technology (2022-2024). Indian diplomat who is a central figure in UN AI governance architecture. Instrumental in establishing the Independent International Scientific Panel on AI and Global Dialogue on AI Governance. Emphasizes evidence-based, science-informed AI policy and inclusive governance that gives all 193 member states a seat at the table. Advocates strongly for Global South participation in AI governance.

name: Matt Turek
notes_head: Former DARPA deputy director of the Information Innovation Office (I2O). Joined DARPA in 2018 as program manager; led the Explainable AI (XAI), Media Forensics (MediFor), Semantic Forensics (SemaFor), and Machine Common Sense (MCS) programs. Served as acting deputy director of I2O (June-Oct 2021), then deputy office director (May 2022-Oct 2025). DARPA tour ended Oct 2025; transitioned to Chief Scientist at STR. PhD in Computer Science from Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute.

name: Benjamin Grosof
notes_head: DARPA program manager in the Defense Sciences Office (joined Sept 2023). Leads the CLARA (Causal Logical AI Reasoning and Auditing) program. Pioneer in knowledge representation and reasoning (KRR), including Rulelog, courteous defeasibility, and neuro-symbolic AI. Extensive background applying AI to legal, regulatory, policy compliance, and defense/intelligence domains. Previously Chief Scientist at Coherent Knowledge. PhD from Stanford, Hertz Fellow.

name: Ted Budd
notes_head: Republican Senator (NC) and Chair of the Senate Subcommittee on Science, Manufacturing, and Competitiveness. Actively shapes AI legislation through bipartisan bills on government data access, AI lab networks, and public health preparedness. Strongly opposes overregulation, favoring a light-touch, innovation-first federal framework.

name: Dick Durbin
notes_head: Democratic Senator from Illinois (1997–2027) and former Senate Judiciary Committee chair. Co-introduced the bipartisan AI LEAD Act to enable products-liability suits against AI developers, and the TEST AI Act to strengthen federal AI testing capacity. Consistently advocated for mandatory AI oversight.

name: Pam Bondi
notes_head: U.S. Attorney General, confirmed February 4, 2025. Previously served as Florida Attorney General (2011-2019). Key AI policy action: created the DOJ AI Litigation Task Force under Trump executive order to challenge state AI laws deemed inconsistent with federal policy or the goal of U.S. "global AI dominance." The task force targets state laws on grounds they are illegal, unconstitutionally regulate interstate commerce, or are overridden by federal regulations. Supports a "minimally burdensome national policy framework for AI" to promote American competitiveness.

name: Mike Johnson
notes_head: Speaker of the U.S. House of Representatives (Republican, Louisiana). Leading congressional effort to implement the Trump administration national AI framework. Outlined a 3-point plan for AI: (1) treat AI as a matter of national security, (2) keep AI in U.S. and allied hands, (3) move "at the speed that victory demands." Stated "America will win the AI race if government resists the siren song of control, and if industry steps up as our patriotic partner." Praised the White House AI roadmap for providing "innovators with much-needed certainty, while protecting consumers and prioritizing kids o

name: Steve Scalise
notes_head: House Majority Leader (Republican, Louisiana). Key Republican leader on AI policy in Congress. Co-authored joint statement with Speaker Johnson committing to implement the Trump administration national AI framework. Led effort to include preemption of state AI regulations in the NDAA (ultimately dropped). Supports Trump executive order on AI and stated willingness to "codify some of the things the President is trying to do" on AI. Advocates for a unified national AI framework that provides certainty for innovators while protecting consumers.

name: Joanna Rodriguez
notes_head: Communications Director at National Republican Senatorial Committee (NRSC) since Jan 2025. Championed NRSC use of AI in political campaigns, defended AI-generated campaign ads including deepfake-style ad targeting Chuck Schumer (Oct 2025). Stated "AI is here and not going anywhere. Adapt & win or pearl clutch & lose." Previously worked in office of Sen. Pete Ricketts.

name: Dave McCormick
notes_head: Republican U.S. Senator from Pennsylvania (sworn in Jan 2025). Former CEO of Bridgewater Associates (2020-2022), world's largest hedge fund. West Point graduate, Bronze Star recipient (82nd Airborne, Gulf War). Under Secretary of Treasury for International Affairs under George W. Bush (2007-2009).
