Holds economics background with focus on AI automation trajectories.

name: Pablo Villalobos
notes_head: Staff Researcher at Epoch AI and one of the organization's earliest members (first volunteer-employee). Joined after impressing founder Jaime Sevilla during a high school presentation. Specializes in AI data scaling and training efficiency. Lead author of influential 2022 study projecting that language models would exhaust publicly available human-generated text between 2026-2032 ("peak data"). Also leads Epoch's Biology AI Dataset covering 360+ models, analyzing safety measures in biological AI systems. His research on data constraints has become a key reference for policymakers evaluating AI

name: Lennart Heim
notes_head: Leading AI compute governance researcher. Associate Information Scientist at RAND and Professor of Policy Analysis at Pardee RAND Graduate School. Former Senior Research Fellow at the Centre for the Governance of AI (GovAI), where he remains an Adjunct Fellow. Co-founder of Epoch AI. Member of the OECD.AI Expert Group on AI Compute and Climate. His research focuses on how compute can be leveraged as an instrument for AI governance — including compute provider KYC schemes, hardware-enabled governance mechanisms (HEMs), and export control policy. Frames AI risk across three categories: accident

name: Anson Ho
notes_head: Staff Researcher and founding team member of Epoch AI (one of seven co-founders). Investigates drivers behind AI developments and societal impacts using tools from economics and deep learning. Focuses on quantitative forecasting of transformative AI timelines, including analysis of the Biological Anchors framework. Has contributed to Epoch's training compute database tracking 100+ ML models across history, with results featured in The Economist and at the OECD. Active on LessWrong and Alignment Forum. Work supports policymakers, grantmakers, and technical researchers making AI strategy decisio

name: Tom Davidson
notes_head: AI safety researcher currently at Forethought (AI Safety Research Nonprofit), previously Senior Research Fellow at Open Philanthropy and research scientist at UK AI Security Institute. Known for work on AI timelines, explosive economic growth from AI, and AI-enabled coup risk. Board member of Epoch AI.

name: Thore Graepel
notes_head: Research Lead at Google DeepMind and Professor of Machine Learning at UCL's Computer Science Department. Leading a team at DeepMind working on post-AGI futures. Champion of responsible innovation in AI, with research focused on multi-agent learning and cooperative AI. Founding member of the Cooperative AI Foundation (CAIF) Board of Trustees. Previously served on the Board of Directors at the Partnership on AI (PAI). Contributed to creating AlphaGo. His work frames cooperative social skills among AI agents as fundamental to safe and beneficial AI development.

name: Lewis Hammond
notes_head: Research Director (Co-Director) of the Cooperative AI Foundation. DPhil candidate in Computer Science at the University of Oxford. Affiliated with the Centre for the Governance of AI (GovAI). Pathways to AI Policy Fellow at the Wilson Center. Contributed to the International AI Safety Report 2026. Research spans game theory, machine learning, and verification — focused on safety and cooperation in multi-agent systems. Motivated by ensuring AI and other powerful technologies are developed and governed safely and democratically. BSc Mathematics and Philosophy (Warwick), MSc Artificial Intelligen

name: Nikki Sun
notes_head: Research and Programme Manager at the Oxford Martin AI Governance Initiative (AIGI). A decade of experience at the intersection of journalism, public policy, and emerging technologies. Previously a researcher at Chatham House examining AI's impact on work and labour in China. Regular speaker at UK parliamentary sessions on labour policies; runs training courses for UK civil servants. Former award-winning journalist and columnist in Hong Kong covering China technology and politics (Nikkei Asia, Financial Times, South China Morning Post). MSc International Political Economy from King's College L

name: Fazl Barez
notes_head: Senior Research Fellow at the University of Oxford and Technical AI Governance Lead at the Oxford Martin AI Governance Initiative (AIGI). Leads research in AI safety, interpretability (mechanistic interpretability of LLMs), and AI governance. Research affiliations with CSER (Cambridge), Digital Trust Centre (NTU Singapore), and University of Edinburgh. Previously Research Consultant at Anthropic, Co-Director and Advisor at Apart Research, and Technology and Security Policy Fellow at RAND Corporation. Teaches Oxford's AI Safety and Alignment course. Leads the research team at Martian. Translate

name: Ben Garfinkel
notes_head: Director of the Centre for the Governance of AI (GovAI), a non-profit research org. Previously postdoctoral researcher at the Oxford Martin AI Governance Initiative. Researches security implications of AI, causes of war, and methodology of technology risk forecasting.

name: Marta Ziosi
notes_head: Postdoctoral Researcher at the Oxford Martin AI Governance Initiative (AIGI), focusing on standards for frontier AI. AI policy expert with experience at DG CNECT (European Commission), Berkman Klein Centre (Harvard), CEIMIA (Montreal International Centre of Expertise in AI), and The Future Society. PhD from the Oxford Internet Institute on algorithmic bias and AI policy. Founder of AI for People, a nonprofit putting technology at the service of people. BSc in Mathematics and Philosophy (University College Maastricht), MSc Philosophy and Public Policy (LSE).

name: Marcel Mir Teijeiro
notes_head: AI policy consultant and researcher at the AI Standards Lab focused on the EU AI framework. Spanish qualified lawyer. Contributed to the Code of Practice multi-stakeholder process for General-Purpose AI Models, advising rights-holder groups across cultural and creative industries on transparency and IP. Previously worked on hash-based technical solutions for training dataset disclosure shared with the AI Office, NIST, and UK AI Safety Institute. AI Policy Fellow in the Equity and Data programme.

name: Koen Holtman
notes_head: Independent AI safety researcher and co-founder of the AI Standards Lab. Founder of Holtman Systems Research (est. 2022). PhD in Software Design from Eindhoven University of Technology for CS research at CERN. Active member of CEN-CENELEC JTC 21 committee writing European AI safety standards under the EU AI Act. Research uses mathematical models to reason about AGI safety. Posts on LessWrong and EA Forum on AI alignment topics.

name: Rokas Gipiškis
notes_head: AI safety researcher at the AI Standards Lab and PhD candidate at Vilnius University (Institute of Data Science and Digital Technologies). Compiled an extensive catalog of risk sources and risk management measures for general-purpose AI systems (arXiv 2410.23472). Presented on AI incident reporting pipelines at the AI and Societal Robustness Conference in Cambridge. Research interests: ML interpretability, XAI, AI safety, and risk management.

name: Jan Betley
notes_head: Independent AI safety researcher and consultant at TruthfulAI (Owain Evans' org). Former software developer (10+ years) who shifted to AI safety in 2023. ARENA and Astra Fellowship alumni. Lead author of the influential "Emergent Misalignment" paper showing that fine-tuning GPT-4o on insecure code causes broadly misaligned behavior. Also researches behavioral self-awareness and situational awareness in LLMs. 156 citations on Google Scholar. Married to co-researcher Anna Sztyber-Betley.

name: James Chua
notes_head: AI safety researcher at TruthfulAI. Former ML engineer at LeadiQ (2020-2023). Mentors Constellation/Anthropic fellows under TruthfulAI. Research focuses on behavioral self-awareness in LLMs, chain-of-thought faithfulness (introduced bias-augmented consistency training/BCT), and reward hacking. Co-author on emergent misalignment and subliminal learning research studied by OpenAI.

name: Anna Sztyber-Betley
notes_head: AI safety researcher collaborating part-time with TruthfulAI. PhD in Automatic Control and Robotics; assistant professor at Warsaw University of Technology (Faculty of Mechatronics). Co-author on the "Emergent Misalignment" paper and subliminal learning research with TruthfulAI. Specializes in system diagnosis and LLMs. Married to co-researcher Jan Betley.

name: Malcolm Murray
notes_head: Research Lead at SaferAI, leading quantitative risk assessment of LLMs on cybersecurity and biosecurity risks. Chapter Lead for the International AI Safety Report. Former Managing VP and Chief of Research at Gartner. Certified Superforecaster at Good Judgment. 20+ years in risk, strategy, research, consulting, and industry. Originally from Sweden, based in Abu Dhabi. Under his leadership SaferAI developed 9 cybersecurity risk models.

name: Esben Kran
notes_head: Founder and Director of Apart Research, an independent AI safety research organization that accelerates safety research through mentorship, collaborations, and research sprints. Left grad school at 22 to start Apart. Organization has produced 20+ papers, award-winning benchmarks, and engaged 4,000+ participants. Board member of EA Denmark and the European Network for AI Safety (ENAIS). Previously worked at Interacting Minds Centre on neuroimaging (fNIRS) research. Also co-founded AI Safety Founders.

name: Saad Siddiqui
notes_head: Senior AI Policy Researcher at the Safe AI Forum (SAIF), supporting the International Dialogues on AI Safety (IDAIS) Track 2 dialogue between senior AI scientists from China and the West. Affiliate of the Oxford Martin AI Governance Initiative. Research focuses on identifying areas of possible agreement between leading AI powers by analyzing overlap in existing regulatory approaches and historical analogies. Previously management consultant at Bain and Company. Winter Fellow at Centre for the Governance of AI (GovAI) focused on Sino-western AI safety cooperation. MA in Global Affairs from Tsin

name: Isabella Duan
notes_head: Senior AI Policy Researcher at Safe AI Forum (SAIF), focused on minimizing extreme AI risks through global coordination. Holds MA in Computational Social Science from University of Chicago and BS in Philosophy, Politics, Economics from UCL. Co-founded Existential Risks Lab at UChicago. Former Summer Research Fellow at Stanford Existential Risks Initiative (SERI). Co-author of "AI Alignment and Deception: A Primer" and "Bare Minimum Mitigations for Autonomous AI Development" at SAIF. MATS mentor. Research spans AI prediction implications on international relations, facial recognition governance

name: Daniel Murfet
notes_head: Co-founder and Head of Research at Timaeus. Mathematician formerly at University of Melbourne School of Mathematics and Statistics (left academia ~early 2025 to direct research at Timaeus full-time). Expert in algebraic geometry, mathematical logic, and singular learning theory (SLT). Established the research agenda pursued by Timaeus — applying SLT to AI alignment and interpretability. MATS mentor stream with Jesse Hoogland. SLT, developed by Sumio Watanabe, connects loss landscape geometry to internal model structure, enabling scalable tools for evaluating and aligning neural networks.

name: William MacAskill
notes_head: Scottish moral philosopher at Oxford, co-founder of the effective altruism movement. Author of "Doing Good Better" (2015) and "What We Owe the Future" (2022). Senior Research Fellow at Forethought, a new AI macrostrategy research nonprofit he co-founded. Currently focused on "AGI Preparedness" — charting the trajectory of rapid AI-driven change and identifying what should be done now to prepare. Has significantly updated his AI timeline views, taking seriously AGI within 3-5 years and the possibility of an intelligence explosion. Advocates slowing the intelligence explosion and proposes an int

name: Amrit Sidhu-Brar
notes_head: Co-founder and Director of Operations at Forethought, an AI macrostrategy research nonprofit co-founded with William MacAskill, Max Dalton, and Tom Davidson. Previously Director of Operations at the Center on Long-term Risk. Forethought researches how to navigate the transition to superintelligent AI, with work on AI-enabled coups, intelligence explosions, and challenges beyond alignment. Their research has fed into AI companies plans and shaped 80,000 Hours priority list.

name: Tolga Bilge
notes_head: Policy researcher at ControlAI, contributing to "A Narrow Path" policy plan with focus on Phase 1 (Stability) — designing an international AI governance framework resistant to collapse. Professional Superforecaster at Good Judgment Inc since 2021, scoring in the top 0.6%% of forecasters. Also forecasts for RAND Forecasting Initiative, The Swift Centre, Samotsvety Forecasting, and Sentinel. Holds BSc in Mathematics from University of St Andrews and MSc in Mathematics from University of Bergen. Main interests are AI safety/governance and existential risk reduction.

name: Adam Shimi
notes_head: Epistemologist specializing in AI alignment and x-risk, currently Policy Researcher at ControlAI. Previously Epistemology Lead at Conjecture (2022–2024). Co-authored 'The Compendium' on AGI risk and co-authored 'A Narrow Path' policy framework. Active writer on LessWrong and the Alignment Forum.

name: Jan Kulveit
notes_head: Czech AI safety researcher and physicist. PhD in physics from Charles University. Principal Investigator and co-founder of the Alignment of Complex Systems (ACS) Research Group at Charles University, Prague. Previously Research Fellow at Future of Humanity Institute, Oxford University, working on macrostrategy, AI alignment, and existential risk. Lead author of "Gradual Disempowerment: Systemic Existential Risks from Incremental AI Development" — argues that even incremental AI capability improvements can undermine human influence over economy, culture, and nation-states through mutually reinf

name: Raymond Douglas
notes_head: AI safety researcher at ACS Research (Alignment of Complex Systems, Charles University) and research affiliate at University of Toronto CS department with David Duvenaud. Previously a research scholar at SERI MATS. Works on understanding how AGI could affect society and on the nature of agency in systems of all kinds. Co-author on "Gradual Disempowerment: Systemic Existential Risks from Incremental AI Development" examining how incremental AI progress can erode human influence across interconnected societal systems. Active contributor to AI Alignment Forum and LessWrong on topics of institutio

name: Theia Vogel
notes_head: AI researcher at the Alignment of Complex Systems (ACS) Research Group, focusing on LLM persona research — how language models represent and reason about themselves, from persona representation and manipulation to model introspection. Creator of the repeng open-source library for representation engineering (control vectors for LLM steering). 2024 New Science Fellow. Background in computational linguistics. Previously worked full-time at SecureDNA (DNA synthesis screening for hazardous material) and at Nous Research (open-source AI). Continues to advise SecureDNA part-time. Active technical blo

name: Ondřej Havlíček
notes_head: Researcher at ACS Research (Alignment of Complex Systems, Charles University), exploring AI psychology, character, identity, and introspection using tools from experimental cognitive science. PhD background as a cognitive scientist studying human intentions and sense of agency through EEG, fMRI, and behavioral experiments at LMU Munich. Pivoted to industry, leading data science and ML teams across finance and retail before joining ACS. Co-author of "The Artificial Self: Characterising the landscape of AI identity" (arXiv, March 2026). Brings unique interdisciplinary lens combining cognitive sc

name: Annie Stephenson
notes_head: Researcher at ACS Research (Alignment of Complex Systems, Charles University). PhD in applied physics from Harvard University. Postdoc at Princeton University studying emergent collective behavior in online communities. At ACS, applies methods from complex systems, physics, and ecology to study the psychology and sociology of large language models. Interdisciplinary background bridges physics, complex systems theory, and AI safety research.

name: Nora Ammann
notes_head: Technical specialist at the Advanced Research and Invention Agency (ARIA) in the UK, serving as Programme Director for ARIA's Safeguarded AI programme. Co-founded and led Principles of Intelligence (PIBBSS), a research organization facilitating knowledge transfer from interdisciplinary sciences into AI safety. Research Affiliate at ACS (Alignment of Complex Systems). Nearly a decade working on making transformative AI go well, spanning AI assurance — hardware guarantees, formal verification, and coordination for safety claims between untrusting parties. Background in complex systems studies, p

name: Robert Huben
notes_head: AI safety researcher and mathematician. PhD in mathematics from University of Nebraska-Lincoln (2021), then worked as a scientist at a defense contractor. Founded the Substack 'From AI to ZI' covering AI safety topics. Supported by an Open Philanthropy grant (Oct 2022-Sep 2023), during which he published research on sparse autoencoders and attention-only transformers, presented at NeurIPS 2023 workshops. Blog now largely dormant as he shifted to formal research output.

name: Dawn Drescher
notes_head: Cofounder of GiveWiki (formerly AI Safety Impact Markets), a crowdsourced charity evaluator and donation recommendation platform for AI safety projects (~220 donors, ~88 projects). Based in Switzerland. Managed regranting of up to $600K to AI safety projects. Also developing an AI-based mental health tool on Gemini. Active in EA community on suffering-focused ethics, s-risks, and AI rights. Advocates for legal personhood and basic rights for AIs rather than coercive control approaches.

name: Diogo de Lucena
notes_head: Chief Scientist at AE Studio, leading brain-computer interface (BCI-OS) development and AI alignment research. Ph.D. in Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering from UC Irvine. Previous work at Harvard on soft robotic systems for stroke rehabilitation. Research focuses on reverse-engineering prosocial mechanisms (empathy, theory of mind) and operationalizing attention schema theory for AI agents. Co-authored 'Towards Safe and Honest AI Agents with Neural Self-Other Overlap' (oral presentation at NeurIPS 2024 Safe Generative AI Workshop).

name: Lauren Mangla
notes_head: Chief Operating Officer of the AI Futures Project, the nonprofit that produced the AI 2027 scenario forecast. Previously Executive Director of the Supervised Program for Alignment Research (SPAR), an AI safety fellowship, and strategic decision-maker at Constellation, an AI safety research center. Also serves as a Manifund regrantor, making funding recommendations for AI safety projects. Focused on AI safety communications, tabletop exercises simulating AGI development, and operations management. Part of the effective altruism / AI safety community.

name: Guillaume Lample
notes_head: Co-founder and Chief Scientist of Mistral AI. Former research scientist at Meta FAIR, where he co-created the LLaMA language model and published influential work on unsupervised machine translation, cross-lingual pretraining, and symbolic mathematics. Studied at École Polytechnique. Co-founded Mistral AI in April 2023 with Arthur Mensch and Timothée Lacroix. His multilingual AI expertise became a key differentiator for Mistral against American-dominated models. One of the first high-profile AI researchers to leave Big Tech for a European startup. Primarily a technical leader; no significant pu

name: Timothée Lacroix
notes_head: Co-founder and CTO of Mistral AI. Former research engineer at Meta FAIR, where he contributed to the LLaMA family of large language models. Studied computer science at École Normale Supérieure, completed a Master's at Paris-Saclay University and PhD at École des Ponts. Co-founded Mistral AI in April 2023 with Arthur Mensch and Guillaume Lample. Focuses on the practical engineering challenges of making AI systems efficient at scale. Author or co-author of 30+ scientific publications with thousands of citations. Named among Top EMEA CTOs 2025. Primarily a technical leader; no significant public

name: Miles Tidmarsh
notes_head: Data scientist and economist focused on AI safety and value alignment. Co-founder and Director of Compassion in Machine Learning (CaML), which develops synthetic pretraining data to steer LLMs toward robustly compassionate values regarding non-human welfare. Co-authored 'Document-tuning for Robust Alignment to Animals' and co-developed the AHB, MORU, and CompassionBench benchmarks. Also co-founded Modeling Cooperation (AI Safety Camp 2019), researching cooperation mechanisms to mitigate risks from competitive AI development races, receiving $300K+ in grants. Co-founded the Centre for AI Respon

name: Jasmine Brazilek
notes_head: Co-founder and technical lead of Compassion in Machine Learning (CaML), which develops synthetic pretraining data to make AI systems more considerate of non-human welfare. Co-authored 'Document-tuning for Robust Alignment to Animals' and co-developed the AHB, MORU, and CompassionBench benchmarks. Background in cybersecurity (6+ years), including designing security systems at Anthropic. Honours degrees in chemistry and microbiology from Monash University. Active in EA/AI safety community, published on EA Forum and LessWrong.

name: Sam Bowman
notes_head: Associate Professor of Data Science and Computer Science at NYU (on long-term leave). Member of technical staff at Anthropic since June 2022, managing AI safety and evaluations research teams. Co-created Stanford Natural Language Inference (SNLI) corpus and the GLUE benchmark — both foundational NLP resources. Founded NYU Alignment Research Group (ARG). Published 'The Checklist: What Succeeding at AI Safety Will Involve' outlining scalable oversight, external safeguards, and monitorability as key priorities. Active on AI Alignment Forum and LessWrong.

name: Scott Aaronson
notes_head: Scott Aaronson is the Schlumberger Centennial Chair of Computer Science at the University of Texas at Austin and director of its Quantum Information Center. A leading quantum computing theorist, he spent 2022-2024 on leave at OpenAI working on AI safety, particularly text watermarking and theoretical foundations of alignment. He now leads an Open Philanthropy-backed AI alignment research group at UT Austin. Aaronson takes AI risk seriously but positions himself as a 'reform' alignment advocate, rejecting both dismissal of AI risk and full doomerism. He has expressed frustration with the collap

name: Rachel Freedman
notes_head: Rachel Freedman is an AI PhD student at UC Berkeley's Center for Human-Compatible AI (CHAI), advised by Stuart Russell. Her research focuses on reinforcement learning, reward modeling, model misspecification, and interpretability, with the goal of ensuring AI is safe and beneficial. Key publications include work on choice set misspecification in reward inference (with Anca Dragan), adapting kidney exchange algorithms to align with human values (AAAI-18 Outstanding Student Paper Honorable Mention, published in AIJ 2020), and active reward learning from multiple teachers (SafeAI at AAAI 2022, wi

name: Sid Black
notes_head: Co-founder of EleutherAI (est. July 2020) alongside Connor Leahy and Leo Gao, where he helped develop landmark open-source LLMs including GPT-J and GPT-NeoX. Co-founded Conjecture in March 2022, an AI alignment research startup based in London focused on the control problem. Active in the AI safety/alignment research community with roots in open-source AI development.

name: Gabriel Alfour
notes_head: Co-founder and CTO of Conjecture (est. March 2022), a London-based AI alignment research startup, alongside Connor Leahy and Sid Black. Previously CEO of Marigold, a firm performing core development on Tezos blockchain infrastructure with 30+ staff. Advisor to ControlAI, an AI policy nonprofit. Background connects the Tezos blockchain ecosystem to AI safety research. Has appeared on AI policy podcasts discussing superintelligence and AI safety.

name: Vivek Chilukuri
notes_head: Senior Fellow and Director of the Technology and National Security Program at CNAS. Research focuses on how AI and emerging technologies reshape national security and great power competition, responsible AI development/deployment, U.S. technology leadership, and the intersection of technology, democracy, and geopolitics. Prior to CNAS, served as senior staff for Senator Michael Bennet (D-CO) in multiple roles: senior policy advisor for technology and democracy, deputy chief of staff, legislative director, and chief speechwriter. Earlier career includes Policy Advisor to the State Department Un

name: Viktoriya Krakovna
notes_head: Research scientist at Google DeepMind focusing on AI alignment — ensuring advanced AI systems act in accordance with human interests. Co-founder of the Future of Life Institute (2014). PhD in statistics and machine learning from Harvard, focused on building interpretable models. Research spans deceptive alignment, dangerous capability evaluations, specification gaming, goal misgeneralization, and avoiding side effects. Active contributor to AI safety discourse on LessWrong and the EA Forum. Has discussed AGI ruin scenarios and the "sharp left turn" alignment challenge. One of the most prominen

name: Daniel Filan
title: Risk Assessor at METR; Host of AXRP - the AI X-risk Research Podcast
notes_head: Researcher at METR focused on loss-of-control risk assessment. Host of AXRP (AI X-risk Research Podcast), interviewing safety researchers since 2020. Previously a senior researcher at MATS. Active contributor to the AI safety research community.

name: Sam McCandlish
title: Co-founder and Chief Scientist, Anthropic
notes_head: Sam McCandlish is a co-founder and Chief Scientist of Anthropic, where he leads scaling and pre-training research. Previously at OpenAI, where he was a lead author on the influential 2020 'Scaling Laws for Neural Language Models' paper (with Jared Kaplan). Left OpenAI with the group that founded Anthropic in 2021.

name: Reva Schwartz
title: Principal Investigator for AI Bias, NIST
primary_org: National Institute of Standards and Technology
notes_head: Reva Schwartz is a Research Scientist at NIST focused on the sociotechnical dimensions of trustworthy AI. Was a principal contributor to the NIST AI Risk Management Framework (AI RMF 1.0, released January 2023) and its Generative AI Profile (2024). Background in cognitive and behavioral science applied to AI systems assessment.

name: Karén Simonyan
title: Chief Scientist, Microsoft AI (former Inflection AI Chief Scientist and co-founder)
notes_head: Karén Simonyan is a co-founder of Inflection AI (2022), where he served as Chief Scientist. Previously a Principal Scientist at DeepMind, where he co-led the Deep Learning Scaling team and contributed to Gemini. Widely known in computer vision for the VGG architecture (Simonyan & Zisserman, 2014). Joined Microsoft in March 2024 alongside Mustafa Suleyman as part of the Inflection-to-Microsoft transition, serving as Chief Scientist of Microsoft AI.

name: Gregory C. Allen
title: Director, Wadhwani AI Center at CSIS
primary_org: Center for Strategic and International Studies (CSIS)
notes_head: Gregory C. Allen is Director of the Wadhwani AI Center at CSIS, where he leads research on AI, national security, and U.S.-China technology competition. A leading public analyst of U.S. chip export controls (especially the October 2022 and October 2023 rules). Previously served as Director of Strategy and Policy at the U.S. Department of Defense's Joint Artificial Intelligence Center (JAIC, the predecessor to the CDAO office). Co-author of the influential 2017 report 'Artificial Intelligence and National Security'. Hosts the biweekly CSIS AI Policy Podcast.

name: Igor Babuschkin
title: Head of Research / Chief Engineer, xAI
notes_head: Igor Babuschkin is a founding member of xAI (2023) and leads the company's largest research team (~29 direct reports per public org-chart data), reporting directly to Elon Musk. Primary non-Musk technical voice on xAI research externally. Formerly a research scientist at Google DeepMind.
